data.name: SystemServiceManager

operations.description: The service manager SHALL start the long-running server in a background goroutine and return immediately without blocking.
operations.name: Start

and: Run callback is invoked in a goroutine
scenarios.given: A Program with a valid Run function
scenarios.name: Normal startup
scenarios.then: The server runs in the background and Start returns without error
scenarios.when: Start is called by the service manager

and: Log fatal message includes service run failed
scenarios.given: A Program with a Run function that returns an error
scenarios.name: Run failure
scenarios.then: The process logs a fatal error and terminates

operations.description: The service manager SHALL gracefully shut down the server by invoking the configured Shutdown callback before process termination.
operations.name: Stop

scenarios.given: A Program with a non-nil Shutdown function
scenarios.name: Graceful shutdown with callback
scenarios.then: The Shutdown callback is invoked and its error returned
scenarios.when: Stop is called by the service manager

scenarios.given: A Program with a nil Shutdown function
scenarios.name: Shutdown without callback
scenarios.then: Stop returns nil without invoking any callback
scenarios.when: Stop is called by the service manager

operations.description: The system SHALL generate a platform-appropriate service configuration resolving the executable path, determining the target user, and setting working directory and environment variables.
operations.name: ServiceConfig

and: Executable path is resolved with symlink evaluation
WorkingDirectory is set to root's home
HOME environment variable is set to root's home
scenarios.given: root is true
scenarios.name: Root/system service configuration
scenarios.then: A Config with root privileges and system paths is returned

and: SUDO_USER environment variable is checked first
DOAS_USER environment variable is checked second
UserName defaults to root if all lookups fail
scenarios.given: root is false and userMode is false
scenarios.name: Non-root service user resolution
scenarios.then: A Config running as the invoking user with their home directory is returned

and: UserService option is set to true
UserName is set to the resolved non-root user
scenarios.given: root is false and userMode is true
scenarios.name: User-mode service configuration
scenarios.then: A user-level service Config is returned

and: UserName is left blank for LocalSystem
scenarios.given: runtime.GOOS is windows and root is true
scenarios.name: Windows system service
scenarios.then: A Windows service Config for LocalSystem is returned

and: UserName is set to resolved user
scenarios.given: runtime.GOOS is windows and root is false
scenarios.name: Windows user service
scenarios.then: A Windows service Config for the specified user is returned

scenarios.given: os.Executable returns an error
scenarios.name: Executable path failure
scenarios.then: An error is returned with nil Config

and: Error message contains 'could not determine service user'
scenarios.given: runtime.GOOS is not windows and svcUser is empty after all resolution attempts
scenarios.name: User determination failure
scenarios.then: An error is returned with nil Config

operations.description: The system SHALL map user-supplied control actions to valid kardianos service actions, normalizing aliases and rejecting unsupported commands.
operations.name: ControlAction

scenarios.given: action is 'install', 'start', 'stop', 'restart', or 'uninstall'
scenarios.name: Valid direct actions
scenarios.then: The action is returned unchanged with no error

scenarios.given: action is 'remove'
scenarios.name: Alias normalization
scenarios.then: The action is mapped to 'uninstall' and returned with no error

scenarios.given: action is not a recognized command
scenarios.name: Unsupported action rejection
scenarios.then: An empty string and error are returned

data.purpose: Wraps the kardianos/service library to provide cross-platform OS service integration for AxonRouter-Go, enabling the binary to run as a managed system service with proper lifecycle handling, privilege configuration, and user context resolution.
sideEffects: Reads os.Executable and resolves symlinks
Reads SUDO_USER, DOAS_USER environment variables
Calls user.Current() and user.Lookup()
Sets HOME environment variable in service config
Spawns background goroutine for server execution
May call log.Fatalf causing process termination

data.name: ProviderSettingsManager

operations.description: Initializes a new Manager that loads existing provider settings from the data directory and sets up the compatibility manager reference.
operations.name: InitializeManager

and: Existing provider setting files loaded into memory
scenarios.given: A valid data directory path
scenarios.name: Successful initialization
scenarios.then: Manager is created with loaded settings and compatibility manager is set

operations.description: Returns the stored ProviderSettings for a given providerID, loading from disk if not cached. Falls back to default settings when no file exists. Uses singleflight to collapse concurrent first-time reads for the same provider into a single disk load.
operations.name: GetProviderSettings

scenarios.given: Provider settings already cached in memory
scenarios.name: Cached settings retrieval
scenarios.then: Returns cached ProviderSettings with nil error
scenarios.when: Get is called for a cached providerID

and: Result cached in memory for subsequent calls
scenarios.given: Provider settings file exists on disk but not in cache
scenarios.name: First-time load from disk
scenarios.then: Returns parsed ProviderSettings with nil error
scenarios.when: Get is called for an uncached providerID

and: Default routing mode set to RoundRobin
No error returned for missing file
scenarios.given: No settings file exists for the provider
scenarios.name: Missing file fallback
scenarios.then: Returns default ProviderSettings with DefaultRoutingMode
scenarios.when: Get is called for a provider with no settings file

scenarios.given: Disk read error occurs (permissions, corruption, etc.)
scenarios.name: Disk read error handling
scenarios.then: Returns default ProviderSettings with error
scenarios.when: Get encounters a non-NotExist read error

scenarios.given: Settings file contains invalid JSON
scenarios.name: Corrupted file handling
scenarios.then: Returns default ProviderSettings with parse error

operations.description: Returns the effective RoutingMode for a provider by delegating to Get. Returns DefaultRoutingMode on any error.
operations.name: GetEffectiveRoutingMode

and: Specific routing mode from stored settings returned
scenarios.given: Provider has stored settings with explicit routing mode
scenarios.name: Explicit routing mode
scenarios.then: Returns the configured RoutingMode

scenarios.given: Provider has no stored settings or error occurs
scenarios.name: Default routing mode fallback
scenarios.then: Returns DefaultRoutingMode

operations.description: Persists ProviderSettings for a provider to a JSON file and updates the in-memory cache. Applies DefaultRoutingMode if empty routing mode provided.
operations.name: SaveProviderSettings

and: JSON file written to provider-settings directory
scenarios.given: Valid providerID and complete settings
scenarios.name: Successful save
scenarios.then: Settings persisted to disk and cached, nil error returned

scenarios.given: Settings with empty RoutingMode
scenarios.name: Default routing mode applied
scenarios.then: Settings saved with DefaultRoutingMode, nil error returned
scenarios.when: Save is called with empty routing mode

scenarios.given: Disk write fails (permissions, disk full, etc.)
scenarios.name: Disk write failure
scenarios.then: Returns write error, in-memory cache remains unchanged

operations.description: Returns the next round-robin index for a provider-model combination, cycling through candidates. Each providerID+modelID pair maintains an independent counter to prevent cross-model interference.
operations.name: GetNextRoundRobinIndex

scenarios.given: No prior round-robin calls for this provider-model pair
scenarios.name: First call initializes counter
scenarios.then: Returns 0 and initializes atomic counter for the key

scenarios.given: Existing counter for provider-model pair
scenarios.name: Sequential rotation
scenarios.then: Returns next index in cyclic sequence

scenarios.given: Multiple provider-model pairs with active counters
scenarios.name: Isolated model counters
scenarios.then: Each pair returns independent sequence unaffected by others
scenarios.when: NextRoundRobinIndex is called for different provider-model pairs

data.purpose: Manages per-provider runtime configuration including routing mode selection and round-robin indexing. Provides file-backed settings storage with in-memory caching and concurrent-safe access to avoid database write contention on hot routing paths.
sideEffects: Reads from provider-settings JSON files on disk
Writes to provider-settings JSON files on disk
Creates provider-settings directory on initialization
Maintains in-memory cache of provider settings
Maintains in-memory atomic counters for round-robin indexing

data.name: KeyManager

operations.description: Initializes the KeyManager and ensures a valid admin API key exists by loading from database or generating a new one.
operations.name: Initialize KeyManager

scenarios.given: No existing admin API key in database settings
scenarios.name: Fresh initialization creates new key
scenarios.then: KeyManager holds a newly generated key with axr_ prefix

scenarios.given: Existing admin API key exists in database settings
scenarios.name: Existing key loaded on initialization
scenarios.then: KeyManager holds the existing key value

operations.description: Returns the current master admin API key from memory without database lookup.
operations.name: Retrieve current key

scenarios.given: KeyManager has been initialized with a valid key
scenarios.name: Read current key from memory
scenarios.then: The current admin API key string is returned

scenarios.given: KeyManager has not been fully initialized
scenarios.name: Read empty key before initialization completes
scenarios.then: Empty string or current in-memory value is returned

operations.description: Generates a new cryptographically secure admin API key, persists it to database settings, and updates the in-memory value.
operations.name: Regenerate master key

and: New key is atomically updated in memory
scenarios.given: KeyManager is initialized with an existing key
scenarios.name: Successful key regeneration
scenarios.then: New key with axr_ prefix is returned and persisted

scenarios.given: Database write fails during regeneration
scenarios.name: Regeneration fails on persistence
scenarios.then: Error is returned and current key is not updated

scenarios.given: Cryptographic random generation fails
scenarios.name: Regeneration fails on key generation
scenarios.then: Error is returned and no database write occurs

data.purpose: Manages the lifecycle of a single master admin API key, including initialization from persistent storage, in-memory retrieval, and secure regeneration with cryptographic randomness.
sideEffects: Reads from database settings table
Writes to database settings table
Cryptographic random generation via crypto/rand

data.name: AuthManager

operations.description: The Manager SHALL create a new instance without persistence when NewManager is called.
operations.name: CreateManagerWithoutPersistence

scenarios.given: A request to initialize the auth subsystem
scenarios.name: Manager creation without persistence
scenarios.then: A Manager is returned with in-memory services map, empty groupTail, empty rotationMap, and nil tokenWriter

operations.description: The Manager SHALL create a new instance with CAS token persistence when NewManagerWithWriter is called.
operations.name: CreateManagerWithWriter

and: TokenWriter is stored under writerMu protection
scenarios.given: A TokenWriter implementation is available
scenarios.name: Manager creation with persistence
scenarios.then: A Manager is returned with the supplied TokenWriter configured for CAS operations

operations.description: The Manager SHALL support dynamic reconfiguration of the TokenWriter at runtime.
operations.name: ConfigureTokenWriter

and: Concurrent readers of tokenWriter must not observe torn writes
scenarios.given: A Manager exists with or without a TokenWriter
scenarios.name: Runtime TokenWriter reconfiguration
scenarios.then: The Manager atomically replaces its TokenWriter with the supplied writer

operations.description: The Manager SHALL register OAuth service implementations per provider type.
operations.name: RegisterOAuthService

and: Services map is protected by mu mutex
scenarios.given: An OAuth service implementation exists for a supported provider
scenarios.name: Service registration
scenarios.then: The service is stored and retrievable by provider type

operations.description: The Manager SHALL retrieve the registered OAuth service for a given provider.
operations.name: GetOAuthService

and: Returns false when provider not found
scenarios.given: Services have been registered for some providers
scenarios.name: Service retrieval
scenarios.then: The correct service and true are returned for registered providers; nil and false for unregistered

operations.description: The Manager SHALL initiate an OAuth authorization flow for a specified provider.
operations.name: StartOAuthFlow

and: Result channel delivers Credentials upon completion
scenarios.given: A valid OAuth service is registered for the provider
scenarios.name: OAuth flow initiation
scenarios.then: An authURL is returned along with a channel that will receive the resulting Credentials

operations.description: The Manager SHALL refresh access tokens using the provider's OAuth service with singleflight deduplication.
operations.name: RefreshAccessToken

and: Uses singleflight.Group to prevent duplicate refresh requests
scenarios.given: Credentials with an expired or expiring access token
scenarios.name: Token refresh
scenarios.then: New Credentials with updated access token are returned

operations.description: The Manager SHALL refresh tokens for a specific connection with stale-caller rotation redirect support.
operations.name: RefreshTokenForConnection

and: Uses CAS pattern via TokenWriter for persistence
Checks rotation cache for recently rotated tokens
scenarios.given: Connection-scoped credentials need refresh
scenarios.name: Connection token refresh with rotation handling
scenarios.then: Fresh credentials are returned, with stale callers redirected via rotation cache

operations.description: The Manager SHALL cache token rotation results to redirect stale callers.
operations.name: RecordTokenRotation

scenarios.given: A successful token refresh produced new refresh token different from old
scenarios.name: Rotation caching
scenarios.then: The old-to-new token mapping is cached with 60-second TTL

operations.description: The Manager SHALL lookup recent token rotations to redirect stale callers.
operations.name: LookupTokenRotation

and: Returns nil if entry expired or not found
Cleans up expired entries on lookup
scenarios.given: A stale refresh token may have been recently rotated
scenarios.name: Rotation lookup
scenarios.then: The current valid credentials are returned if rotation occurred within TTL

data.purpose: Orchestrates OAuth 2.0 token lifecycle across multiple providers, including authorization flow initiation, token refresh with singleflight deduplication, connection-scoped persistence via CAS pattern, and stale-caller rotation redirect through a time-bound cache.
sideEffects: In-memory cache writes (rotationMap with 60s TTL)
Potential TokenWriter.GetRefreshToken database read
Potential TokenWriter.SaveTokens database write
Singleflight coordination for refresh deduplication
Synchronization primitives (mutex, RWMutex) for concurrent access

subSpecs.name: getService

operations.description: The GetService operation SHALL return the registered OAuthService for the specified provider type when a service has been configured.
operations.name: retrieveConfiguredService

and: the returned boolean SHALL be true
scenarios.given: the AuthManager has a registered OAuthService for provider 'GitHub'
scenarios.name: serviceExists
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return the GitHub OAuthService

and: the returned boolean SHALL be false
scenarios.given: the AuthManager has no registered OAuthService for provider 'Slack'
scenarios.name: serviceMissing
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return nil

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves the OAuth service implementation associated with a specific provider type.

subSpecs.name: errorFormatting

operations.description: The error formatting operation SHALL construct formatted error messages with contextual details for operational failures within the token lifecycle.
operations.name: formatErrorMessage

and: the error message SHALL contain the provider identifier
scenarios.given: a token refresh failure for provider 'Google'
scenarios.name: providerContextInError
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL produce an error containing 'Google' and the underlying failure reason
scenarios.when: fmt.Errorf is invoked with format 'provider %s: refresh failed: %w' and arguments 'Google', err

subSpecs.purpose: Constructs descriptive error values with formatted context for failure propagation.

subSpecs.name: lookupRotation

operations.description: The lookupRotation operation SHALL check whether a stale refresh token was recently rotated and return the rotation entry if found within the time-bound cache.
operations.name: checkStaleTokenRotation

and: the rotation entry SHALL contain the new credentials
scenarios.given: a refresh token 'old_token_abc' for provider 'Google' was rotated 30 seconds ago and cached with TTL 5 minutes
scenarios.name: recentRotationFound
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return the non-nil rotationEntry with the new credentials
scenarios.when: lookupRotation is called with provider 'Google' and refreshToken 'old_token_abc'

and: the returned pointer SHALL be nil
scenarios.given: no rotation has been recorded for refresh token 'unknown_token' for provider 'Google'
scenarios.name: rotationNotFound
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return nil
scenarios.when: lookupRotation is called with provider 'Google' and refreshToken 'unknown_token'

and: the returned pointer SHALL be nil
scenarios.given: a refresh token 'expired_token' for provider 'Google' was rotated 10 minutes ago with cache TTL 5 minutes
scenarios.name: rotationExpired
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return nil
scenarios.when: lookupRotation is called with provider 'Google' and refreshToken 'expired_token'

subSpecs.purpose: Checks the rotation cache to determine if a stale refresh token was recently replaced with new credentials.

subSpecs.name: contextDone

operations.description: The context done check operation SHALL determine whether the request context has been canceled or exceeded its deadline.
operations.name: checkContextCancellation

and: the returned channel SHALL be closed
scenarios.given: a context with a 100ms timeout that has elapsed
scenarios.name: timeoutExpired
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return a closed channel signaling cancellation

and: the returned channel SHALL be open and nil receive blocks
scenarios.given: a background context with no deadline or cancellation
scenarios.name: contextActive
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return a non-closed channel

subSpecs.purpose: Monitors request context for cancellation signals to enable cooperative goroutine termination.

subSpecs.name: contextError

operations.description: The context error retrieval operation SHALL return the specific error indicating why a context was canceled or timed out.
operations.name: getContextErrorReason

scenarios.given: a context with a 50ms timeout that has expired
scenarios.name: deadlineExceeded
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return context.DeadlineExceeded
scenarios.when: ctx.Err() is called after the timeout

scenarios.given: a context that was explicitly canceled via cancel function
scenarios.name: explicitlyCanceled
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return context.Canceled

scenarios.given: a context that has not been canceled and has no expired deadline
scenarios.name: noErrorActive
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return nil

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves the specific cancellation reason from an expired or canceled context.

subSpecs.name: sleepDelay

operations.description: The sleep operation SHALL pause the current goroutine execution for the specified duration to implement backoff or synchronization delays.
operations.name: pauseExecution

and: the goroutine SHALL resume with elapsed time >= requested duration
scenarios.given: a backoff strategy requiring 200ms delay between retry attempts
scenarios.name: backoffDelay
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL block the goroutine for at least 200ms before returning

subSpecs.purpose: Introduces controlled delays in goroutine execution for backoff or timing coordination.

subSpecs.name: durationType

operations.description: The duration type operation SHALL represent time intervals in nanoseconds for timeout, TTL, and delay specifications.
operations.name: defineTimeInterval

and: the duration SHALL equal 5 minutes in nanoseconds
scenarios.given: a rotation cache TTL requirement of 5 minutes
scenarios.name: cacheTTL
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL produce a time.Duration of 300000000000 nanoseconds
scenarios.when: time.Duration(300000000000) is used or 5 * time.Minute is evaluated

subSpecs.purpose: Provides type-safe representation of time intervals for configuration and computation.

subSpecs.name: stringFormatting

operations.description: The string formatting operation SHALL construct formatted strings for cache keys, identifiers, and diagnostic messages using specified format verbs.
operations.name: formatCacheKey

scenarios.given: a rotation cache key format of 'rotation:%s:%s'
scenarios.name: rotationCacheKey
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL produce the formatted string 'rotation:github:abc123'

subSpecs.purpose: Composes formatted strings for cache keys and other parameterized identifiers.

subSpecs.name: refreshToken

operations.description: The refresh token operation SHALL exchange a refresh token for new access and refresh credentials through the OAuth provider's token endpoint.
operations.name: exchangeRefreshToken

and: the returned credentials SHALL contain a new access token and refresh token
scenarios.given: a valid refresh token 'valid_refresh' for provider 'Google'
scenarios.name: successfulRefresh
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return new Credentials with updated tokens and expiration
scenarios.when: svc.RefreshToken is called with the valid refresh token

and: the error SHALL indicate token revocation
scenarios.given: a revoked refresh token 'revoked_refresh' for provider 'Google'
scenarios.name: revokedToken
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return an error indicating the refresh token is invalid or revoked
scenarios.when: svc.RefreshToken is called with the revoked token

subSpecs.purpose: Obtains new OAuth credentials by presenting a refresh token to the provider's token endpoint.

subSpecs.name: getPersistedRefreshToken

operations.description: The get persisted refresh token operation SHALL retrieve the current refresh token stored for a connection from persistent storage.
operations.name: readConnectionRefreshToken

and: the returned string SHALL be the stored refresh token
scenarios.given: connection 'conn_123' has stored refresh token 'stored_token_xyz'
scenarios.name: tokenExists
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return 'stored_token_xyz'

and: the error SHALL indicate connection not found
scenarios.given: no connection exists with ID 'conn_missing'
scenarios.name: connectionNotFound
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return an empty string and a not-found error

subSpecs.purpose: Reads the currently persisted refresh token associated with a specific connection identifier.

subSpecs.name: persistTokens

operations.description: The persist tokens operation SHALL atomically store new access and refresh tokens for a connection using compare-and-swap semantics to prevent lost updates.
operations.name: atomicTokenPersistence

and: the stored record SHALL include the provider-specific metadata
scenarios.given: connection 'conn_456' with current token version 'v2', new tokens 'new_access'/'new_refresh', expiration at Unix timestamp 1893456000, and providerSpecific {'scope': 'read_write'}
scenarios.name: successfulCASUpdate
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL persist the tokens and return nil
scenarios.when: writer.SaveTokens is called with matching current version

and: the error SHALL indicate version conflict
scenarios.given: connection 'conn_456' where concurrent modification changed the token version
scenarios.name: casConflict
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return a compare-and-swap conflict error without updating
scenarios.when: writer.SaveTokens is called with stale version information

subSpecs.purpose: Atomically persists token credentials and metadata for a connection with optimistic concurrency control.

subSpecs.name: recordRotation

operations.description: The record rotation operation SHALL cache a token rotation mapping from old refresh token to new credentials with a time-bound TTL for stale caller redirect.
operations.name: cacheTokenRotation

and: the cached entry SHALL be discoverable via lookupRotation for the TTL duration
scenarios.given: provider 'Google', old refresh token 'old_token_789', and new credentials with access token 'new_access_456'
scenarios.name: rotationRecorded
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL store the rotation entry with current timestamp and configured TTL
scenarios.when: recordRotation is called with the old token and new credentials

and: subsequent lookupRotation calls SHALL return the new credentials within TTL
scenarios.given: a recorded rotation with 5-minute TTL
scenarios.name: lookupReturnsNewCredentials
scenarios.then: lookupRotation for the old token SHALL return the rotationEntry containing 'new_access_456'
scenarios.when: lookupRotation is called within 5 minutes of recording

subSpecs.purpose: Records a mapping from superseded refresh token to new credentials in a time-bound cache for stale caller redirection.

subSpecs.name: tokenReaderGetRefreshToken

operations.description: The token reader get refresh token operation SHALL read the current oauth_refresh_token value for a connection from the underlying storage.
operations.name: readCurrentRefreshToken

and: the returned string SHALL be the oauth_refresh_token column value
scenarios.given: database row for connection 'conn_789' has oauth_refresh_token = 'db_token_abc'
scenarios.name: readFromDatabase
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return 'db_token_abc'

and: the error SHALL wrap the database error
scenarios.given: the database connection is unavailable
scenarios.name: databaseUnavailable
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL return an error indicating the query failure

subSpecs.purpose: Reads the current refresh token for a connection from persistent storage.

data.name: ModelCapabilitiesService

operations.description: Loads model capabilities from embedded JSON file into an in-memory map with thread-safe locking.
operations.name: loadCapabilities

and: capsMap is populated with parsed entries
scenarios.given: embeddedCapabilitiesJSON contains valid JSON mapping model IDs to ModelCapabilities
scenarios.name: Valid embedded capabilities JSON
scenarios.then: capsMap is populated with parsed entries

scenarios.given: embeddedCapabilitiesJSON is empty
scenarios.name: Empty embedded capabilities JSON
scenarios.then: capsMap remains empty

scenarios.given: embeddedCapabilitiesJSON contains invalid JSON
scenarios.name: Invalid embedded capabilities JSON
scenarios.then: capsMap remains empty

operations.description: Returns capabilities for a given full model ID with wildcard fallback to progressively shorter suffixes separated by '-'.
operations.name: GetCapabilities

scenarios.given: Model ID exists exactly in capsMap
scenarios.name: Exact model ID match
scenarios.then: Corresponding ModelCapabilities is returned
scenarios.when: GetCapabilities is called with the model ID

and: Suffixes are progressively stripped from the rightmost '-'
scenarios.given: Exact model ID not in capsMap but a shorter suffix variant exists
scenarios.name: Wildcard fallback match
scenarios.then: Capabilities of the matching shorter suffix variant are returned
scenarios.when: GetCapabilities is called with a dated or suffixed model ID

and: All possible suffixes have been attempted
scenarios.given: No exact or wildcard match exists for the model ID
scenarios.name: No match found
scenarios.then: Empty ModelCapabilities is returned
scenarios.when: GetCapabilities is called with an unknown model ID

and: No '-' separator exists in the model ID
scenarios.given: Model ID contains no '-' separator for wildcard fallback
scenarios.name: No wildcard separator
scenarios.then: Empty ModelCapabilities is returned
scenarios.when: GetCapabilities is called with a model ID without dashes that is not in capsMap

data.purpose: Manages model capability metadata by loading embedded JSON configuration and providing thread-safe lookup with wildcard suffix fallback for dated model variants.
sideEffects: Reads embedded capabilities.json file at init time
Maintains in-memory cache with sync.RWMutex

data.name: RestoreService

operations.description: Reads an NDJSON backup stream, validates its header, runs migrations, and restores all rows into the application database with retry logic for lock contention.
operations.name: Restore

scenarios.given: A valid NDJSON backup stream with a recognized header and matching schema version
scenarios.name: Successful full restore
scenarios.then: All rows are inserted into their respective tables and a summary result is returned

scenarios.given: A valid backup stream and a database under light contention
scenarios.name: Retry on transient database errors
scenarios.then: The operation retries with backoff and eventually succeeds
scenarios.when: Restore encounters a retryable error during row insertion

and: The header schema version does not match the application
scenarios.given: A backup stream with an unsupported or mismatched header version
scenarios.name: Reject invalid header version
scenarios.then: Restore returns an error before modifying any data

operations.description: Parses and validates the first line of the backup stream as a JSON header containing metadata such as version and categories.
operations.name: ReadRestoreHeader

and: The line decodes into a valid Header struct
scenarios.given: A backup stream whose first line is a valid JSON header
scenarios.name: Valid header parsed
scenarios.then: The Header struct is returned without error

and: The scanner reaches EOF or produces malformed JSON
scenarios.given: A backup stream with a missing or malformed header line
scenarios.name: Invalid header rejected
scenarios.then: An error is returned describing the header problem

operations.description: Validates that the backup header contains required fields and a supported schema version.
operations.name: ValidateHeader

and: Categories list is non-empty and normalized
scenarios.given: A parsed header with valid version and populated categories
scenarios.name: Valid header accepted
scenarios.then: No error is returned

and: Schema version is missing or unsupported
scenarios.given: A header with an unsupported schema version
scenarios.name: Unsupported version rejected
scenarios.then: An error is returned indicating version mismatch

operations.description: Iterates over the remaining NDJSON lines, decrypts if needed, normalizes row data, and inserts batches into the target database with transaction retry logic.
operations.name: RestoreRows

and: Rows are grouped into batches and inserted via insertRestoreBatch
Transactions are committed after all batches
scenarios.given: A scanner positioned after the header with multiple valid NDJSON row lines
scenarios.name: Restore all rows from backup
scenarios.then: All rows are persisted to their target tables and a summary with counts is returned

scenarios.given: An encrypted backup stream and the correct password
scenarios.name: Restore encrypted backup
scenarios.then: Each line is decrypted before decoding and insertion
scenarios.when: restoreRows is called with a password

and: DELETE statements are executed for tables in deleteOrder before inserts
scenarios.given: A backup header specifying categories whose tables need clearing
scenarios.name: Clear existing data before restore
scenarios.then: Target tables are emptied in dependency-safe order before new rows are inserted

operations.description: Decrypts a single backup line if encryption is enabled, then decodes the JSON into a Row struct and normalizes its data values.
operations.name: DecodeRestoreRow

and: JSON numbers are preserved via UseNumber
Map values are normalized via normalizeRowData
scenarios.given: A plaintext NDJSON line representing a database row
scenarios.name: Decode plaintext row
scenarios.then: A Row with normalized data is returned

scenarios.given: An encrypted NDJSON line and the matching password
scenarios.name: Decode encrypted row
scenarios.then: The line is decrypted and then decoded into a normalized Row

operations.description: Normalizes JSON-decoded values to ensure consistent types for database insertion, including handling json.Number and nested maps.
operations.name: NormalizeRowData

and: json.Number values are preserved as strings
scenarios.given: A row map containing mixed JSON types including numbers and nested objects
scenarios.name: Normalize heterogeneous row data
scenarios.then: All values are converted to insertion-safe Go types

operations.description: Returns the ordered list of categories for deletion, ensuring dependent tables are cleared before their dependencies.
operations.name: DeleteOrder

and: The order respects foreign key constraints
scenarios.given: A list of categories from the backup header
scenarios.name: Dependency-safe deletion order
scenarios.then: Categories are returned in an order that prevents constraint violations during DELETE

operations.description: Constructs and executes a single INSERT statement for a batch of rows into the specified table within a transaction.
operations.name: InsertRestoreBatch

and: Column names are quoted via quoteIdentifier
Placeholders are generated for all values
scenarios.given: A transaction and a batch of rows with uniform columns for a single table
scenarios.name: Insert batch of rows
scenarios.then: All rows are inserted in one statement and the transaction remains open

and: Row values are flattened in column-sorted order
scenarios.given: Rows with map[string]any data where keys may vary
scenarios.name: Handle varying column sets
scenarios.then: The INSERT uses the union of all columns with NULLs for missing values

operations.description: Begins a database transaction for restore operations, retrying on transient lock/busy errors.
operations.name: BeginRestoreTx

scenarios.given: A database under light contention
scenarios.name: Begin transaction with retry
scenarios.then: A valid transaction is returned after at most a few retries

operations.description: Executes an operation with exponential backoff retry when the database returns busy, locked, or timeout errors.
operations.name: ExecWithRetry

scenarios.given: An operation that fails with SQLITE_BUSY on first attempt
scenarios.name: Retry succeeds on transient error
scenarios.then: The operation is retried and succeeds on a subsequent attempt

and: The context is cancelled or the error is not retryable
scenarios.given: An operation that fails with a permanent error
scenarios.name: Fail on non-retryable error
scenarios.then: The error is returned immediately without retry

operations.description: Determines whether a database error indicates a transient condition suitable for retry.
operations.name: IsRetryableDBError

and: Error message contains 'busy', 'locked', 'timeout', or 'database is locked'
scenarios.given: A database error with message 'database is locked'
scenarios.name: Detect retryable error
scenarios.then: true is returned

and: Error message indicates a permanent failure like constraint violation
scenarios.given: A database error with message 'UNIQUE constraint failed'
scenarios.name: Detect non-retryable error
scenarios.then: false is returned

data.purpose: Restores application database state from an NDJSON backup stream, supporting encryption, schema validation, migration, batched insertion, and resilient retry logic for database contention.
sideEffects: Reads from io.Reader (backup stream)
Writes to sql.DB (application database)
Executes database migrations via appdb.RunMigrations
Decrypts backup lines when password is provided

subSpecs.name: errorCreation

operations.description: SHALL create a new error value with a static text message.
operations.name: createStaticError

and: the returned error's Error() method returns the exact input message
scenarios.given: a caller provides a non-empty error message string
scenarios.name: staticErrorCreation
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a non-nil error value

subSpecs.purpose: Creates sentinel or simple error values used for control flow and error identification throughout the restore pipeline.

subSpecs.name: scannerCreation

operations.description: SHALL instantiate a buffered scanner that reads from the provided io.Reader and splits on lines.
operations.name: createLineScanner

and: the scanner's Split method defaults to ScanLines
scenarios.given: a valid io.Reader containing NDJSON data
scenarios.name: scannerInstantiation
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a non-nil *bufio.Scanner positioned at the start of the stream

subSpecs.purpose: Constructs a line-oriented scanner to iterate over NDJSON backup records from an input stream.

subSpecs.name: bufferConfiguration

operations.description: SHALL set the initial buffer size and maximum token size for the scanner.
operations.name: configureBufferLimits

and: the scanner uses the provided buffer if non-nil
subsequent Scan calls respect the configured maximum token size
scenarios.given: a scanner and buffer parameters where max exceeds the default 64K
scenarios.name: largeBufferConfiguration
scenarios.then: the scanner SHALL be configured to handle tokens up to max bytes without returning ErrTooLong
scenarios.when: Buffer is invoked with valid parameters

subSpecs.purpose: Expands scanner capacity to accommodate large NDJSON lines that may exceed default buffer limits.

subSpecs.name: headerParsing

operations.description: SHALL read and parse the first non-empty line from the scanner as a RestoreHeader.
operations.name: parseHeaderLine

and: the scanner advances past the header line
scenarios.given: a scanner positioned at the start of a valid NDJSON backup with a header object as first line
scenarios.name: validHeaderExtraction
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a populated Header with version and categories fields

and: the error message SHALL indicate the header is missing or malformed
scenarios.given: a scanner where the first line is empty or not valid JSON
scenarios.name: invalidHeaderHandling
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a non-nil error

operations.description: SHALL validate that the scanner has not encountered an error before attempting to read.
operations.name: checkScannerHealth

and: the error is propagated without modification
scenarios.given: a scanner with a pre-existing error from prior operations
scenarios.name: scannerErrorPropagation
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a zero Header and the scanner's error

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts and deserializes the metadata header from the NDJSON backup stream to guide subsequent restore operations.

subSpecs.name: headerValidation

operations.description: SHALL verify that the header contains a supported backup version and non-empty categories.
operations.name: validateHeaderContents

scenarios.given: a Header with Version="1.0" and Categories containing "users","orders"
scenarios.name: validHeaderAcceptance
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return nil error

and: the error SHALL identify the unsupported version
scenarios.given: a Header with Version="0.5"
scenarios.name: unsupportedVersionRejection
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a non-nil error

and: the error SHALL indicate categories are required
scenarios.given: a Header with empty Categories slice
scenarios.name: emptyCategoriesRejection
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a non-nil error

subSpecs.purpose: Ensures backup headers declare compatible schema versions and specify target tables for restoration.

subSpecs.name: schemaMigration

operations.description: SHALL execute pending database migrations to bring schema to the version required by the backup header.
operations.name: runSchemaMigrations

and: migration state is tracked in the schema_migrations table
scenarios.given: a database at schema version 2 and backup requiring version 5
scenarios.name: forwardMigration
scenarios.then: the function SHALL apply migrations 3, 4, and 5 and return nil error

and: the error SHALL indicate which migration failed
scenarios.given: a database where migration 4 is corrupted or conflicts
scenarios.name: failedMigration
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a non-nil error and leave the database at the last successful version

subSpecs.purpose: Aligns the target database schema with the version expected by the backup before data insertion begins.

subSpecs.name: errorFormatting

operations.description: SHALL construct formatted error values with contextual messages using format verbs.
operations.name: formatErrorMessage

and: the error message contains the formatted values
scenarios.given: a format string "table %s: %w" with args "users", err
scenarios.name: wrappedErrorFormatting
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return an error with message containing "table users: " and the wrapped error

subSpecs.purpose: Produces descriptive, potentially wrapped errors that preserve causal chains for debugging restore failures.

subSpecs.name: rowRestoration

operations.description: SHALL iterate through all remaining scanner lines, decode each row, and insert into the appropriate table in category-dependent order.
operations.name: restoreAllRows

and: RestoreResult contains counts of successful inserts and failed rows
scenarios.given: a valid scanner with 1000 NDJSON rows across categories ["users","orders"]
scenarios.name: fullRestoreSuccess
scenarios.then: the function SHALL insert all rows and return RestoreResult{Processed: 1000, Failed: 0}
scenarios.when: restoreRows is called with correct password

and: failed rows are logged but do not abort the restore
scenarios.given: a scanner where 5 rows have malformed JSON
scenarios.name: partialFailureHandling
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return RestoreResult with Failed: 5 and continue processing remaining rows

operations.description: SHALL decrypt rows when the backup header indicates encryption and a password is provided.
operations.name: decryptEncryptedRows

scenarios.given: a header with Encrypted=true and a scanner with AES-GCM encrypted rows
scenarios.name: decryptionWithPassword
scenarios.then: the function SHALL decrypt each row before insertion and return successful result
scenarios.when: restoreRows is called with matching password

and: the error SHALL indicate password is required for encrypted backup
scenarios.given: a header with Encrypted=true and empty password string
scenarios.name: missingPasswordRejection
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a non-nil error before processing rows

operations.description: SHALL delete existing table data in dependency order before inserting restored rows.
operations.name: clearExistingData

and: deletion order respects foreign key dependencies via deleteOrder
scenarios.given: categories ["orders","users"] where orders has FK to users
scenarios.name: dependencyAwareDeletion
scenarios.then: the function SHALL delete from "orders" before "users" to avoid constraint violations

subSpecs.purpose: Coordinates the core restore workflow: scanning, decryption, transaction management, ordered deletion, batched insertion, and result aggregation.

subSpecs.name: retryableExecution

operations.description: SHALL execute the provided operation, retrying with exponential backoff when isRetryableDBError returns true.
operations.name: executeWithRetry

and: context cancellation is checked between retries
scenarios.given: an operation that fails twice with SQLITE_BUSY then succeeds
scenarios.name: successfulRetry
scenarios.then: the function SHALL call op three times total and return nil error

and: the final error is returned with retry count context
scenarios.given: an operation that always returns SQLITE_LOCKED
scenarios.name: exhaustedRetries
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a non-nil error after max retries

operations.description: SHALL immediately return when the context is cancelled without invoking the operation.
operations.name: respectContextCancellation

and: the error wraps context.Canceled or context.DeadlineExceeded
scenarios.given: a context that has been cancelled before execWithRetry is called
scenarios.name: cancelledContext
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return ctx.Err() without calling op

subSpecs.purpose: Provides resilient execution against transient database contention by retrying operations with backoff.

subSpecs.name: directExecution

operations.description: SHALL execute a SQL statement directly on the database without transaction guarantees.
operations.name: executeDirectSQL

and: result contains rows affected and last insert ID
scenarios.given: a valid INSERT statement and context
scenarios.name: directInsertExecution
scenarios.then: the function SHALL execute the statement and return non-nil sql.Result

subSpecs.purpose: Executes non-transactional SQL statements for operations that do not require atomic batch guarantees.

subSpecs.name: backgroundContext

operations.description: SHALL return a non-nil, empty Context with no deadline, no values, and no cancellation.
operations.name: createBackgroundContext

and: the context is never cancelled automatically
scenarios.given: any call to context.Background
scenarios.name: backgroundContextCreation
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a Context whose Done() channel is nil

subSpecs.purpose: Provides a root context for operations that do not inherit from a request-scoped context.

subSpecs.name: transactionInitiation

operations.description: SHALL begin a database transaction with retry logic for lock contention.
operations.name: beginResilientTransaction

and: the transaction uses serializable isolation if configured
scenarios.given: a database under light read load
scenarios.name: successfulTransactionBegin
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a valid *sql.Tx after at most one retry

and: BeginTx errors are classified by isRetryableDBError
scenarios.given: a database experiencing heavy contention with SQLITE_BUSY
scenarios.name: retriedTransactionBegin
scenarios.then: the function SHALL retry via execWithRetry and eventually return *sql.Tx

subSpecs.purpose: Starts a restore-scoped database transaction that tolerates transient lock conflicts.

subSpecs.name: transactionRollback

operations.description: SHALL discard all changes made within the transaction and release locks.
operations.name: rollbackTransaction

and: subsequent operations on the transaction return errors
scenarios.given: a transaction with uncommitted INSERT statements
scenarios.name: successfulRollback
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return nil and the database state SHALL be unchanged

scenarios.given: a transaction that has already been committed
scenarios.name: doubleRollback
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return sql.ErrTxDone

subSpecs.purpose: Ensures atomic cleanup of failed restore batches by aborting the active transaction.

subSpecs.name: deletionOrdering

operations.description: SHALL return category names sorted in dependency order for safe deletion (children before parents).
operations.name: computeDeletionOrder

and: the order reverses insertion order to respect foreign keys
scenarios.given: categories ["users","orders","order_items"] with FK chain users<-orders<-order_items
scenarios.name: dependencyBasedOrdering
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return ["order_items","orders","users"]

scenarios.given: categories ["Users","users","ORDERS"]
scenarios.name: normalizedOrdering
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return lowercase deduplicated categories in delete order

subSpecs.purpose: Determines the safe sequence for truncating tables to avoid foreign key constraint violations during restore.

subSpecs.name: transactionalExecution

operations.description: SHALL execute a SQL statement within the transaction boundary.
operations.name: executeTransactionalSQL

and: changes are visible only within the transaction until commit
scenarios.given: a valid DELETE statement within an active transaction
scenarios.name: transactionalDelete
scenarios.then: the function SHALL execute the statement and return sql.Result

and: the error does not auto-rollback; caller must handle
scenarios.given: an invalid SQL syntax
scenarios.name: transactionalError
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a non-nil error and the transaction remains active

subSpecs.purpose: Executes SQL statements atomically within the restore transaction for batch consistency.

subSpecs.name: identifierQuoting

operations.description: SHALL escape SQL identifiers to prevent injection and handle special characters.
operations.name: quoteSQLIdentifier

and: double quotes are doubled per SQL standard
scenarios.given: identifier "user"table"
scenarios.name: quoteEscaping
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return """user""table"""

and: the result is wrapped in double quotes
scenarios.given: identifier "orders"
scenarios.name: simpleQuoting
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return ""orders""

subSpecs.purpose: Sanitizes table and column names for safe inclusion in dynamic SQL statements.

subSpecs.name: lengthMeasurement

operations.description: SHALL return the number of elements in a collection or length of a string.
inputs: v array | slice | map | string | channel
operations.name: measureLength

and: the result is non-negative and fits in int
scenarios.given: a slice with 3 elements
scenarios.name: sliceLength
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return 3

subSpecs.purpose: Provides cardinality information for buffer sizing, batch capacity checks, and loop bounds.

subSpecs.name: batchInsertion

operations.description: SHALL construct and execute a multi-row INSERT statement for the given table and rows.
operations.name: insertRowBatch

and: column names are derived from Row keys and sorted for determinism
scenarios.given: table "users" with 50 rows having keys ["id","name","email"]
scenarios.name: successfulBatchInsert
scenarios.then: the function SHALL execute one INSERT with 50 value tuples and return nil error

and: the statement uses quoteIdentifier for table and column names
scenarios.given: table name "user-data" with rows
scenarios.name: quotedIdentifierUsage
scenarios.then: the function SHALL generate INSERT INTO ""user-data"" ... with properly quoted identifier

operations.description: SHALL return error when the batch is empty without executing SQL.
operations.name: rejectEmptyBatch

and: no SQL is executed on the transaction
scenarios.given: an empty rows slice
scenarios.name: emptyBatchHandling
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a non-nil error indicating empty batch

subSpecs.purpose: Efficiently persists batches of restored rows via parameterized multi-value INSERT statements.

subSpecs.name: lineScanning

operations.description: SHALL advance the scanner to the next token and return true if a token was found.
operations.name: advanceToNextToken

and: scanner.Text() returns the token bytes as string
scenarios.given: a scanner with remaining lines in the input
scenarios.name: successfulScan
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return true and position after the next line terminator

and: Scan returns false when input is exhausted
scenarios.given: a scanner at end of input
scenarios.name: endOfInput
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return false

subSpecs.purpose: Iterates through the NDJSON stream line by line for incremental processing.

subSpecs.name: whitespaceTrimming

operations.description: SHALL remove leading and trailing Unicode whitespace from a string.
operations.name: trimWhitespace

scenarios.given: a string "  hello\tworld\n  "
scenarios.name: trimWhitespace
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return "hello\tworld"

subSpecs.purpose: Normalizes input strings by removing extraneous surrounding whitespace before parsing.

data.name: ConnectionStateStore

operations.description: Retrieves the connection state for a given connection ID.
operations.name: Get

scenarios.given: A connection state exists for connID
scenarios.name: Existing connection
scenarios.then: The existing ConnectionState is returned

scenarios.given: No connection state exists for connID
scenarios.name: Missing connection
scenarios.then: nil is returned

operations.description: Retrieves existing connection state or atomically creates a new one with StatusUnknown.
operations.name: GetOrCreate

scenarios.given: A connection state exists for connID
scenarios.name: Existing connection
scenarios.then: The existing ConnectionState is returned

scenarios.given: No connection state exists for connID
scenarios.name: New connection
scenarios.then: A new ConnectionState is created and returned

operations.description: Finds a connection state by provider prefix using linear scan.
operations.name: GetByPrefix

scenarios.given: Multiple connections exist with matching prefix
scenarios.name: Multiple matches
scenarios.then: The first matching ConnectionState is returned

scenarios.given: No connection has matching prefix
scenarios.name: No match
scenarios.then: nil is returned

operations.description: Creates or updates a connection state entry unconditionally.
operations.name: Set

scenarios.given: A connection state exists for connID
scenarios.name: Update existing
scenarios.then: The connection state is replaced with provided state
scenarios.when: Set is called with connID and new state

scenarios.given: No connection state exists for connID
scenarios.name: Create new
scenarios.then: A new connection state entry is created
scenarios.when: Set is called with connID and state

operations.description: Removes a connection state from the store.
operations.name: Delete

scenarios.given: A connection state exists for connID
scenarios.name: Existing connection
scenarios.then: The connection state is removed

scenarios.given: No connection state exists for connID
scenarios.name: Missing connection
scenarios.then: No action is taken

operations.description: Records a successful request, promoting connection to Ready status if not already.
operations.name: RecordSuccess

scenarios.given: Connection status is already StatusReady
scenarios.name: Already ready fast path
scenarios.then: Status remains Ready and no write lock is taken

scenarios.given: Connection status is not StatusReady
scenarios.name: Promote to ready
scenarios.then: Connection status is set to StatusReady with empty message

operations.description: Records a failed request with error detection, applying appropriate cooldown or status change.
operations.name: RecordFailure

scenarios.given: ErrorDetection has Scope 'model' and ModelID is present
scenarios.name: Model-level cooldown
scenarios.then: Model-specific cooldown is applied via SetModelCooldown

scenarios.given: ErrorDetection has Scope 'model' but ModelID is empty and Category is ErrorRateLimit
scenarios.name: Model scope without model ID
scenarios.then: Connection-level cooldown is applied

scenarios.given: ErrorDetection has Scope 'connection', Category ErrorQuota, and CooldownUntil is set
scenarios.name: Quota exhaustion
scenarios.then: Quota cooldown is applied via SetQuotaCooldown

scenarios.given: ErrorDetection has Scope 'connection' and CooldownUntil is set
scenarios.name: Generic cooldown
scenarios.then: Cooldown is applied via SetCooldown

scenarios.given: ErrorDetection Category is ErrorRateLimit without CooldownUntil
scenarios.name: Rate limit default
scenarios.then: Cooldown is set to current time plus 60 seconds

scenarios.given: ErrorDetection has Scope 'connection' with no cooldown
scenarios.name: Status change
scenarios.then: Connection status and message are set from ErrorDetection

operations.description: Updates connection status with optional reason for disabled status.
operations.name: UpdateStatus

scenarios.given: Status is StatusDisabled and reason is provided
scenarios.name: Disabled with reason
scenarios.then: Status is set to Disabled with reason preserved

scenarios.given: Status is not StatusDisabled or no reason provided
scenarios.name: Status update without reason
scenarios.then: Status is updated with empty reason

operations.description: Sets a cooldown timer for a connection.
operations.name: UpdateCooldown

and: Connection is created if not exists
scenarios.given: Connection may or may not exist
scenarios.name: Set cooldown
scenarios.then: Connection cooldown is set to specified time

operations.description: Iterates over all connection states with callback function.
operations.name: Range

scenarios.given: Multiple connections exist in store
scenarios.name: Iterate all
scenarios.then: Callback is invoked for each connection; iteration stops if callback returns false

operations.description: Iterates over all connection states by connection ID.
operations.name: RangeByConnID

scenarios.given: Multiple connections exist in store
scenarios.name: Iterate by connID
scenarios.then: Callback is invoked for each connection with connID and state

operations.description: Returns immutable snapshots of all connection states.
operations.name: Snapshot

scenarios.given: Multiple connections exist in store
scenarios.name: Capture snapshot
scenarios.then: Slice of ConnectionStateSnapshot containing all states is returned

scenarios.given: Store is empty
scenarios.name: Empty store
scenarios.then: Empty slice is returned

operations.description: Counts connections with StatusReady status.
operations.name: HealthyCount

scenarios.given: Store contains mix of ready and non-ready connections
scenarios.name: Mixed health
scenarios.then: Count of StatusReady connections is returned

scenarios.given: No connections have StatusReady
scenarios.name: No healthy
scenarios.then: Zero is returned

operations.description: Classifies provider unavailability by analyzing failure modes across all provider connections.
operations.name: ClassifyProviderUnavailable

scenarios.given: All connections for provider have quota-related statuses
scenarios.name: Uniform quota failure
scenarios.then: ErrorQuota is returned

scenarios.given: All connections for provider are disabled with auth reasons
scenarios.name: Uniform auth failure
scenarios.then: ErrorAuth is returned

scenarios.given: All connections for provider are disabled with balance_empty reason
scenarios.name: Uniform balance failure
scenarios.then: ErrorBalanceEmpty is returned

scenarios.given: Provider has mixed failure modes or no connections
scenarios.name: Mixed or unknown
scenarios.then: ErrorUnknown is returned

operations.description: Creates or updates connection state from database seed data with atomic initialization.
operations.name: SeedConnection

scenarios.given: Connection does not exist in store
scenarios.name: Seed new connection
scenarios.then: New ConnectionState is created with mapped status and stored atomically

and: Update under lock to prevent partial reads
scenarios.given: Connection already exists in store
scenarios.name: Update existing seed
scenarios.then: Existing ConnectionState is updated with new prefix, priority, and status under mutex lock

operations.description: Returns all connection states as a slice.
operations.name: All

and: Returns direct pointers to internal state
scenarios.given: Multiple connections exist in store
scenarios.name: Get all connections
scenarios.then: Slice containing all ConnectionState pointers is returned

operations.description: Collects all active connection IDs into a map.
operations.name: RangeActive

scenarios.given: Multiple connections exist in store
scenarios.name: Collect active
scenarios.then: Map with all connection IDs as keys and true as values is returned

data.purpose: Manages in-memory connection states with high-concurrency access using sync.Map, providing CRUD operations, health tracking, failure recording with cooldown management, and provider-level failure classification.
sideEffects: In-memory state mutations via sync.Map
Lock acquisition on ConnectionState during updates

data.name: StreamOptionsService

operations.description: SHALL inject stream_options.include_usage=true into the request body when streaming is enabled, the provider format is OpenAI, and the request is an eligible chat completion path.
operations.name: sanitizeStreamOptions

scenarios.given: A streaming chat completion request for an OpenAI provider
scenarios.name: Inject usage for OpenAI streaming chat
scenarios.then: stream_options.include_usage is set to true in the request body
scenarios.when: sanitizeStreamOptions is called with stream=true, providerFmt=FormatOpenAI, and eligible chat path

scenarios.given: A streaming unified text request for an OpenAI provider
scenarios.name: Inject usage for OpenAI unified text
scenarios.then: stream_options.include_usage is set to true in the request body
scenarios.when: sanitizeStreamOptions is called with stream=true, providerFmt=FormatOpenAI, and path=/v1/unified with mode=text

scenarios.given: A streaming Claude messages request translated to chat completions for an OpenAI provider
scenarios.name: Inject usage for Claude-to-OpenAI translation
scenarios.then: stream_options.include_usage is set to true in the request body
scenarios.when: sanitizeStreamOptions is called with stream=true, providerFmt=FormatOpenAI, path=/v1/messages, and clientFmt=FormatClaude

scenarios.given: A non-streaming request with stream_options present in body
scenarios.name: Strip usage for non-streaming request
scenarios.then: stream_options object is removed from the request body

scenarios.given: A streaming request for a non-OpenAI provider with stream_options present in body
scenarios.name: Strip usage for non-OpenAI provider
scenarios.then: stream_options object is removed from the request body
scenarios.when: sanitizeStreamOptions is called with providerFmt != FormatOpenAI

scenarios.given: A streaming request to /v1/messages from a non-Claude client with stream_options present in body
scenarios.name: Strip usage for non-Claude messages path
scenarios.then: stream_options object is removed from the request body
scenarios.when: sanitizeStreamOptions is called with path=/v1/messages and clientFmt != FormatClaude

and: path is not a chat completion endpoint
scenarios.given: A streaming request to a non-chat endpoint with stream_options present in body
scenarios.name: Strip usage for non-chat path
scenarios.then: stream_options object is removed from the request body
scenarios.when: sanitizeStreamOptions is called with an ineligible path

and: stream_options does not exist in body
scenarios.given: A request where stream_options is not present and conditions for injection are not met
scenarios.name: No modification when stream_options absent
scenarios.then: The original body is returned unchanged

and: sjson.SetBytes or sjson.DeleteBytes encounters an error
scenarios.given: A request where JSON manipulation fails
scenarios.name: Graceful fallback on JSON error
scenarios.then: The original body is returned unchanged

data.purpose: Manages stream_options manipulation in request bodies to enable or disable token usage reporting in streaming responses based on provider compatibility, client format, and endpoint eligibility.
sideEffects: Modifies request body byte array via sjson.SetBytes
Modifies request body byte array via sjson.DeleteBytes

data.name: RequestOptimizationService

operations.description: SHALL apply compression to request body when compression is enabled and the request does not contain prompt-cache markers. SHALL be fail-open, returning the original body on error or when cache control markers are present. SHALL record compression metrics asynchronously without blocking the request.
operations.name: CompressRequestBody

and: Request body does not contain cache control markers
scenarios.given: A request body and enabled compression strategy
scenarios.name: Compression applied successfully
scenarios.then: Compressed body is returned and metrics are recorded asynchronously

scenarios.given: A request body and disabled compression strategy
scenarios.name: Compression disabled
scenarios.then: Original body is returned unchanged

and: Request body contains cache control markers
scenarios.given: A request body with prompt-cache markers
scenarios.name: Cache control markers present
scenarios.then: Original body is returned unchanged

and: Request body does not contain cache control markers
scenarios.given: A request body where compression fails
scenarios.name: Compression error fail-open
scenarios.then: Original body is returned unchanged

operations.description: SHALL persist aggregated compression statistics for the active compression mode. SHALL be best-effort with failures logged but never blocking. SHALL upsert metrics into compression_metrics table via write queue when available, falling back to direct database write.
operations.name: RecordCompressionMetrics

and: Compression mode is not empty and not Off
scenarios.given: Valid compression statistics with active mode
scenarios.name: Metrics recorded via queue
scenarios.then: Upsert operation is enqueued to writeQueue

and: Compression mode is not empty and not Off
scenarios.given: Valid compression statistics without write queue
scenarios.name: Metrics recorded directly
scenarios.then: Upsert operation is executed directly on database

and: Compression mode is Off or empty
scenarios.given: Compression statistics with disabled mode
scenarios.name: Disabled mode skipped
scenarios.then: No metrics are recorded

and: Both original and compressed tokens are zero
scenarios.given: Compression statistics with zero tokens
scenarios.name: Zero tokens skipped
scenarios.then: No metrics are recorded

operations.description: SHALL compute a cache key for exact-match non-streaming requests without tools or cache_control markers. SHALL return empty string when request should not be cached.
operations.name: ExactCacheKey

and: Request does not contain cache control markers
scenarios.given: A cacheable request body with model
scenarios.name: Cache key computed
scenarios.then: Non-empty cache key is returned

scenarios.given: A streaming request
scenarios.name: Streaming request not cached
scenarios.then: Empty string is returned

scenarios.given: A request without exact cache configured
scenarios.name: No cache configured
scenarios.then: Empty string is returned

scenarios.given: A request with tool definitions
scenarios.name: Tools present not cached
scenarios.then: Empty string is returned

scenarios.given: A request with cache control markers
scenarios.name: Cache control markers present
scenarios.then: Empty string is returned

operations.description: SHALL write a cached response to the client with appropriate headers. SHALL account for request tokens against the API key budget before returning. SHALL return true indicating cache hit was served.
operations.name: ServeCacheHit

and: API key ID is present in context
scenarios.given: A valid cache entry and request context
scenarios.name: Cache hit served
scenarios.then: Response is written with HIT header, content type set, status code written, body written, and API key usage incremented

operations.description: SHALL persist a successful non-streaming response to exact cache when a valid cache key was computed. SHALL silently skip when cache key is empty or exact cache is not configured.
operations.name: StoreExactCache

scenarios.given: A successful response with valid cache key
scenarios.name: Response cached
scenarios.then: Cache entry is stored with body, status code, and JSON content type

scenarios.given: A response with empty cache key
scenarios.name: Empty key skipped
scenarios.then: No cache storage occurs

scenarios.given: A response without exact cache configured
scenarios.name: No cache configured
scenarios.then: No cache storage occurs

operations.description: SHALL write a JSON response to the client with appropriate content type header. SHALL mark response as cache miss via X-Cache-Status header.
operations.name: WriteJSONResponse

scenarios.given: A response to be written as JSON
scenarios.name: JSON response written
scenarios.then: Response is written with Content-Type application/json, X-Cache-Status MISS, specified status code, and body

data.purpose: Optimizes API requests through compression and exact-match caching. Manages request body compression with fail-open semantics, compression metrics aggregation, cache key computation for cacheable requests, cache hit serving with usage accounting, response caching, and standardized JSON response writing with cache status headers.
sideEffects: Writes to compression_metrics table via SQL upsert
Enqueues database operations to writeQueue
Writes to exact cache store
Writes HTTP response headers and body to gin.Context
Increments API key usage via usage service

data.name: StreamTokenExtractor

operations.description: SHALL extract token counts from a non-streaming response body supporting OpenAI-format, native Claude-format, Gemini usageMetadata, and OpenAI Responses API usage shapes.
operations.name: ExtractTokensFromBody

scenarios.given: A non-streaming response body in OpenAI format with usage.prompt_tokens and usage.completion_tokens
scenarios.name: OpenAI format with prompt and completion tokens
scenarios.then: Returns StreamTokenCounts with extracted values

scenarios.given: A non-streaming response body in OpenAI format with usage.input_tokens and usage.output_tokens
scenarios.name: OpenAI format with input and output tokens
scenarios.then: Returns StreamTokenCounts with extracted values

scenarios.given: A non-streaming response body with prompt_tokens_details containing cached_tokens and cache_creation_tokens
scenarios.name: OpenAI format with cache details
scenarios.then: Returns StreamTokenCounts with cache information

scenarios.given: A non-streaming response body with completion_tokens_details containing reasoning_tokens
scenarios.name: OpenAI format with reasoning tokens
scenarios.then: Returns StreamTokenCounts with reasoning information

and: InputTokens is sum of input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, and cache_read_input_tokens
scenarios.given: A non-streaming response body in native Claude format with usage.input_tokens, usage.output_tokens, usage.cache_creation_input_tokens, and usage.cache_read_input_tokens
scenarios.name: Native Claude format
scenarios.then: Returns StreamTokenCounts with aggregated input and cache information

scenarios.given: A non-streaming response body in Gemini format with usageMetadata containing promptTokenCount and candidatesTokenCount
scenarios.name: Gemini non-streaming format
scenarios.then: Returns StreamTokenCounts with extracted metadata

scenarios.given: A non-streaming response body in OpenAI Responses API format with response.usage.input_tokens and response.usage.output_tokens
scenarios.name: OpenAI Responses API format
scenarios.then: Returns StreamTokenCounts with extracted values

and: All fields remain at zero values
scenarios.given: A response body that does not match any known format or has no valid usage data
scenarios.name: Unrecognized or empty format
scenarios.then: Returns StreamTokenCounts with zero values

scenarios.given: A response body that could potentially match multiple formats but has zero or invalid values in earlier formats
scenarios.name: Format precedence with fallback
scenarios.then: Returns StreamTokenCounts from the first format with positive input tokens

operations.description: SHALL extract token counts from a Server-Sent Events (SSE) chunk supporting streaming formats including Codex and OpenAI Responses API response.completed/done shapes.
operations.name: ExtractTokensFromSSEChunk

scenarios.given: An SSE chunk containing valid token usage data in a supported streaming format
scenarios.name: Valid SSE chunk with token data
scenarios.then: Returns StreamTokenCounts with extracted values and true

scenarios.given: An SSE chunk that does not contain token usage data or is in an unsupported format
scenarios.name: Invalid or non-token SSE chunk
scenarios.then: Returns empty StreamTokenCounts and false

operations.description: SHALL extract cost in USD ticks from an SSE chunk using top-level usage.cost_in_usd_ticks field used by Grok CLI.
operations.name: ExtractCostInUsdTicksFromSSEChunk

scenarios.given: An SSE chunk containing usage.cost_in_usd_ticks field
scenarios.name: Valid SSE chunk with Grok CLI cost
scenarios.then: Returns cost value divided by grokCLICostUsdTicksDivisor and true

scenarios.given: An SSE chunk without usage.cost_in_usd_ticks field
scenarios.name: SSE chunk without Grok CLI cost
scenarios.then: Returns zero and false

operations.description: SHALL merge source token counts into destination, where incoming non-zero values overwrite target values, matching OmniRoute's mergeUsage pattern.
operations.name: MergeTokenCounts

and: Other dst fields remain unchanged if src fields are zero
scenarios.given: Destination StreamTokenCounts with existing values and source with non-zero InputTokens
scenarios.name: Merge non-zero input tokens
scenarios.then: Destination is updated with source's non-zero values

and: All fields in dst remain unchanged
scenarios.given: Destination StreamTokenCounts with existing values and source with all zero values
scenarios.name: Merge all-zero source
scenarios.then: Destination remains unchanged

and: dst.InputTokens, OutputTokens, ReasoningTokens, CachedTokens, CacheCreationTokens, and CostUsd all updated
scenarios.given: Destination with partial values and source with all non-zero values
scenarios.name: Complete merge overwrite
scenarios.then: Destination is fully updated with all source values

data.purpose: Extracts and merges token usage counts and costs from streaming and non-streaming API responses across multiple provider formats including OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, and Grok CLI. *(analyzed in 2 chunks)*

data.name: BodyTokenExtractor

operations.description: Parses a complete response body and extracts token counts from Gemini usageMetadata and OpenAI Responses API usage shapes.
operations.name: Extract tokens from response body

scenarios.given: Response body contains Gemini usageMetadata with promptTokenCount > 0
scenarios.name: Gemini body extraction
scenarios.then: Returns populated StreamTokenCounts

scenarios.given: Response body contains OpenAI Responses API usage with non-zero tokens
scenarios.name: OpenAI Responses API body extraction
scenarios.then: Returns populated StreamTokenCounts

and: All token count fields remain zero
scenarios.given: Response body contains JSON without recognized usage shapes
scenarios.name: Unknown body format
scenarios.then: Returns zero StreamTokenCounts

data.purpose: Extracts token usage metrics from complete (non-streaming) response bodies, supporting Gemini and OpenAI Responses API formats. *(analyzed in 2 chunks)*

data.name: MessagesHandler

operations.description: SHALL accept and process POST /v1/messages requests in Anthropic format, performing request validation, model authorization, token budget checks, caching, request routing, failover handling, streaming/non-streaming execution, and usage logging.
operations.name: Messages

and: Request body is parsed and validated
Model is checked against allowed models
Cache is checked for exact hits
Request is executed with failover support
Cache is stored for successful responses
scenarios.given: A valid Anthropic-format chat completion request with authorized model and sufficient token budget
scenarios.name: Successful non-streaming request
scenarios.then: Response is returned with generated content and usage metadata

and: Failover is applied on connection errors
scenarios.given: A streaming-enabled request with valid parameters
scenarios.name: Successful streaming request
scenarios.then: Server-sent events are streamed to client with proper termination

and: Error response is formatted as Anthropic-compatible error
scenarios.given: A request with disallowed model or exceeded token budget
scenarios.name: Request validation failure
scenarios.then: Appropriate 4xx error is returned with error type and message

and: Cache key is computed from request
scenarios.given: An exact cache hit for the request
scenarios.name: Cache hit optimization
scenarios.then: Cached response is returned immediately with reduced latency
scenarios.when: Messages handler checks exact cache before execution

and: Ban count is reset on success
Error response is built on exhaustion
scenarios.given: A request where primary connection fails but alternatives exist
scenarios.name: Connection failover
scenarios.then: Request is retried on fallback connection and succeeds or returns aggregated error
scenarios.when: Primary connection returns error or is unavailable

and: Fusion request handler is invoked for multi-model scenarios
Combo request handler is invoked for capability-based routing
scenarios.given: A request requiring model fusion or combo routing
scenarios.name: Advanced routing patterns
scenarios.then: Request is delegated to fusion or combo handler with proper context
scenarios.when: Messages handler detects fusion or combo routing requirements

operations.description: SHALL handle streaming Claude responses by reading stream events, managing connection lifecycle, tracking rate limits, persisting Codex-specific data, and detecting client cancellation.
operations.name: handleClaudeStreamResponse

and: Stream events are flushed to client
Rate limit headers are parsed from connection
Codex data is persisted if applicable
scenarios.given: An active streaming response from Claude executor
scenarios.name: Normal stream processing
scenarios.then: All events are proxied to client with proper SSE formatting

scenarios.given: A client that closes connection mid-stream
scenarios.name: Client cancellation handling
scenarios.then: Streaming stops gracefully without error propagation
scenarios.when: Client connection is closed during stream

scenarios.given: A stream that encounters errors or requires holdback
scenarios.name: Stream error and holdback
scenarios.then: Error is handled via failover mechanism or holdback is applied

data.purpose: Handles Anthropic-format message API requests including chat completions and streaming responses, with full lifecycle management from validation through execution, caching, failover, and usage tracking.
sideEffects: Reads HTTP request body
Writes HTTP responses (streaming and non-streaming)
