Reads from and writes to exact cache
Executes requests via executor registry
Accumulates usage metrics
Logs requests
Persists Codex-specific data
Updates connection state (rate limits, cooldowns, bans)
May send failover requests to alternative connections

data.name: CountTokensHandler

operations.description: SHALL accept POST /v1/messages/count_tokens requests, validate the model, resolve the appropriate executor, and delegate token counting to the executor's token counter.
operations.name: CountTokens

and: Request body is read and parsed
Model is validated against allowed models
Executor is resolved from model string
Token counter is invoked with proxied context
scenarios.given: A valid token count request with authorized model
scenarios.name: Successful token counting
scenarios.then: Token count is returned in Anthropic-compatible format

and: Error is formatted as claudeError response
scenarios.given: A request with invalid model or unavailable executor
scenarios.name: Token count validation failure
scenarios.then: Appropriate 4xx error is returned
scenarios.when: CountTokens handler cannot resolve model or executor

and: Proxy context is built with candidates
scenarios.given: A request requiring specific connection routing
scenarios.name: Connection-routed token counting
scenarios.then: Token count is computed using selected connection's capabilities
scenarios.when: CountTokens handler resolves connection for provider

data.purpose: Provides token counting endpoint for Anthropic-format messages, delegating to model-specific token counters with proper validation and routing.
sideEffects: Reads HTTP request body
Writes HTTP response
Resolves executor via registry
Obtains connection from pool
Invokes token counter on executor

subSpecs.name: captureStartTime

operations.description: The system SHALL capture the current timestamp at the beginning of request processing to enable latency measurement.
operations.name: recordRequestStart

and: the returned timestamp is stored in a variable named start
scenarios.given: a new request arrives at the CountTokens endpoint
scenarios.name: successfulTimestampCapture
scenarios.then: the timestamp represents the current wall-clock time

subSpecs.purpose: Captures the start time for request latency tracking.

subSpecs.name: readRequestBody

operations.description: The system SHALL read the HTTP request body from the gin context with a maximum size limit enforced by http.MaxBytesReader.
operations.name: readBodyWithSizeLimit

scenarios.given: a valid HTTP request with a body smaller than the configured maximum size
scenarios.name: successfulBodyRead
scenarios.then: the complete body bytes are returned without error
scenarios.when: readBody is called with the gin context

scenarios.given: an HTTP request with a body exceeding the configured maximum size
scenarios.name: bodyTooLargeError
scenarios.then: errBodyTooLarge is returned and the body is not fully read
scenarios.when: readBody is called with the gin context

and: the returned error is not errBodyTooLarge
scenarios.given: an HTTP request where the underlying reader fails
scenarios.name: otherReadError
scenarios.then: errReadBody is returned wrapping the underlying error
scenarios.when: readBody is called with the gin context

subSpecs.purpose: Reads and validates the HTTP request body with size limits.

subSpecs.name: errorTypeCheck

operations.description: The system SHALL determine whether an error matches a specific sentinel error using errors.Is for proper error classification.
operations.name: checkErrorIdentity

scenarios.given: an error chain containing errBodyTooLarge
scenarios.name: detectBodyTooLarge
scenarios.then: errors.Is returns true when checked against errBodyTooLarge
scenarios.when: errors.Is is called with the error and errBodyTooLarge

scenarios.given: an error that is not errBodyTooLarge
scenarios.name: rejectNonMatchingError
scenarios.then: errors.Is returns false when checked against errBodyTooLarge
scenarios.when: errors.Is is called with the error and errBodyTooLarge

subSpecs.purpose: Performs sentinel error comparison for error type classification.

subSpecs.name: buildClaudeError

operations.description: The system SHALL construct a standardized Claude-format error response object with the specified error type and message.
operations.name: createClaudeErrorResponse

and: the returned gin.H contains 'type' and 'error' keys
scenarios.given: an error type of 'invalid_request_error' and message 'body required'
scenarios.name: standardClaudeError
scenarios.then: the returned map has type='invalid_request_error' and error.message='body required'
scenarios.when: claudeError is called with the error type and message

subSpecs.purpose: Constructs standardized Claude API error response objects.

subSpecs.name: extractErrorMessage

operations.description: The system SHALL extract the human-readable error message string from an error value.
operations.name: getErrorString

scenarios.given: an error with message 'failed to read body'
scenarios.name: extractErrorMessage
scenarios.then: the returned string equals 'failed to read body'
scenarios.when: err.Error is called on the error

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves the string representation of an error for logging or response inclusion.

subSpecs.name: extractReadBodyError

operations.description: The system SHALL extract the specific error message from a body read failure error.
operations.name: getReadBodyErrorString

and: the returned string describes the read failure
scenarios.given: a body read error from a network timeout
scenarios.name: readBodyErrorMessage
scenarios.then: the returned string contains details about the read failure cause

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves the string message from body read errors for diagnostic purposes.

subSpecs.name: compressRequestBody

operations.description: The system SHALL asynchronously compress the request body when cache control conditions are met, without blocking the request.
operations.name: asyncBodyCompression

scenarios.given: a request body that qualifies for compression based on cache control headers
scenarios.name: compressionApplied
scenarios.then: the compressed body is returned and compression metrics are recorded

and: the original body is returned unchanged
scenarios.given: a request body that does not qualify for compression
scenarios.name: compressionSkipped
scenarios.then: the original body is returned without modification

subSpecs.purpose: Asynchronously compresses request bodies based on cache control conditions.

subSpecs.name: extractJSONField

operations.description: The system SHALL extract a specific field value from a JSON document using a path expression.
operations.name: getJSONFieldValue

and: the returned value is the extracted string
scenarios.given: a JSON body with field 'model' at path 'model'
scenarios.name: extractModelField
scenarios.then: the value 'claude-3-opus-20240229' is returned
scenarios.when: executor.JSONGet is called with body and path 'model'

and: the returned error indicates path not found
scenarios.given: a JSON body without the requested field
scenarios.name: missingFieldError
scenarios.then: an error is returned indicating the path was not found
scenarios.when: executor.JSONGet is called with a non-existent path

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts values from JSON request bodies using path expressions.

subSpecs.name: validateModelAccess

operations.description: The system SHALL verify that the requested model is in the allowed models list for the current context.
operations.name: checkModelPermission

scenarios.given: a request with model 'claude-3-opus-20240229' and context containing this model in allowedModels
scenarios.name: allowedModel
scenarios.then: isModelAllowed returns true
scenarios.when: isModelAllowed is called with the model and context

scenarios.given: a request with model 'unauthorized-model' not in the allowed models list
scenarios.name: disallowedModel
scenarios.then: isModelAllowed returns false and the request should be rejected
scenarios.when: isModelAllowed is called with the model and context

subSpecs.purpose: Validates that the requested model is permitted for the current request context.

subSpecs.name: detectStreamingRequest

operations.description: The system SHALL determine whether the request specifies streaming based on the request body content.
operations.name: checkStreamFlag

scenarios.given: a request body with 'stream': true
scenarios.name: streamingEnabled
scenarios.then: executor.IsStreamRequest returns true
scenarios.when: executor.IsStreamRequest is called with the body

scenarios.given: a request body with 'stream': false or absent
scenarios.name: streamingDisabled
scenarios.then: executor.IsStreamRequest returns false
scenarios.when: executor.IsStreamRequest is called with the body

subSpecs.purpose: Detects whether the request enables streaming based on body content.

subSpecs.name: enforceTokenBudget

operations.description: The system SHALL verify that the request does not exceed the API key's token budget and abort the request if exceeded.
operations.name: validateTokenBudget

scenarios.given: a request within the API key's remaining token budget
scenarios.name: budgetSufficient
scenarios.then: checkTokenBudget returns without aborting and processing continues

scenarios.given: a request exceeding the API key's token budget
scenarios.name: budgetExceeded
scenarios.then: the context is aborted with status 429 and an error response

subSpecs.purpose: Enforces API key token budget limits before processing requests.

subSpecs.name: generateExactCacheKey

operations.description: The system SHALL generate a deterministic cache key from the request body, model, and stream flag for exact-match caching.
operations.name: computeCacheKey

and: the returned string is a hash or encoded value
scenarios.given: a request body, model 'claude-3-opus-20240229', and stream=false
scenarios.name: deterministicCacheKey
scenarios.then: the same inputs always produce the same cache key string

scenarios.given: two requests differing only in the stream flag
scenarios.name: streamAffectsCacheKey
scenarios.then: the generated cache keys are different
scenarios.when: exactCacheKey is called for both requests

subSpecs.purpose: Generates deterministic cache keys for exact-match request caching.

subSpecs.name: serveCachedResponse

operations.description: The system SHALL serve a cached response when available and deduct the cached token count against the API key budget.
operations.name: returnCacheHit

and: the response is written from cache
scenarios.given: a matching cache entry exists for the request
scenarios.name: cacheHitServed
scenarios.then: the cached response is returned and true is returned

scenarios.given: no matching cache entry or cache entry is expired
scenarios.name: cacheMiss
scenarios.then: false is returned and normal processing continues

subSpecs.purpose: Serves cached responses and accounts for token budget usage on cache hits.

subSpecs.name: detectComboCapabilities

operations.description: The system SHALL analyze the request to determine which combo capabilities are required for processing.
operations.name: analyzeRequiredCapabilities

scenarios.given: a complex request requiring multiple model capabilities
scenarios.name: multiCapabilityDetection
scenarios.then: the required capabilities are identified and returned

subSpecs.purpose: Identifies required combo capabilities from request content.

subSpecs.name: handleFusionRequest

operations.description: The system SHALL synthesize panel answers from multiple models into a single unified response using a fusion model.
operations.name: synthesizeFusionResponse

and: the response is streamed or returned as a single object
scenarios.given: a combo result with multiple panel responses and effectiveStrategy='fusion'
scenarios.name: fusionSynthesis
scenarios.then: a single synthesized response is generated from the panel answers and returned to the client

subSpecs.purpose: Synthesizes multiple model responses into a single fusion response.

subSpecs.name: handleComboRequest

operations.description: The system SHALL handle combo request failures with detailed per-step error information rather than generic failure messages.
operations.name: processComboWithDetails

and: error response includes which steps failed and why

scenarios.name: detailedComboFailure

scenarios.when: handleComboRequest is called with a partially failed combo result

subSpecs.name: splitModelIdentifier

operations.description: The system SHALL parse a model identifier into its provider and model name components.

scenarios.given: a malformed model identifier without separator

scenarios.then: an error indicating invalid format is returned

subSpecs.name: resolveExecutor

operations.description: The system SHALL locate and return the appropriate executor and provider format for a given provider and model combination.

and: the returned executor supports the model
scenarios.given: provider 'anthropic' and model 'claude-3-opus-20240229'

scenarios.then: a valid executor and Anthropic format are returned

scenarios.given: an unsupported provider or model combination
scenarios.name: executorNotFound
scenarios.then: an error indicating no executor is available is returned

subSpecs.name: modifyJSONField

operations.description: The system SHALL set or update a specific field in a JSON document at the given path with the provided value.
operations.name: setJSONField

and: the returned body contains the updated field
scenarios.given: a JSON body and path 'model' with new value 'claude-3-haiku-20240307'

and: an error is returned for invalid path

scenarios.name: invalidPathError
scenarios.then: an error indicating the path cannot be set is returned

subSpecs.purpose: Modifies JSON documents by setting values at specified paths.

data.name: ClaudeErrorFormatter

subSpecs.name: captureTimestamp

operations.description: The system SHALL capture the current UTC timestamp at the start of request processing.

and: The returned timestamp is recorded in the request context

scenarios.name: requestStartTimestamp

subSpecs.name: readRequestBody

operations.description: The system SHALL read the raw HTTP request body from the gin context without exceeding the configured maximum size limit.

and: The body bytes are returned for downstream processing

subSpecs.name: errorTypeCheck

and: The comparison traverses wrapped errors in the chain

scenarios.then: errors.Is returns true when checked against errBodyTooLarge
scenarios.when: errors.Is is called with the error and errBodyTooLarge target

subSpecs.name: formatClaudeError

operations.description: The system SHALL construct an Anthropic-compatible error response object with the specified error type and message.

and: The response follows Anthropic API error schema conventions
scenarios.given: An invalid_model error needs to be returned to the client

scenarios.then: A gin.H map contains 'type' and 'message' fields with the provided values
scenarios.when: claudeError is called with 'invalid_request_error' and 'Model not found'

subSpecs.name: extractErrorMessage

operations.description: The system SHALL retrieve the human-readable error message string from an error value.
operations.name: getErrorString

and: The string is suitable for logging or client-facing messages
scenarios.given: A non-nil error from body reading
scenarios.name: errorMessageExtraction
scenarios.then: The error's descriptive text is returned
scenarios.when: Error() is called on the error value

subSpecs.purpose: Obtains the string representation of an error for logging, debugging, or response inclusion.

subSpecs.name: extractReadBodyError

operations.description: The system SHALL retrieve the specific error message from a body read failure for diagnostic purposes.
operations.name: getReadBodyErrorString

and: The message distinguishes this from errBodyTooLarge
scenarios.given: A body read operation failed due to network error
scenarios.name: readBodyErrorMessage
scenarios.then: The underlying read failure description is returned
scenarios.when: Error() is called on the errReadBody value

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts descriptive text from body read errors to differentiate failure modes in error handling.

subSpecs.name: compressRequestBody

operations.description: The system SHALL compress the request body when cache control headers indicate compression is appropriate.
operations.name: applyCompression

scenarios.given: A request body with cache-control allowing compression
scenarios.name: successfulCompression
scenarios.then: The compressed byte slice is returned

operations.description: The system SHALL return the original body unchanged when compression is not indicated or applicable.
operations.name: skipCompression

and: No metrics are recorded for skipped compression
scenarios.given: A request body with no-cache directive
scenarios.name: compressionBypass
scenarios.then: The original uncompressed body is returned
scenarios.when: compressRequestBody determines compression is not needed

subSpecs.purpose: Conditionally compresses request bodies based on cache control policies without blocking request processing.

subSpecs.name: extractJSONField

operations.description: The system SHALL extract a value from a JSON document at the specified path using dot notation.
operations.name: getJSONValue

and: The path uses dot notation for nested access
scenarios.given: A JSON body containing {"model": "claude-3-opus"}
scenarios.name: extractModelField
scenarios.then: The string 'claude-3-opus' is returned

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves values from JSON request bodies using path-based navigation for parameter extraction.

subSpecs.name: validateModelAccess

operations.description: The system SHALL determine whether the requested model is permitted for the current API key context.
operations.name: checkModelPermission

and: Allowed models are derived from context metadata
scenarios.given: An API key restricted to specific model families
scenarios.name: allowedModelCheck
scenarios.then: True is returned for permitted models, false otherwise
scenarios.when: isModelAllowed is called with a model ID

subSpecs.purpose: Enforces API key-level model access controls by checking against context-derived allowed model sets.

subSpecs.name: detectStreamingRequest

operations.description: The system SHALL determine whether the request specifies streaming response delivery.
operations.name: checkStreamFlag

and: The detection is based on the 'stream' field in the request body
scenarios.given: A request body with '{"stream": true}'
scenarios.name: streamingEnabled
scenarios.then: True is returned indicating streaming mode

subSpecs.purpose: Identifies streaming mode requests to route to appropriate response handling paths.

subSpecs.name: enforceTokenBudget

operations.description: The system SHALL verify that the requested token usage does not exceed the API key's allocated budget.
operations.name: validateBudget

and: Budget information is retrieved from context
scenarios.given: An API key with 1000 remaining tokens in budget
scenarios.name: budgetWithinLimit
scenarios.then: Nil error is returned allowing request continuation
scenarios.when: checkTokenBudget is called with 500 requested tokens

operations.description: The system SHALL abort the request with a 429 status when the token budget is exceeded.
operations.name: rejectOverBudget

and: The response includes a budget exceeded error message
scenarios.given: An API key with 100 remaining tokens in budget
scenarios.name: budgetExceeded
scenarios.then: The context is aborted with status 429 and an error is returned
scenarios.when: checkTokenBudget is called with 500 requested tokens

subSpecs.purpose: Prevents API key token budget overruns by validating requests against remaining quota before execution.

subSpecs.name: generateCacheKey

operations.description: The system SHALL compute a deterministic cache key from request body, model, and stream flag.
operations.name: computeExactKey

and: The key is stable for identical inputs across requests
scenarios.given: A request body, model 'claude-3-opus', and stream=false
scenarios.name: deterministicCacheKey
scenarios.then: A consistent string key is returned
scenarios.when: exactCacheKey is called with the same parameters twice

subSpecs.purpose: Creates deterministic cache keys for exact-match request caching to enable response reuse.

subSpecs.name: serveCachedResponse

operations.description: The system SHALL serve a cached response when an exact cache match exists and budget is available.
operations.name: returnCacheHit

and: Request tokens are deducted from API key budget before response
scenarios.given: A cache entry exists for the current request and sufficient budget remains
scenarios.name: successfulCacheServe
scenarios.then: True is returned, the cached response is written, and budget is charged
scenarios.when: serveCacheHit is called with matching cache entry

operations.description: The system SHALL return false without writing a response when no valid cache entry exists or budget is insufficient.
operations.name: cacheMissOrBudgetFail

and: No response is written to the client
scenarios.given: No cache entry exists for the request
scenarios.name: noCacheMatch
scenarios.then: False is returned with no side effects
scenarios.when: serveCacheHit is called with nil or non-matching entry

subSpecs.purpose: Delivers cached responses while enforcing token budget accounting for cache hits.

subSpecs.name: detectCapabilities

operations.description: The system SHALL analyze the request to determine which model capabilities are required for processing.
operations.name: identifyRequiredCapabilities

and: Capabilities include vision, tools, and extended thinking
scenarios.given: A request containing image content and tool use
scenarios.name: multiCapabilityDetection
scenarios.then: Vision and tool capabilities are identified as required

subSpecs.purpose: Identifies model capability requirements from request content to inform routing and model selection.

subSpecs.name: handleFusionRequest

operations.description: The system SHALL synthesize multiple panel responses into a single coherent response using a fusion model.
operations.name: synthesizePanelResponses

and: The fusion model is selected based on the effective strategy
scenarios.given: A combo result with three panel responses requiring synthesis
scenarios.name: fusionSynthesis
scenarios.then: A single unified response is streamed or returned to the client
scenarios.when: handleFusionRequest is called with the combo result

subSpecs.purpose: Combines multiple model panel outputs into one client-facing response through a synthesis model.

subSpecs.name: handleComboRequest

operations.description: The system SHALL execute combo routing with informative error messages indicating which specific steps failed.
operations.name: executeComboWithDetails

and: Failure details include attempted connection and model information
scenarios.given: A combo request where one panel step fails
scenarios.name: comboPartialFailure
scenarios.then: The error response specifies which connection/model failed rather than generic failure
scenarios.when: handleComboRequest processes a failed combo result

subSpecs.purpose: Processes combo routing requests with detailed failure attribution for debugging and client transparency.

subSpecs.name: splitModelIdentifier

operations.description: The system SHALL decompose a model identifier into its provider and model name components.
operations.name: parseModelID

scenarios.given: A model identifier 'anthropic/claude-3-opus'
scenarios.name: validModelSplit
scenarios.then: Provider 'anthropic' and model 'claude-3-opus' are returned

subSpecs.purpose: Parses composite model identifiers to extract provider and model components for executor resolution.

subSpecs.name: resolveExecutor

operations.description: The system SHALL locate and return the appropriate executor for the specified provider and model combination.
operations.name: findExecutor

and: The provider format indicates the API dialect supported
scenarios.given: Provider 'anthropic' and model 'claude-3-opus'
scenarios.name: executorResolution
scenarios.then: An Anthropic-compatible executor and format are returned

subSpecs.purpose: Maps provider/model pairs to their corresponding execution engines and API format handlers.

subSpecs.name: modifyJSONField

operations.description: The system SHALL update or insert a value in a JSON document at the specified path.
operations.name: setJSONValue

and: The path uses dot notation for nested access
scenarios.given: A JSON body and path 'model' with new value 'claude-3-haiku'
scenarios.name: updateModelField
scenarios.then: The modified JSON body contains the updated model value

subSpecs.purpose: Modifies JSON request bodies by setting values at specified paths for request transformation.

subSpecs.name: registerRequest

operations.description: The system SHALL record request metadata in the registry for observability and rate tracking.
operations.name: trackRequest

and: Registration includes timestamp, model, and API key information
scenarios.given: A new incoming request to the orchestrator
scenarios.name: requestRegistration
scenarios.then: The request is recorded in the registry with unique identification
scenarios.when: registry.Request is called with request details

subSpecs.purpose: Maintains request

data.name: EmbeddingsHandler

operations.description: SHALL validate and normalize the incoming embedding request body, ensuring required fields are present and well-formed.
operations.name: validateEmbeddingRequest

scenarios.given: A POST request to /v1/embeddings with empty or missing model field
scenarios.name: Missing model field
scenarios.then: Request SHALL be rejected with invalid_request_error

scenarios.given: A POST request to /v1/embeddings with malformed JSON body
scenarios.name: Malformed request body
scenarios.then: Request SHALL be rejected with invalid_request_error

operations.description: SHALL enforce model access control by verifying the requested model is in the allowlist for the authenticated API key.
operations.name: enforceModelAccessControl

and: Error response contains 'model not allowed for this API key'
scenarios.given: An authenticated request with API key restricted to specific models
scenarios.name: Model not in allowlist
scenarios.then: Request SHALL be rejected with invalid_request_error
scenarios.when: Requested model is not in the API key's allowed models list

and: Request proceeds to token budget check
scenarios.given: An authenticated request with API key that has model allowlist
scenarios.name: Model explicitly allowed
scenarios.then: Request SHALL proceed to next validation stage
scenarios.when: Requested model is in the API key's allowed models list

operations.description: SHALL verify the request does not exceed the API key's token budget quota before processing.
operations.name: checkTokenBudgetQuota

scenarios.given: An API key with configured token budget that has been exhausted
scenarios.name: Token budget exceeded
scenarios.then: Request SHALL be rejected with rate limit error
scenarios.when: checkTokenBudget determines remaining quota is insufficient

scenarios.given: An API key with configured token budget that has remaining quota
scenarios.name: Token budget available
scenarios.then: Request SHALL proceed to next validation stage

operations.description: SHALL resolve the appropriate executor for the provider and verify provider supports embedding service kind.
operations.name: resolveEmbeddingExecutor

and: Error response contains 'provider not configured for embeddings'
scenarios.given: A provider type without configured service_kinds in database
scenarios.name: Provider not configured for embeddings
scenarios.then: Request SHALL be rejected with invalid_request_error
scenarios.when: Database query for provider service kinds fails

and: Error response contains 'provider does not support embeddings'
scenarios.given: A provider type with service_kinds that lacks embedding capability
scenarios.name: Provider lacks embedding service kind
scenarios.then: Request SHALL be rejected with invalid_request_error
scenarios.when: providerpkg.HasServiceKind confirms embedding is not supported

and: Error response contains 'embeddings only supported for OpenAI-compatible providers'
scenarios.given: A provider whose executor does not implement EmbeddingsExecutor interface
scenarios.name: Executor type mismatch
scenarios.then: Request SHALL be rejected with invalid_request_error

operations.description: SHALL validate the model is available for embeddings on the provider through the modalities registry when provider has registered embedding models.
operations.name: validateModelModality

and: Error response contains 'model not available for embeddings on provider'
scenarios.given: A provider with registered embedding models in modalities registry
scenarios.name: Model not in modalities registry
scenarios.then: Request SHALL be rejected with invalid_request_error
scenarios.when: Normalized model name is not found in provider's embedding models

scenarios.given: A provider with no registered embedding models in modalities registry
scenarios.name: Empty modalities registry
scenarios.then: Request SHALL skip modality validation and proceed
scenarios.when: modalities.Models returns empty slice for provider

operations.description: SHALL acquire an available connection for the provider and model, with proactive token refresh for expiring credentials.
operations.name: acquireProviderConnection

scenarios.given: All connections for provider/model are at capacity or unhealthy
scenarios.name: No available connection
scenarios.then: Request SHALL fail with server_error

and: Connection credentials refreshed if near expiry
scenarios.given: A valid connection with access token near expiration
scenarios.name: Proactive token refresh
scenarios.then: Connection SHALL be returned with refreshed credentials
scenarios.when: proactiveRefreshToken is called on acquired connection

operations.description: SHALL execute the embedding request through the provider's executor with retry logic for transient failures.
operations.name: executeEmbeddingWithRetry

and: Upstream error response proxied to client with original status code
scenarios.given: An embedding request that receives structured error from upstream
scenarios.name: Upstream error with body
scenarios.then: Original upstream error body and status SHALL be returned to client
scenarios.when: executeWithRetry returns UpstreamError with non-empty body

and: Client error written with appropriate logging
scenarios.given: An embedding request that fails with client-side error
scenarios.name: Client-side execution failure
scenarios.then: Error SHALL be handled by writeUpstreamClientError

operations.description: SHALL record API usage metrics including latency, token consumption, and quota accumulation for billing.
operations.name: recordEmbeddingUsage

and: Usage data persisted to usage tracking system
scenarios.given: A successful embedding response
scenarios.name: Successful usage accumulation
scenarios.then: Usage metrics SHALL be accumulated via accumulateAPIKeyUsage

and: Request logged with duration and device tracking
scenarios.given: Any embedding request completion
scenarios.name: Request logging
scenarios.then: Request details SHALL be logged via logRequest with timing

data.purpose: Handles HTTP requests for the OpenAI-compatible /v1/embeddings endpoint, orchestrating request validation, model access control, provider resolution, connection management, and execution of embedding generation with usage tracking and error handling.
sideEffects: Reads request body from HTTP connection
Writes HTTP response with JSON error or success body
Queries provider_types database table for service_kinds
Acquires connection from connection pool
Makes upstream HTTP request to embedding provider
Writes usage metrics to usage tracking system
Logs request details via structured logging
May refresh OAuth tokens proactively
Applies request body compression if configured

subSpecs.name: captureTimestamp

operations.description: The system SHALL capture the current time at the start of request processing for latency measurement and usage logging.
operations.name: captureRequestStartTime

and: timestamp is recorded in usage log entry
scenarios.given: an incoming embeddings request is received
scenarios.name: timestampCapturedForLatency
scenarios.then: the recorded start time is within 1 second of actual request arrival time
scenarios.when: time.Now is invoked at handler entry

subSpecs.purpose: Captures the current timestamp to enable request latency measurement and usage logging.

subSpecs.name: readRequestBody

operations.description: The system SHALL read the HTTP request body and enforce a maximum size limit, returning errBodyTooLarge when exceeded.
operations.name: enforceBodySizeLimit

and: HTTP 413 status is returned to client
scenarios.given: a request body exceeds the configured maximum size
scenarios.name: bodyTooLargeRejected
scenarios.then: readBody returns errBodyTooLarge with nil body
scenarios.when: readBody is called with oversized request

and: body is available for downstream parsing
scenarios.given: a request body within size limits
scenarios.name: bodyReadSuccessfully
scenarios.then: readBody returns full body content with nil error
scenarios.when: readBody is called with valid request

operations.description: The system SHALL return errReadBody for any non-size-limit read failure.
operations.name: handleReadFailures

and: HTTP 400 status is returned to client
scenarios.given: a network error occurs during body read
scenarios.name: networkReadError
scenarios.then: readBody returns errReadBody containing the underlying error
scenarios.when: io.ReadAll fails due to connection reset

subSpecs.purpose: Reads and validates the HTTP request body, enforcing size limits and handling read failures.

subSpecs.name: writeBodyReadError

operations.description: The system SHALL write an appropriate HTTP error response based on the type of body read failure.
operations.name: mapErrorToResponse
outputs: HTTP response written to gin context

and: response body contains 'Request body too large' message
scenarios.given: errBodyTooLarge is returned from readBody
scenarios.name: bodyTooLargeResponse
scenarios.then: writeReadBodyError writes HTTP 413 with error details

and: response body contains generic read failure message
scenarios.given: errReadBody is returned from readBody
scenarios.name: genericReadErrorResponse
scenarios.then: writeReadBodyError writes HTTP 400 with error details

subSpecs.purpose: Translates body read errors into appropriate HTTP error responses for the client.

subSpecs.name: trackClientDevice

operations.description: The system SHALL extract and record device identification from the request context for analytics, regardless of authentication status.
operations.name: extractDeviceInfo

and: device ID appears in usage logs
scenarios.given: a request contains X-Device-ID header
scenarios.name: deviceHeaderPresent
scenarios.then: trackDevice extracts and stores the device identifier

scenarios.given: a request has no device identification headers
scenarios.name: noDeviceHeader
scenarios.then: trackDevice stores empty or default device identifier
scenarios.when: trackDevice is called on unauthenticated request

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts and records client device information from request context for analytics tracking.

subSpecs.name: compressRequestPayload

operations.name: conditionalCompression

and: compression ratio is recorded in metrics
scenarios.given: a request body with cache-control: compressible

scenarios.then: compressRequestBody returns compressed body and records metrics

scenarios.given: a request body without compressible cache-control

scenarios.then: compressRequestBody returns original body unchanged

subSpecs.name: extractJSONField

and: error propagates to client as 400 response

subSpecs.name: enforceTokenBudget
