operations.description: SHALL normalize Cloudflare-style model identifiers from 'provider/author/model' format to '@provider/author/model' format used by the modalities registry.
operations.name: normalizeCloudflareModelID

scenarios.given: A Cloudflare Workers AI model with provider 'cf' and model 'author/model'
scenarios.name: Cloudflare model normalization
scenarios.then: Model SHALL be prefixed with '@' to match registry format '@cf/author/model'

scenarios.given: A non-Cloudflare provider or already-normalized model name
scenarios.name: Non-Cloudflare passthrough
scenarios.then: Model name SHALL be returned without modification
scenarios.when: normalizeEmbeddingModel is called with provider other than 'cf'

data.purpose: Normalizes model identifier formats between API request conventions and internal registry representations, specifically handling Cloudflare Workers AI's '@' prefix convention.

data.name: ImageGenerationHandler

operations.description: SHALL accept and process POST /v1/images/generations requests by parsing the request body, validating model permissions, checking token budgets, resolving the appropriate executor, establishing provider connections, and orchestrating image generation through the execution pipeline.
operations.name: Images
outputs: HTTP response with generated image data

and: Model is in allowed models list
scenarios.given: Valid image generation request with supported model
scenarios.name: Successful image generation
scenarios.then: Returns 200 OK with generated image URLs or base64 data

and: Model is not in allowed models list
scenarios.given: Image generation request with disallowed model
scenarios.name: Model not allowed
scenarios.then: Returns 4xx error indicating model is not permitted

scenarios.given: Image generation request exceeding token budget
scenarios.name: Token budget exceeded
scenarios.then: Returns 4xx error indicating insufficient token budget

and: Request body exceeds maximum size limit
scenarios.given: Image generation request with oversized body
scenarios.name: Request body too large
scenarios.then: Returns 4xx error indicating request body exceeds limit

and: Provider connection fails or returns error
scenarios.given: Valid request but upstream provider error
scenarios.name: Upstream provider error
scenarios.then: Returns 5xx or proxied error response from provider
scenarios.when: Images handler executes request through provider

data.purpose: Handles HTTP requests for image generation endpoints, orchestrating request validation, authorization, provider resolution, and execution of image generation workflows through the proxy pipeline.
sideEffects: Reads HTTP request body from client
Writes HTTP response to client
Calls upstream provider APIs for image generation
Updates usage metrics and token budgets
Logs request details for observability
Potentially refreshes authentication tokens proactively

data.name: ImageGeneratorAdapter

operations.description: SHALL adapt and execute image generation requests through the executor framework by delegating to the underlying images executor implementation.
operations.name: Execute

and: Underlying images executor is properly configured
scenarios.given: Valid image generation request in executor format
scenarios.name: Successful adapted execution
scenarios.then: Returns executor response with generated image data
scenarios.when: Execute is called on image generator adapter

scenarios.given: Image generation request that fails in executor
scenarios.name: Executor failure
scenarios.then: Returns error from underlying executor

operations.description: SHALL adapt and execute streaming image generation requests through the executor framework by delegating to the underlying images executor's streaming capabilities.
operations.name: ExecuteStream

scenarios.given: Valid streaming image generation request
scenarios.name: Successful streaming execution
scenarios.then: Returns stream result with streaming response handle
scenarios.when: ExecuteStream is called on image generator adapter

and: Streaming not supported by underlying executor
scenarios.given: Image generation request for non-streaming capable model
scenarios.name: Streaming not supported
scenarios.then: Returns error indicating streaming is not available
scenarios.when: ExecuteStream is called on unsupported configuration

data.purpose: Adapts the image generation handler to the executor framework interface, enabling standardized request execution and streaming support for image generation workloads through the proxy pipeline.
sideEffects: Delegates to underlying images executor for actual provider communication
May establish streaming connections to upstream providers

data.name: STTHandler

operations.description: The STT handler SHALL accept a multipart/form-data POST request to /v1/audio/transcriptions, validate the requested model against allowed models, enforce token budget constraints, select an available upstream connection, and forward the audio transcription request to the upstream provider.
operations.name: HandleSpeechToTextTranscription

and: At least one connection is available
scenarios.given: A valid multipart request with audio file and model parameter
scenarios.name: Successful transcription forwarding
scenarios.then: Request is forwarded to upstream provider and response is streamed back to client

scenarios.given: A request with a model not in the allowed models list
scenarios.name: Model not allowed
scenarios.then: Request is rejected with 400 Bad Request

scenarios.given: A request that would exceed the API key's token budget
scenarios.name: Token budget exceeded
scenarios.then: Request is rejected with 429 Too Many Requests

scenarios.given: All primary connections are in cooldown or unhealthy
scenarios.name: Connection fallback
scenarios.then: A fallback connection is selected or error is returned
scenarios.when: getConnection cannot find healthy primary connection

and: Usage is accumulated for the API key
scenarios.given: A successful upstream response
scenarios.name: Usage tracking
scenarios.then: API key usage is recorded with audio-specific metrics
scenarios.when: accumulateAPIKeyUsage is called after response completes

data.purpose: Handles HTTP requests for speech-to-text audio transcriptions by validating requests, enforcing quotas, selecting upstream connections, and proxying multipart audio data to provider backends.
sideEffects: Reads multipart form data from HTTP request
Writes HTTP response to client
Calls upstream provider API over network
Writes to usage/quota tracking system
Logs request details

data.name: MultipartBodyForwarder

operations.description: The forwardMultipartBody function SHALL read the original multipart form data from the incoming HTTP request and reconstruct it as a byte buffer with the appropriate content type for forwarding to upstream providers.
operations.name: ForwardMultipartBody

and: All file handles are properly closed
scenarios.given: A valid multipart/form-data request with file and form fields
scenarios.name: Successful body reconstruction
scenarios.then: Returns complete body bytes and correct multipart content type

and: File handles are closed on error
scenarios.given: A multipart request with unreadable file parts
scenarios.name: File read error handling
scenarios.then: Returns error and ensures resources are cleaned up

data.purpose: Reconstructs incoming multipart form data into a forwardable byte buffer while preserving form fields and file attachments for upstream proxying.
sideEffects: Reads HTTP request body
Allocates memory for buffer
Opens and reads uploaded file handles

subSpecs.name: timestampCapture

operations.description: The timestampCapture operation SHALL record the current system time for request lifecycle tracking and latency measurement.
operations.name: captureCurrentTime

and: the returned time value is non-zero
the returned time value represents UTC
scenarios.given: the system clock is operational and synchronized
scenarios.name: validSystemTimeCapture
scenarios.then: a valid time.Time value representing the current instant SHALL be returned
scenarios.when: timestamp capture is invoked during request processing

subSpecs.purpose: Captures the current system time to enable request timing, latency measurement, and cooldown period calculations.

subSpecs.name: headerExtraction

operations.description: The headerExtraction operation SHALL retrieve a specified HTTP header value from the incoming request context.
operations.name: extractRequestHeader

and: the returned value is the exact string from the header
empty string distinguishes absence from empty value
scenarios.given: an incoming HTTP request with headers present in the gin context
scenarios.name: existingHeaderRetrieval
scenarios.then: the corresponding header value string SHALL be returned
scenarios.when: a specific header key is requested

and: the caller can distinguish missing header from empty header via additional checks
scenarios.given: an incoming HTTP request without the specified header
scenarios.name: missingHeaderHandling
scenarios.then: an empty string SHALL be returned
scenarios.when: a non-existent header key is requested

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts specific HTTP header values from the incoming request for routing, authentication, and metadata propagation.

subSpecs.name: errorStringConversion

operations.description: The errorStringConversion operation SHALL convert an error interface value to its human-readable string representation.
operations.name: convertErrorToString

and: the output contains the complete error message
nil error handling is defined by caller
scenarios.given: a non-nil error value with a descriptive message
scenarios.name: validErrorConversion
scenarios.then: the error's message string SHALL be returned
scenarios.when: Error() is called on the error

scenarios.given: a nil error value
scenarios.name: nilErrorHandling
scenarios.then: the operation SHALL not panic and behavior is defined by the specific error type implementation

subSpecs.purpose: Converts error values into human-readable strings for logging, response formatting, and debugging.

subSpecs.name: formFieldExtraction

operations.description: The formFieldExtraction operation SHALL retrieve a specified form field value from parsed multipart form data.
operations.name: extractFormField

and: the returned value is the first value if multiple exist
scenarios.given: a parsed multipart form containing the specified field
scenarios.name: existingFormFieldRetrieval
scenarios.then: the form field value string SHALL be returned
scenarios.when: a specific form field key is requested

and: caller must use PostFormArray for multiple values
scenarios.given: a parsed multipart form without the specified field
scenarios.name: missingFormFieldHandling
scenarios.then: an empty string SHALL be returned
scenarios.when: a non-existent form field key is requested

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts named form field values from parsed multipart form data for request processing and validation.

subSpecs.name: modelAccessControl

operations.description: The modelAccessControl operation SHALL determine whether a requested model identifier is permitted for use by the current request context.
operations.name: checkModelPermission

and: the check considers context-derived allowed models
scenarios.given: a request context with configured allowed models and a model identifier to validate
scenarios.name: permittedModelAccess
scenarios.then: true SHALL be returned if the modelID exists in the allowed set
scenarios.when: modelIDAllowed is called with a permitted model

and: the check is case-sensitive unless specified otherwise
scenarios.given: a request context with configured allowed models and a model identifier to validate
scenarios.name: deniedModelAccess
scenarios.then: false SHALL be returned if the modelID does not exist in the allowed set
scenarios.when: modelIDAllowed is called with a non-permitted model

operations.description: The modelAccessControl operation SHALL retrieve the set of allowed models from the request context for validation purposes.
operations.name: retrieveAllowedModels

and: the models are derived from context values
empty set implies no restrictions or all denied based on policy
scenarios.given: a request context that may contain allowed model configuration
scenarios.name: contextModelRetrieval
scenarios.then: the set of allowed model identifiers SHALL be extracted from context
scenarios.when: allowedModelsFromContext is invoked during request processing

subSpecs.purpose: Validates whether a requested AI model is permitted for use based on context-derived access control policies.

subSpecs.name: modelPathParsing

operations.description: The modelPathParsing operation SHALL decompose a composite model identifier into its constituent provider and model name components.
operations.name: splitModelIdentifier

and: the split point is the first separator occurrence
provider defaults if no separator present
scenarios.given: a valid composite model path in format 'provider/model-name'
scenarios.name: validModelPathSplit
scenarios.then: the provider and model name components SHALL be returned separately
scenarios.when: SplitModel is called with a properly formatted path

scenarios.given: an empty or malformed model path string
scenarios.name: invalidModelPathHandling
scenarios.then: an error SHALL be returned describing the parsing failure
scenarios.when: SplitModel is called with invalid input

subSpecs.purpose: Parses composite model identifiers into separate provider and model name components for routing and connection selection.

subSpecs.name: fileResourceCleanup

operations.description: The fileResourceCleanup operation SHALL release operating system resources associated with an open file descriptor.
operations.name: closeFileHandle

and: subsequent operations on the file fail
the file descriptor is released to the OS
scenarios.given: an open file handle from multipart form processing
scenarios.name: successfulFileClose
scenarios.then: the file resources SHALL be released and nil error returned
scenarios.when: Close is called on a valid open file

and: error indicates resource leak or already-closed state
scenarios.given: a file handle that may already be closed or invalid
scenarios.name: idempotentCloseHandling
scenarios.then: an error MAY be returned if the file is already closed
scenarios.when: Close is called on a closed or invalid file

subSpecs.purpose: Releases file system resources for uploaded file handles to prevent descriptor exhaustion.

subSpecs.name: streamConsumption

operations.description: The streamConsumption operation SHALL read the complete contents of an io.Reader until EOF and return the accumulated byte slice.
operations.name: readCompleteStream

and: the returned slice length equals total bytes read
scenarios.given: a readable stream with finite, known content
scenarios.name: completeStreamRead
scenarios.then: all bytes from the reader SHALL be returned as a single byte slice
scenarios.when: ReadAll is called on the stream

and: error indicates read failure or partial consumption
scenarios.given: a stream that encounters an error during reading
scenarios.name: streamReadFailure
scenarios.then: the bytes read before failure and the error SHALL be returned

and: memory allocation is bounded by available RAM
scenarios.given: a stream larger than available memory
scenarios.name: memoryExhaustionHandling
scenarios.then: the operation may fail with memory allocation error
scenarios.when: ReadAll is called on an excessively large stream

subSpecs.purpose: Consumes entire readable streams into memory buffers for processing, forwarding, or transformation.

subSpecs.name: tokenBudgetValidation

operations.description: The tokenBudgetValidation operation SHALL extract the organization identifier from the request context for quota attribution.
operations.name: extractOrganizationID

and: the key is 'org' or organization-specific context key
scenarios.given: a gin context with organization metadata attached
scenarios.name: organizationContextRetrieval
scenarios.then: the organization identifier string SHALL be returned
scenarios.when: GetString is called with the organization key

operations.description: The tokenBudgetValidation operation SHALL retrieve arbitrary context values for budget calculation parameters.
operations.name: getContextValue

and: exists flag distinguishes zero value from missing key
scenarios.given: a gin context with various metadata keys
scenarios.name: genericContextRetrieval
scenarios.then: the value and existence flag SHALL be returned
scenarios.when: Get is called with any context key

operations.description: The tokenBudgetValidation operation SHALL convert error values to strings for response formatting.
operations.name: formatBudgetError

scenarios.given: a budget validation error occurs
scenarios.name: budgetErrorFormatting
scenarios.then: the error message SHALL be converted to string for response
scenarios.when: error is formatted for client response

operations.description: The tokenBudgetValidation operation SHALL abort the request with a JSON error when token budget is insufficient.
operations.name: abortWithBudgetError

and: the context is aborted preventing further handlers
scenarios.given: a request that exceeds available token budget
scenarios.name: budgetExceededResponse
scenarios.then: the specified HTTP status and JSON error body SHALL be written
scenarios.when: AbortWithStatusJSON is called with 429 or 403 status

operations.description: The tokenBudgetValidation operation SHALL create new error values for budget violation conditions.
operations.name: createBudgetError

and: error message is exactly the input text
scenarios.given: a token budget violation condition is detected
scenarios.name: newBudgetViolationError
scenarios.then: a new error with the specified message SHALL be created
scenarios.when: errors.New is called with a descriptive message

operations.description: The tokenBudgetValidation operation SHALL calculate the requested token budget from request parameters.
operations.name: calculateTokenBudget

and: considers max_tokens, messages, and content length
may estimate when exact count unavailable
scenarios.given: a request with token-related parameters
scenarios.name: tokenBudgetCalculation
scenarios.then: the estimated or exact token budget SHALL be returned

operations.description: The tokenBudgetValidation operation SHALL format structured error responses for budget violations.
operations.name: formatStructuredError

and: includes error code, message, and retry guidance
scenarios.given: a budget validation failure
scenarios.name: structuredBudgetError
scenarios.then: a structured error object SHALL be returned for JSON serialization
scenarios.when: Error is called in the error handling chain

subSpecs.purpose: Validates that the current request's token consumption is within the organization's allocated budget, aborting with error if exceeded.

subSpecs.name: multipartBodyConstruction

operations.description: The multipartBodyConstruction operation SHALL assemble form fields and file attachments into a properly encoded multipart MIME body.
operations.name: buildForwardableMultipart

and: the boundary is unique and RFC-compliant
file contents are base64-encoded or binary preserved
scenarios.given: valid form fields and file attachments to forward
scenarios.name: completeMultipartAssembly
scenarios.then: a byte slice containing valid multipart/form-data SHALL be returned
scenarios.when: BuildMultipartBody is called with all components

scenarios.given: successful body construction
scenarios.name: contentTypeGeneration
scenarios.then: the content-type header value with boundary SHALL be returned

and: error indicates encoding or size limit failure
scenarios.given: file attachments exceeding size limits
scenarios.name: oversizedFileHandling
scenarios.then: an error SHALL be returned describing the limit violation

subSpecs.purpose: Constructs a complete multipart/form-data body from discrete form fields and file attachments for upstream proxying.

subSpecs.name: sttExecutorFactoryAccess

operations.description: The sttExecutorFactoryAccess operation SHALL retrieve the speech-to-text executor factory from the handler's dependency container.
operations.name: getSTTFactory

and: factory is non-nil when handler is properly initialized
scenarios.given: a Handler instance with initialized dependencies
scenarios.name: factoryRetrieval
scenarios.then: the STT executor factory SHALL be accessible
scenarios.when: the factory field is accessed during request processing

scenarios.given: a Handler with uninitialized or nil factory
scenarios.name: nilFactoryDetection
scenarios.then: subsequent operations must validate factory before use

subSpecs.purpose: Provides access to the speech-to-text executor factory for creating STT-specific request executors.

subSpecs.name: sttExecutorInstantiation

operations.description: The sttExecutorInstantiation operation SHALL create a new speech-to-text specialized executor with STT-specific configuration and behaviors.
operations.name: createSTTExecutor

and: the STT executor wraps or extends the base executor
STT-specific headers and transformations are applied
scenarios.given: a valid base executor and STT configuration
scenarios.name: successfulSTTCreation
scenarios.then: an STT-specialized executor SHALL be instantiated
scenarios.when: NewSTTExecutor is called with valid parameters

and: error indicates invalid configuration or missing base
scenarios.given: nil base executor or invalid STT configuration
scenarios.name: sttCreationFailure
scenarios.then: an error SHALL be returned describing the failure

subSpecs.purpose: Instantiates a speech-to-text specialized executor that handles audio transcription request formats and upstream protocols.

subSpecs.name: baseExecutorInstantiation

operations.description: The baseExecutorInstantiation operation SHALL create a foundational executor with common proxying capabilities shared by all specialized executors.
operations.name: createBaseExecutor

and: the base executor contains HTTP client, timeouts, and retry policies
specialized executors embed or wrap this base
scenarios.given: valid executor configuration and HTTP client
scenarios.name: successfulBaseCreation
scenarios.then: a base executor with common proxying logic SHALL be instantiated
scenarios.when: NewBaseExecutor is called with valid parameters

scenarios.given: nil HTTP client or invalid timeout configurations
scenarios.name: baseCreationFailure
scenarios.then: an error SHALL be returned describing the configuration issue

subSpecs.purpose: Creates the foundational executor structure containing shared HTTP proxying, retry, and error handling logic.

subSpecs.name: connectionSelection

operations.description: The connectionSelection operation SHALL resolve provider aliases to canonical provider identifiers.
operations.name: resolveProviderAlias

and: aliases map to multiple providers for redundancy
scenarios.given: a provider alias string that may be non-canonical
scenarios.name: aliasResolution
scenarios.then: the canonical provider identifier SHALL be returned
scenarios.when: ResolveAlias is called with a known alias

operations.description: The connectionSelection operation SHALL capture current time for cooldown and exhaustion timestamp checks.
operations.name: captureConnectionCheckTime

and: used to compare against connection cooldown_until timestamps
used to evaluate rate limit window boundaries
scenarios.given: connection selection requires temporal validation
scenarios.name: connectionTimeCapture
scenarios.then: the current system time SHALL be recorded
scenarios.when: getConnection begins cooldown and exhaustion evaluation

operations.description: The connectionSelection operation SHALL determine the number of candidate connections available for selection.
operations.name: countAvailableConnections

scenarios.given: a slice of candidate connections for a provider-model pair
scenarios.name: connectionPoolSizing
scenarios.then: the number of available connections SHALL be determined

operations.description: The connectionSelection operation SHALL select a starting index for round-robin or randomized connection selection.
operations.name: determineSelectionStart

scenarios.given: a non-empty connection pool and selection strategy
scenarios.name: startIndexSelection
scenarios.then: a valid index into the connections slice SHALL be returned
scenarios.when: pickStartIndex is called with pool parameters

operations.description: The connectionSelection operation SHALL attempt to select a viable connection from the pool starting at the given index.
operations.name: attemptConnectionSelection

scenarios.given: a connection pool with potentially viable connections
scenarios.name: viableConnectionPick
scenarios.then: a non-exhausted, non-cooled-down connection supporting the model SHALL be returned if found
scenarios.when: tryPickConnection iterates from the start index

and: iterates through all candidates wrapping around
scenarios.given: a connection pool with no immediately viable connections
scenarios.name: exhaustedPoolIteration
scenarios.then: all connections SHALL be evaluated before returning not found
scenarios.when: tryPickConnection wraps through the entire pool

subSpecs.purpose: Selects an appropriate upstream connection from a pool based on provider, model compatibility, cooldown status, and load balancing strategy.

data.name: MultipartBoundaryParser

operations.description: The parseMultipartBoundary function SHALL extract the boundary parameter from a Content-Type header string for multipart/form-data parsing.
operations.name: ParseMultipartBoundary

and: Boundary may be quoted or unquoted
scenarios.given: A Content-Type header with multipart/form-data and boundary parameter
scenarios.name: Valid boundary extraction
scenarios.then: Returns the boundary string without quotes
scenarios.when: parseMultipartBoundary is called with valid content type

scenarios.given: A Content-Type header without boundary parameter
scenarios.name: Missing boundary
scenarios.then: Returns error indicating missing boundary

scenarios.given: A non-multipart Content-Type header
scenarios.name: Non-multipart content type
scenarios.then: Returns error or empty boundary

data.purpose: Extracts the boundary delimiter from multipart Content-Type headers to enable proper parsing of multipart message bodies.

subSpecs.name: timestampCapture

operations.description: The system SHALL capture the current system time for use in timing measurements and cooldown checks.
operations.name: captureCurrentTime

and: the captured time is stored for later use
scenarios.given: a request requires timing measurement or cooldown validation
scenarios.name: timeCaptureForMeasurement
scenarios.then: the current system time is returned as a time.Time value

subSpecs.purpose: Captures the current system time for timing operations and cooldown validation.

subSpecs.name: headerRetrieval

operations.description: The system SHALL retrieve a specified HTTP header value from the incoming request context.
operations.name: getHeaderValue

and: the header value is returned as a string
scenarios.given: an HTTP request with headers is received
scenarios.name: retrieveExistingHeader
scenarios.then: the value of the specified header is returned
scenarios.when: c.GetHeader is called with a valid header key

scenarios.given: an HTTP request without the specified header
scenarios.name: retrieveMissingHeader
scenarios.then: an empty string is returned
scenarios.when: c.GetHeader is called with a non-existent header key

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves HTTP header values from the incoming request context.

subSpecs.name: errorStringConversion

operations.description: The system SHALL convert an error interface value to its human-readable string representation.
operations.name: convertErrorToString

and: the string can be logged or returned in responses
scenarios.given: an error has occurred during processing
scenarios.name: errorMessageExtraction
scenarios.then: the error's message is returned as a string
scenarios.when: err.Error is invoked on the error value

subSpecs.purpose: Converts error values to human-readable string messages for logging and response generation.

subSpecs.name: formDataRetrieval

operations.description: The system SHALL retrieve a specified form field value from POST request form data.
operations.name: getPostFormValue

and: the form field value is returned as a string
scenarios.given: a POST request with form data is received
scenarios.name: retrieveExistingFormField
scenarios.then: the value of the specified form field is returned
scenarios.when: c.PostForm is called with a valid field key

scenarios.given: a POST request without the specified form field
scenarios.name: retrieveMissingFormField
scenarios.then: an empty string is returned
scenarios.when: c.PostForm is called with a non-existent field key

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves form field values from POST request body data.

subSpecs.name: modelAccessValidation

operations.description: The system SHALL determine if a requested model ID is permitted for use by validating against allowed models list.
operations.name: validateModelPermission

and: the modelIDAllowed function is called for validation
scenarios.given: a request specifies a model ID and allowed models are configured
scenarios.name: allowedModelAccess
scenarios.then: true is returned indicating the model is permitted
scenarios.when: isModelAllowed is called with a model ID present in the allowed list

and: the allowedModelsFromContext function provides the allowed models list
scenarios.given: a request specifies a model ID not in the allowed list
scenarios.name: disallowedModelAccess
scenarios.then: false is returned indicating the model is not permitted
scenarios.when: isModelAllowed is called with a model ID absent from the allowed list

subSpecs.purpose: Validates whether a requested model ID is permitted for use in the current request context.

subSpecs.name: modelIdentifierSplitting

operations.description: The system SHALL decompose a composite model identifier into its constituent provider and model name components.
operations.name: splitModelID

and: the provider prefix and model name are separated
scenarios.given: a composite model identifier in format 'provider/model-name'
scenarios.name: validModelSplit
scenarios.then: the provider and model name are returned as separate values
scenarios.when: executor.SplitModel is called with a valid composite identifier

and: appropriate error handling occurs for malformed input
scenarios.given: a model identifier without provider prefix
scenarios.name: invalidModelSplit
scenarios.then: an error is returned indicating invalid format
scenarios.when: executor.SplitModel is called with a malformed identifier

subSpecs.purpose: Decomposes composite model identifiers into provider and model name components.

subSpecs.name: fileResourceCleanup

operations.description: The system SHALL release file descriptor resources by closing an open file handle.
operations.name: closeFileHandle

scenarios.given: a file has been opened for reading or writing
scenarios.name: successfulFileClose
scenarios.then: the file descriptor is closed and nil error is returned
scenarios.when: file.Close is called on a valid file handle

and: any buffered data is flushed before closing
scenarios.given: a file with pending write operations
scenarios.name: flushAndCloseFile
scenarios.then: buffered data is written and the file is closed
scenarios.when: file.Close is called with buffered content

subSpecs.purpose: Releases file descriptor resources by closing open file handles.

subSpecs.name: streamConsumption

operations.description: The system SHALL read all remaining data from an io.Reader until EOF and return the complete byte slice.
operations.name: readAllData

and: the complete content is available in memory
scenarios.given: a readable stream with known content length
scenarios.name: completeStreamRead
scenarios.then: all bytes are read and returned as a single slice
scenarios.when: io.ReadAll is called on the reader

and: memory allocation is proportional to stream size
scenarios.given: an empty or exhausted reader
scenarios.name: emptyStreamRead
scenarios.then: an empty byte slice and nil error are returned
scenarios.when: io.ReadAll is called with no remaining data

subSpecs.purpose: Consumes all remaining data from an io.Reader stream into a byte slice.

subSpecs.name: tokenBudgetVerification

operations.description: The system SHALL verify that the requested token budget does not exceed configured limits and abort the request if exceeded.
operations.name: validateTokenBudget

and: c.GetString retrieves the configured budget limit
scenarios.given: a request with token budget within configured limits
scenarios.name: budgetWithinLimit
scenarios.then: nil error is returned allowing request to proceed
scenarios.when: checkTokenBudget is called with acceptable budget

and: and requestedTokenBudget calculates the actual budget
scenarios.given: a request with token budget exceeding configured limits
scenarios.name: budgetExceeded
scenarios.then: the request is aborted with status JSON error and error is returned
scenarios.when: checkTokenBudget is called with excessive budget

subSpecs.purpose: Validates requested token budgets against configured limits and aborts requests that exceed them.

subSpecs.name: multipartBodyConstruction

operations.description: The system SHALL construct a multipart/form-data request body from provided form fields and file data.
operations.name: buildMultipartPayload

and: the body includes proper boundary delimiters
scenarios.given: valid form fields and file attachments
scenarios.name: successfulMultipartBuild
scenarios.then: a valid multipart body with correct Content-Type is returned
scenarios.when: executor.BuildMultipartBody is called with complete data

and: file content is properly encoded in the body
scenarios.given: multiple files and form fields
scenarios.name: complexMultipartBuild
scenarios.then: all parts are included with correct headers and boundaries
scenarios.when: executor.BuildMultipartBody is called with mixed content

subSpecs.purpose: Constructs multipart/form-data request bodies with proper boundary delimiters and encoding.

subSpecs.name: sttExecutorFactoryAccess

operations.description: The system SHALL access the STT executor factory from the handler to create speech-to-text execution instances.
operations.name: accessFactory

and: the factory is used for subsequent executor creation
scenarios.given: a handler with configured STT executor factory
scenarios.name: factoryRetrieval
scenarios.then: the STT executor factory is returned

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves the STT executor factory from the handler for creating speech-to-text executors.

subSpecs.name: sttExecutorInstantiation

operations.description: The system SHALL create a new speech-to-text executor configured for the specified model and parameters.
operations.name: createSTTExecutor

and: the executor is ready for audio transcription operations
scenarios.given: valid model ID and configuration parameters
scenarios.name: successfulSTTExecutorCreation
scenarios.then: a configured STT executor is returned

and: the executor implements the STT-specific interface
scenarios.given: a supported speech-to-text model
scenarios.name: supportedModelExecutor
scenarios.then: an executor capable of audio processing is returned
scenarios.when: executor.NewSTTExecutor is called with supported model

subSpecs.purpose: Instantiates speech-to-text executors configured for specific models and parameters.

subSpecs.name: baseExecutorInstantiation

operations.description: The system SHALL create a new base executor with common functionality shared by all executor types.
operations.name: createBaseExecutor

and: the base executor provides common HTTP client and retry logic
scenarios.given: valid executor configuration
scenarios.name: successfulBaseExecutorCreation
scenarios.then: a base executor with common capabilities is returned

and: specialized executors embed this base functionality
scenarios.given: any executor type requiring HTTP operations
scenarios.name: baseForSpecializedExecutors
scenarios.then: the base executor provides shared HTTP handling

subSpecs.purpose: Instantiates base executors providing common HTTP handling and retry functionality for all executor types.

subSpecs.name: connectionSelection

operations.description: The system SHALL select an available connection for the specified provider and model, respecting cooldown and exhaustion constraints.
operations.name: selectConnection

scenarios.given: multiple available connections with no active cooldowns
scenarios.name: successfulConnectionSelection
scenarios.then: a valid connection is returned
scenarios.when: getConnection is called with active provider and model

scenarios.given: all connections in cooldown or exhausted
scenarios.name: connectionExhaustion
scenarios.then: an error indicating no available connections is returned
scenarios.when: getConnection is called with constrained resources

subSpecs.purpose: Selects an available API connection for the specified provider and model while respecting cooldown and exhaustion constraints.

subSpecs.name: tokenProactiveRefresh

operations.description: The system SHALL determine whether a connection's token requires proactive refresh based on provider-specific lead time thresholds.
operations.name: evaluateTokenRefresh

and: the decision matches OmniRoute checkAndRefreshToken behavior
scenarios.given: a connection with token nearing expiration per provider lead time
scenarios.name: refreshRequired
scenarios.then: true is returned indicating proactive refresh should occur

scenarios.given: a connection with token well within validity period
scenarios.name: refreshNotRequired
scenarios.then: false is returned indicating no refresh needed

subSpecs.purpose: Evaluates whether API tokens require proactive refresh based on provider-specific expiration lead times.

subSpecs.name: jsonDeserialization

operations.description: The system SHALL parse JSON-encoded data into the specified Go data structure.
operations.name: parseJSON

and: the target struct fields are populated from JSON properties
scenarios.given: valid JSON data matching the target structure
scenarios.name: successfulJSONParse
scenarios.then: nil error is returned and target struct contains parsed data
scenarios.when: json.Unmarshal is called with valid JSON

and: appropriate error is returned for malformed input
scenarios.given: JSON data with type mismatches or syntax errors
scenarios.name: invalidJSONParse
scenarios.then: a non-nil error describing the parse failure is returned
scenarios.when: json.Unmarshal is called with invalid JSON

subSpecs.purpose: Deserializes JSON-encoded data into Go data structures.

subSpecs.name: identifierShortening

operations.description: The system SHALL generate a shortened unique identifier from a full-length identifier for compact logging and display.
operations.name: generateShortID

and: the shortened ID maintains uniqueness within practical limits
scenarios.given: a standard UUID or long identifier
scenarios.name: standardIDShortening
scenarios.then: a shortened version suitable for display is returned
scenarios.when: shortID is called with valid identifier

and: the output length is consistent regardless of input length
scenarios.given: identifiers of varying lengths
scenarios.name: consistentShortLength
scenarios.then: a fixed-length short identifier is returned
scenarios.when: shortID is called with different input lengths

subSpecs.purpose: Generates compact, display-friendly identifiers from full-length unique identifiers.

subSpecs.name: proxyContextCreation

operations.description: The system SHALL create a context enriched with proxy-specific metadata for request routing and tracing.
operations.name: createProxyContext

and: the context carries proxy pool and routing information
scenarios.given: a base context and proxy configuration parameters
scenarios.name: contextWithProxyMetadata
scenarios.then: a context with embedded proxy metadata is returned

and: downstream operations can retrieve proxy settings from context
scenarios.given: a request requiring proxy routing
scenarios.name: retrievableProxySettings
scenarios.then: proxy configuration is accessible via context values
scenarios.when: proxyContext is used in request handling

subSpecs.purpose: Creates request contexts enriched with proxy routing and pool metadata.

subSpecs.name: retryableExecution

operations.description: The system SHALL execute an operation with configurable retry logic on transient failures.
operations.name: executeWithRetries

and: the retry policy specifies maximum attempts and backoff strategy
scenarios.given: an operation that fails transiently then succeeds
scenarios.name: eventualSuccessWithRetry
scenarios.then: the operation result is returned after one or more retries
scenarios.when: executeWithRetry is called with lenient retry policy

and: non-transient failures fail fast without retry
scenarios.given: an operation with permanent failure
scenarios.name: permanentFailureNoRetry
scenarios.then: the error is returned immediately without retry attempts
scenarios.when: executeWithRetry is called with non-retryable error

subSpecs.purpose: Executes operations with automatic retry on transient failures according to configured policies.

subSpecs.name: upstreamErrorReporting

operations.description: The system SHALL write an appropriate error response when the upstream API client encounters an error.
operations.name: reportUpstreamError

and: the response status reflects the upstream error nature
scenarios.given: an upstream API returns a client error (4xx)
scenarios.name: upstreamClientError
scenarios.then: the corresponding HTTP error status is written to the response
scenarios.when: writeUpstreamClientError is called with client error

and: error details are sanitized before exposure
scenarios.given: an upstream API error with sensitive details
scenarios.name: sanitizedErrorResponse
scenarios.then: a safe error message is written without sensitive information
scenarios.when: writeUpstreamClientError is called with sensitive error

subSpecs.purpose: Writes sanitized error responses for upstream API client errors to the downstream client.

subSpecs.name: requestLogging

operations.description: The system SHALL capture and persist request metadata for usage tracking and auditing.
operations.name: recordRequest

and: the log entry contains request timing and routing metadata
scenarios.given: a completed request with associated usage data
scenarios.name: successfulRequestLog
scenarios.then: the log entry is persisted to the usage logging system
scenarios.when: logRequest is called with valid context and entry

and: logging failures do not affect request response
scenarios.given: a request where logging system is unavailable
scenarios.name: resilientRequestLogging
scenarios.then: the log attempt fails silently and response proceeds normally

subSpecs.purpose: Captures and persists request metadata for usage tracking, auditing, and analytics.

subSpecs.name: contextStringRetrieval

operations.description: The system SHALL retrieve a string value stored in the Gin context by key.
operations.name: getContextString

and: the value was previously set in context
scenarios.given: a Gin context with stored string values
scenarios.name: existingContextString
scenarios.then: the stored string value is returned
scenarios.when: c.GetString is called with a valid key

scenarios.given: a Gin context without the requested key
scenarios.name: missingContextString
scenarios.then: an empty string is returned
scenarios.when: c.GetString is called with non-existent key

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves string values from the Gin request context by key.

subSpecs.name: proxyPoolIdentification

operations.description: The system SHALL extract the proxy pool identifier from the request context for routing decisions.
operations.name: extractPoolID

and: the pool ID was set during context creation
scenarios.given: a request context with embedded proxy metadata
scenarios.name: existingPoolID
scenarios.then: the proxy pool identifier is returned

and: empty string for contexts without proxy metadata
scenarios.given: a request context without proxy configuration
scenarios.name: missingPoolID
scenarios.then: an empty string is returned

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts proxy pool identifiers from request contexts for routing decisions.

data.name: TTSHandler

operations.description: SHALL parse and validate the incoming TTS request body from the HTTP context, enforcing maximum size limits and returning structured errors for malformed or oversized payloads.
operations.name: readBody

scenarios.given: A POST request to /v1/audio/speech with payload exceeding max bytes limit
scenarios.name: Request body exceeds maximum size
scenarios.then: Request SHALL be rejected with payload too large error

scenarios.given: A POST request with malformed or unreadable body
scenarios.name: Request body read failure
scenarios.then: Request SHALL be rejected with bad request error

operations.description: SHALL determine whether the requested model identifier is permitted for the authenticated API key based on context-derived allowed models configuration.
operations.name: isModelAllowed

scenarios.given: API key context allows all models or explicitly includes requested model
scenarios.name: Model explicitly permitted
scenarios.then: isModelAllowed SHALL return true
scenarios.when: isModelAllowed is called with allowed model

and: Request rejected with 403 or equivalent error
scenarios.given: API key context restricts models and requested model not in allowlist
scenarios.name: Model not permitted
scenarios.then: isModelAllowed SHALL return false
scenarios.when: isModelAllowed is called with disallowed model

operations.description: SHALL enforce token budget constraints for the requesting API key by inspecting request payload dimensions and comparing against configured quota limits, aborting request if exceeded.
operations.name: checkTokenBudget

scenarios.given: API key has sufficient remaining token budget for requested input
scenarios.name: Token budget sufficient
scenarios.then: checkTokenBudget SHALL return nil

and: HTTP 429 returned with quota exceeded message
scenarios.given: API key has exhausted or insufficient token budget
scenarios.name: Token budget exceeded
scenarios.then: checkTokenBudget SHALL abort request with quota exceeded error

operations.description: SHALL select an appropriate upstream provider connection for the TTS request using alias resolution, load balancing strategy, and fallback mechanisms with proactive token refresh.
operations.name: getConnection

scenarios.given: Multiple healthy connections available for provider-model combination
scenarios.name: Successful connection selection
scenarios.then: getConnection SHALL return active connection using round-robin or weighted selection

and: Fallback connection returned with warning logged
scenarios.given: Primary connections unhealthy but fallback configured
scenarios.name: Connection fallback activated
scenarios.then: getConnection SHALL return fallback connection

scenarios.given: No healthy connections available for provider-model
scenarios.name: No available connection
scenarios.then: getConnection SHALL return error indicating service unavailable

operations.description: SHALL execute the text-to-speech request through the selected upstream connection with retry logic for transient failures, streaming the audio response back to the client.
operations.name: TTS
outputs: HTTP response with audio/mpeg or error

scenarios.given: Valid TTS request with allowed model and available connection
scenarios.name: Successful TTS generation
scenarios.then: TTS SHALL stream audio content with 200 OK and record usage

and: Error response with appropriate HTTP status
scenarios.given: Valid request but upstream returns transient error
scenarios.name: Upstream retry with eventual success
scenarios.then: TTS SHALL retry execution and return success on recovery

scenarios.given: Request with disallowed model
scenarios.name: Model validation failure
scenarios.then: TTS SHALL reject before connection selection

scenarios.given: Request exceeding token budget
scenarios.name: Quota enforcement
scenarios.then: TTS SHALL reject with quota exceeded

scenarios.given: All connections fail or upstream unavailable
scenarios.name: Upstream execution failure
scenarios.then: TTS SHALL return appropriate proxy error

data.purpose: Handles HTTP requests for text-to-speech synthesis, orchestrating request validation, model authorization, quota enforcement, connection selection, upstream execution with retry, and usage accounting.
sideEffects: Reads HTTP request body via MaxBytesReader
Writes HTTP response with audio content or error
Accumulates API key usage metrics
Logs request details for observability
Proactively refreshes upstream authentication tokens
Sets response headers including X-Request-ID and content-type

subSpecs.name: timestampCapture

operations.description: The system SHALL capture the current UTC timestamp at the start of request processing.
operations.name: captureRequestStart

and: The returned timestamp is recorded in request context
scenarios.given: A new TTS request has been received
scenarios.name: requestStartTimestamp
scenarios.then: The timestamp is non-zero and represents current wall-clock time
scenarios.when: time.Now is invoked at handler entry

subSpecs.purpose: Captures the current time for request duration measurement and logging.

subSpecs.name: requestBodyReading

operations.description: The system SHALL read the HTTP request body up to a configured maximum size limit.
operations.name: readBodyWithinLimit

scenarios.given: A POST request with Content-Length within configured limit
scenarios.name: successfulBodyRead
scenarios.then: The complete body bytes are returned with nil error
scenarios.when: readBody is called with the request context

operations.description: The system SHALL return errBodyTooLarge when the request body exceeds the configured maximum size.
operations.name: rejectOversizedBody

scenarios.given: A POST request with body exceeding maxBytes configuration
scenarios.name: bodyTooLargeError
scenarios.then: errBodyTooLarge is returned and body bytes are nil
scenarios.when: readBody attempts to read the oversized body

operations.description: The system SHALL return errReadBody for any non-size-limit read failure.
operations.name: handleReadFailure

and: strings.Contains checks error message for size-related indicators
scenarios.given: A request where the body stream is unreadable due to network error
scenarios.name: otherReadError
scenarios.then: errReadBody is returned wrapping the original error
scenarios.when: readBody encounters a non-EOF read failure

subSpecs.purpose: Reads and validates the HTTP request body against size limits, returning appropriate errors for violations or read failures.

subSpecs.name: bodyErrorResponse

operations.description: The system SHALL write an appropriate HTTP error response based on the body read error type.
operations.name: mapBodyErrorToResponse

scenarios.given: readBody returned an error
scenarios.name: bodyErrorResponseMapping
scenarios.then: HTTP 413 is returned for errBodyTooLarge; HTTP 400 is returned for errReadBody with appropriate error message
scenarios.when: writeReadBodyError is called with the error

subSpecs.purpose: Translates body read errors into proper HTTP error responses with correct status codes.

subSpecs.name: jsonFieldExtraction

operations.description: The system SHALL extract a string value from a JSON object by key path.
operations.name: extractStringField

and: Nested keys are accessed via dot notation
scenarios.given: A parsed JSON body containing {\"model\": \"tts-1\"}
scenarios.name: extractModelField
scenarios.then: The string \"tts-1\" is returned with nil error

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts string values from parsed JSON request bodies using dot-notation key paths.

subSpecs.name: modelAuthorization

operations.description: The system SHALL determine if the requested model ID is in the caller's allowed models list.
operations.name: checkModelPermission

scenarios.given: Request context contains allowed models [\"tts-1\", \"tts-1-hd\"] and modelID is \"tts-1\"
scenarios.name: allowedModel
scenarios.then: true is returned

and: Empty allowed models list denies all requests
scenarios.given: Request context has no allowed models configured
scenarios.name: noAllowedModels
scenarios.then: false is returned for any modelID
scenarios.when: isModelAllowed is called with any model

subSpecs.purpose: Validates that the requested TTS model is authorized for the current caller.

subSpecs.name: quotaEnforcement

operations.description: The system SHALL verify the caller has sufficient token budget for the requested synthesis.
operations.name: validateSufficientBudget

and: c.GetString retrieves API key from context
requestedTokenBudget calculates required tokens from input text
scenarios.given: API key \"ak_123\" has 1000 remaining tokens and request needs 500
scenarios.name: sufficientBudget
scenarios.then: nil error is returned and request proceeds

and: c.AbortWithStatusJSON returns HTTP 429 with budget error details
scenarios.given: API key \"ak_123\" has 100 remaining tokens and request needs 500
scenarios.name: insufficientBudget
scenarios.then: HTTP 429 is written to response and non-nil error is returned

subSpecs.purpose: Enforces per-API-key token quota limits before allowing synthesis execution.

subSpecs.name: modelParsing

operations.description: The system SHALL parse a model identifier into provider and model components.
operations.name: splitModelIdentifier

and: Format is \"provider/model\" or \"provider:model\" depending on convention
scenarios.given: modelID is \"openai/tts-1\"
scenarios.name: standardModelSplit
scenarios.then: provider=\"openai\", model=\"tts-1\" are returned

subSpecs.purpose: Decomposes composite model identifiers into provider and model name components.

subSpecs.name: executorFactory

operations.description: The system SHALL create a TTS executor instance using the handler's configured factory method.
operations.name: createTTSExecutor

and: Factory may return cached or pooled executors
scenarios.given: Handler is initialized with valid TTS executor factory
scenarios.name: factoryCreatesExecutor
scenarios.then: A non-nil TTSExecutor is returned with nil error
scenarios.when: ttsExecutorFactory is called with valid configuration

subSpecs.purpose: Instantiates provider-specific TTS executors through the handler's factory dependency.

subSpecs.name: ttsExecutorConstruction

operations.description: The system SHALL construct a new TTS executor with the provided base executor and configuration.
operations.name: buildTTSExecutor

and: Base executor provides common HTTP client and retry logic
TTSConfig contains voice and format settings
scenarios.given: Valid base executor and TTS configuration
scenarios.name: constructExecutor
scenarios.then: A configured TTSExecutor is returned

subSpecs.purpose: Creates a new TTS executor wrapping a base executor with TTS-specific configuration.

subSpecs.name: baseExecutorConstruction

operations.description: The system SHALL construct a base executor with HTTP client, retry policy, and logging.
operations.name: buildBaseExecutor

and: HTTP timeout and retry configuration are applied
Logger is attached for request tracing
scenarios.given: Valid base configuration with HTTP client settings
scenarios.name: constructBaseExecutor
scenarios.then: A BaseExecutor with configured transport and retry behavior is returned

subSpecs.purpose: Creates the foundational executor with HTTP transport, retry logic, and observability hooks.

subSpecs.name: connectionSelection

operations.description: The system SHALL select an available upstream connection for the specified provider and model.
operations.name: selectAvailableConnection

scenarios.given: Provider \"openai\" has 3 connections with 2 available
scenarios.name: successfulConnectionSelection
scenarios.then: An available Connection with valid token is returned

scenarios.given: All primary connections are in cooldown or exhausted
scenarios.name: fallbackConnectionSelection
scenarios.then: A fallback Connection is returned or error if none available

operations.description: The system SHALL reject connections that fail model-level cooldown or exhaustion checks.
operations.name: enforceCooldownExhaustion

and: Token validity window is checked against current time
scenarios.given: Connection was rate-limited 30 seconds ago with 60-second cooldown
scenarios.name: connectionInCooldown
scenarios.then: The connection is skipped in selection; error if no alternatives

subSpecs.purpose: Selects an upstream connection for request proxying, respecting cooldowns, exhaustion, and fallback strategies.

subSpecs.name: tokenRefresh

operations.description: The system SHALL proactively refresh authentication tokens before expiration based on provider-specific lead times.
operations.name: refreshBeforeExpiry

and: Per-provider lead time configuration determines refresh threshold
scenarios.given: Connection token expires in 5 minutes and provider lead time is 10 minutes
scenarios.name: proactiveRefreshNeeded
scenarios.then: true is returned and token refresh is initiated

and: Refresh is skipped if token lifetime exceeds lead time
scenarios.given: Connection token expires in 30 minutes and provider lead time is 10 minutes
scenarios.name: noRefreshNeeded
scenarios.then: false is returned

subSpecs.purpose: Preemptively refreshes authentication tokens using provider-configured lead times to prevent mid-request expiration.

subSpecs.name: requestDeserialization

operations.description: The system SHALL parse JSON request bodies into the expected TTS request structure.
operations.name: parseTTSRequest

and: Target struct contains Model, Input, Voice fields
scenarios.given: Valid JSON body: {\"model\":\"tts-1\",\"input\":\"hello\",\"voice\":\"alloy\"}
scenarios.name: validRequestParse
scenarios.then: Struct is populated and nil error returned

scenarios.given: Invalid JSON body: {\"model\":\"tts-1\",\"input\":}
scenarios.name: invalidJSON
scenarios.then: Non-nil error is returned

subSpecs.purpose: Deserializes JSON request payloads into typed request structures for validation and processing.

subSpecs.name: requestIdentification

operations.description: The system SHALL generate a short unique identifier for each request for tracing and logging.
operations.name: generateTraceID

and: ID is used in log entries and upstream request headers
scenarios.given: A new TTS request is being processed
scenarios.name: traceIdGeneration
scenarios.then: A non-empty string of fixed length is returned
scenarios.when: shortID is called at request start

subSpecs.purpose: Generates concise unique identifiers for request correlation and distributed tracing.

subSpecs.name: errorStringification

operations.description: The system SHALL convert errors to human-readable strings for logging and HTTP responses.
operations.name: formatErrorMessage

and: Called on various error types throughout the handler
scenarios.given: An error with message \"connection refused\" exists
scenarios.name: errorToString
scenarios.then: The string \"connection refused\" is returned

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts human-readable messages from error values for logging and client responses.

subSpecs.name: contextPreparation

operations.description: The system SHALL create a derived context with timeout and values for upstream proxy execution.
operations.name: prepareProxyContext

and: Timeout is derived from handler configuration
Values include request ID and authentication
scenarios.given: Parent context with 30-second remaining deadline
scenarios.name: contextWithTimeout
scenarios.then: Child context has min(parent deadline, configured timeout) and cancel function

subSpecs.purpose: Prepares execution contexts with appropriate timeouts and metadata for upstream requests.

subSpecs.name: retryExecution

operations.description: The system SHALL execute the upstream TTS request with configurable retry logic for transient failures.
operations.name: retryableExecution

and: Retry count and backoff are from executor configuration
scenarios.given: Upstream returns 503 on first attempt, 200 on second
scenarios.name: successfulRetry
scenarios.then: Response is returned after second attempt

scenarios.given: Context cancelled during first retry backoff
scenarios.name: retryAborted
scenarios.then: Context error is returned immediately

subSpecs.purpose: Executes upstream requests with automatic retry for transient failures and context-aware cancellation.

subSpecs.name: upstreamErrorHandling

operations.description: The system SHALL translate upstream client errors into appropriate HTTP responses.
operations.name: mapUpstreamError

and: Status code mapping preserves upstream semantics
Error body may be proxied or sanitized
scenarios.given: Upstream returned 401 with authentication error
scenarios.name: proxyAuthError
scenarios.then: HTTP 401 is written with appropriate error body

subSpecs.purpose: Converts upstream service errors into properly formatted HTTP responses for the client.

subSpecs.name: requestLogging

operations.description: The system SHALL record request metadata and outcome to the usage logging system.
operations.name: persistRequestLog

and: Log entry includes timing, model, tokens consumed, and success/failure status
scenarios.given: A TTS request has completed processing
scenarios.name: successfulLogWrite
scenarios.then: Log entry is persisted to configured logging backend
scenarios.when: logRequest is called with completed request data

subSpecs.purpose: Records request telemetry for billing, monitoring, and debugging purposes.

data.name: UnifiedHandler

operations.description: The Unified handler SHALL accept a POST request to /v1/unified and dispatch it to the appropriate downstream endpoint based on the mode field in the request body.
operations.name: UnifiedRequestDispatch

scenarios.given: A valid POST request to /v1/unified with mode='chat.completions'
scenarios.name: Chat completions mode dispatch
scenarios.then: Request is routed to ChatCompletions handler

scenarios.given: A valid POST request to /v1/unified with mode='images'
scenarios.name: Images mode dispatch
scenarios.then: Request is routed to Images handler

scenarios.given: A valid POST request to /v1/unified with mode='video'
scenarios.name: Video mode dispatch
scenarios.then: Request is routed to Video handler

and: HTTP 400 error is returned with descriptive message
scenarios.given: A POST request to /v1/unified with missing or unsupported mode
scenarios.name: Invalid mode rejection
scenarios.then: Request is rejected with an error

data.purpose: Acts as the single entry point for all API requests, routing unified requests to specialized handlers (chat completions, images, video) based on the request mode. Encapsulates request body reading, token budget validation, and model allowlist checks before dispatch.
sideEffects: Reads HTTP request body from network
Writes HTTP response to client
May invoke downstream handlers that perform network calls to AI providers

subSpecs.name: readBody

operations.description: SHALL limit the request body size to a predefined maximum and return errBodyTooLarge when the limit is exceeded.
operations.name: enforceSizeLimit

scenarios.given: an HTTP request with a body exceeding the configured maximum size
scenarios.name: bodyExceedsMaxSize
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return an error indicating the body is too large without reading the full content
scenarios.when: readBody is called with the gin context

and: the returned byte slice is nil
scenarios.given: an HTTP request with a body within the size limit
scenarios.name: bodyWithinSizeLimit
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return the complete body bytes and a nil error
scenarios.when: readBody is called with the gin context

operations.description: SHALL return errReadBody for any failure during body reading that is not a size-limit violation.
operations.name: handleReadFailures

scenarios.given: an HTTP request where the underlying reader returns a non-EOF error
scenarios.name: otherReadError
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return an error indicating body read failure
scenarios.when: readBody is called with the gin context

subSpecs.purpose: Reads and returns the HTTP request body with size limiting, distinguishing between size violations and other read failures.

subSpecs.name: writeReadBodyError

operations.description: SHALL write an appropriate HTTP error response based on the type of body read error encountered.
operations.name: errorResponseMapping

scenarios.given: a gin context and a body read error that matches errReadBody
scenarios.name: errReadBodyResponse
scenarios.then: the function SHALL write an HTTP error response with the errReadBody message

and: the response status indicates request entity too large
scenarios.given: a gin context and an error matching errBodyTooLarge
scenarios.name: errBodyTooLargeResponse
scenarios.then: the function SHALL write an HTTP 413 or appropriate size-limit error response

subSpecs.purpose: Translates body read errors into appropriate HTTP error responses sent to the client.

subSpecs.name: checkTokenBudget

operations.description: SHALL validate that the requested token budget does not exceed available limits and abort the request with an error if exceeded.
operations.name: budgetValidation

and: c.AbortWithStatusJSON is called with an error status
scenarios.given: a request context with a token budget exceeding the available limit
scenarios.name: budgetExceeded
scenarios.then: the function SHALL abort the request and return an error indicating insufficient token budget

scenarios.given: a request context with a token budget within available limits
scenarios.name: budgetWithinLimit
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return nil without aborting the request

subSpecs.purpose: Validates token budget constraints from request context and aborts with error if limits are exceeded.

subSpecs.name: executorJSONGet

operations.description: SHALL extract a value from a JSON document at the specified path and return it as the requested type.
operations.name: jsonPathExtraction

scenarios.given: a valid JSON document and an existing path
scenarios.name: pathExists
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return the value at the specified path

and: the returned error indicates path not found
scenarios.given: a valid JSON document and a non-existent path
scenarios.name: pathMissing
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return an error indicating the path was not found

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts typed values from JSON documents using path expressions.

subSpecs.name: isModelAllowed

operations.description: SHALL determine whether a given model identifier is present in the allowlist derived from context.
operations.name: modelAllowlistCheck

scenarios.given: a model ID that exists in the allowed models from context
scenarios.name: allowedModel
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return true

scenarios.given: a model ID not present in the allowed models from context
scenarios.name: disallowedModel

data.name: VideoHandler

operations.description: Reads and validates the HTTP request body for video generation requests, enforcing maximum size limits and parsing JSON content.

scenarios.given: A valid HTTP POST request with JSON body under size limit
scenarios.name: Successful body read
scenarios.then: Request body is returned as byte slice without error
scenarios.when: readBody is called with gin context and maxBytes limit

and: Error response is written to client
scenarios.given: An HTTP request with body exceeding maxBytes limit
scenarios.name: Body exceeds maximum size
scenarios.then: http.MaxBytesReader error is returned and client receives 413 Payload Too Large

and: Error response is written to client
scenarios.given: An HTTP request with malformed or unreadable body
scenarios.name: Body read failure
scenarios.then: IO error is returned and client receives appropriate error response

operations.description: Writes a standardized error response when request body reading fails, mapping specific errors to appropriate HTTP status codes and error messages.
operations.name: writeReadBodyError

scenarios.given: Request body exceeds maximum allowed size
scenarios.name: Max bytes exceeded error
scenarios.then: HTTP 413 status is written with 'Request body too large' error message

scenarios.given: Any other body read failure occurs
scenarios.name: Generic read body error
scenarios.then: HTTP 400 status is written with 'Failed to read request body' error message

operations.description: Validates whether the requested model ID is permitted for use by the current API key based on context-derived allowed models configuration.
operations.name: isModelAllowed

scenarios.given: No allowed models restriction is configured for API key
scenarios.name: No model restrictions configured
scenarios.then: Model is permitted and true is returned
scenarios.when: isModelAllowed is called with empty allowed models context

scenarios.given: Requested model ID matches wildcard pattern in allowed models
scenarios.name: Wildcard model match
scenarios.then: Model is permitted and true is returned
scenarios.when: isModelAllowed is called with model matching wildcard pattern

scenarios.given: Requested model ID exactly matches entry in allowed models list
scenarios.name: Exact model match
scenarios.then: Model is permitted and true is returned
scenarios.when: isModelAllowed is called with model in allowed list

scenarios.given: Requested model ID is not in allowed models list and does not match wildcard
scenarios.name: Model not allowed
scenarios.then: Model is denied, false is returned, and client receives 403 with model not allowed error
scenarios.when: isModelAllowed is called with disallowed model

operations.description: Validates that the API key has sufficient token budget for the requested operation by checking current usage against quota limits.
operations.name: checkTokenBudget

and: Request processing continues to connection selection
scenarios.given: API key has remaining token budget sufficient for request
scenarios.name: Sufficient token budget
scenarios.then: No error is returned and request proceeds
scenarios.when: checkTokenBudget is called with valid budget context

and: Request is aborted with 429 Too Many Requests
scenarios.given: API key has exceeded its token quota
scenarios.name: Token budget exhausted
scenarios.then: Error is returned, request is aborted with 429 status and budget exceeded message
scenarios.when: checkTokenBudget is called with exhausted quota

and: Request is aborted with 429 Too Many Requests
scenarios.given: Requested tokens exceed remaining budget
scenarios.name: Requested tokens exceed budget
scenarios.then: Error is returned, request is aborted with 429 status and insufficient budget message
scenarios.when: checkTokenBudget is called with oversized request

operations.description: Selects an appropriate upstream connection for video generation requests using provider alias resolution, load balancing, and fallback mechanisms.
operations.name: getConnection

and: Connection metadata is attached to context
scenarios.given: Multiple healthy connections available for provider-model combination
scenarios.name: Successful connection selection
scenarios.then: Optimal connection is selected using round-robin and returned
scenarios.when: getConnection is called with valid provider and model

scenarios.given: No healthy connections in primary pool
scenarios.name: Primary pool exhausted
scenarios.then: Fallback connection pool is consulted and connection returned if available
scenarios.when: getConnection is called with exhausted primary pool

scenarios.given: No connections available in primary or fallback pools
scenarios.name: All connections unavailable
scenarios.then: Error is returned indicating no available connections
scenarios.when: getConnection is called with all pools exhausted

and: Provider alias is resolved before selection
scenarios.given: Provider alias is configured
scenarios.name: Provider alias resolution
scenarios.then: Alias is resolved to actual provider before connection selection
scenarios.when: getConnection is called with aliased provider

operations.description: Orchestrates the complete video generation request lifecycle including validation, connection selection, execution, and response handling.
operations.name: Video

and: Request is logged with timing metrics
scenarios.given: Valid video generation request with allowed model and sufficient budget
scenarios.name: Successful video generation
scenarios.then: Request is proxied to upstream, response is streamed, and success metrics recorded

scenarios.given: Request body exceeds maximum size limit
scenarios.name: Oversized request body
scenarios.then: 413 error is returned without upstream call

scenarios.given: Requested model is not in API key allowed models
scenarios.name: Disallowed model request
scenarios.then: 403 error is returned with model not allowed message
scenarios.when: Video handler receives request for unauthorized model

scenarios.given: API key token budget is exhausted
scenarios.name: Budget exceeded
scenarios.then: 429 error is returned with budget exceeded message
scenarios.when: Video handler receives request from over-quota key

and: Errors are mapped to appropriate status codes
scenarios.given: Upstream connection fails during execution
scenarios.name: Upstream execution failure
scenarios.then: Retry is attempted with fallback connection, or error is propagated to client

scenarios.given: Connection requires authentication refresh
scenarios.name: Proactive token refresh
scenarios.then: Token is refreshed before execution to prevent mid-request failures
scenarios.when: Video handler processes request with stale token

data.purpose: Handles HTTP requests for AI video generation endpoints, managing request validation, model authorization, token budget enforcement, connection selection, and proxy execution with retry logic and usage tracking.
sideEffects: Reads HTTP request body from network
Writes HTTP response to client
Queries connection pool for available upstream connections
Calls upstream AI provider API via proxy
Writes usage metrics to billing/usage tracking system
Logs request details and timing metrics
May trigger proactive token refresh via external identity provider

subSpecs.name: requestBodyReading

scenarios.given: A POST request with body of 2MB and max body size of 1MB

outputs: HTTP response with deployment result or error

and: Edge function source is built and uploaded
Deployed URL is tested and responds healthy

and: Error details are parsed from response body

scenarios.then: HTTP error response is returned with parsed Vercel error message

scenarios.given: Vercel deployment hangs or takes longer than timeout

scenarios.then: True returned

scenarios.name: Missing file error
scenarios.then: True returned

and: Error does not match any client error pattern
scenarios.given: Restore error with server failure pattern
scenarios.name: Server error
scenarios.then: False returned

scenarios.given: No error occurred
scenarios.name: Nil error
scenarios.then: False returned

data.purpose: Handles HTTP requests for database backup download and restore operations, including password-protected backups, multipart file uploads, and post-restore restart coordination.
sideEffects: Streams backup data to HTTP response writer
Reads multipart form file from HTTP request
Writes to database via backup.Restore
Invokes asynchronous restart callback after successful restore
Interacts with appdb.WriteQueue for database writes during restore

subSpecs.name: formFileExtraction

operations.description: The FormFile extraction SHALL retrieve the uploaded file from the multipart form data using the specified form field name.
operations.name: extractMultipartFile

and: the form field 'backup' contains a valid .db file
scenarios.given: an HTTP POST request with multipart/form-data content-type
scenarios.name: validBackupFileUpload
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return the FileHeader without error
scenarios.when: FormFile is called with field name 'backup'

and: the request body contains no multipart data
scenarios.given: an HTTP POST request without multipart form data
scenarios.name: missingMultipartData
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return an error indicating missing file
scenarios.when: FormFile is called with field name 'backup'

operations.description: The FormFile extraction SHALL validate that the uploaded file has an acceptable extension for database backups.
operations.name: validateFileExtension

scenarios.given: a multipart form upload with filename 'backup.db'
scenarios.name: validDbExtension
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return the FileHeader without error

scenarios.given: a multipart form upload with filename 'backup.zip'
scenarios.name: invalidZipExtension
scenarios.then: the function SHOULD return an error or pass to downstream validation

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts and validates the uploaded backup file from multipart form data in HTTP requests.

subSpecs.name: fileOpening

operations.description: The file opening operation SHALL open the uploaded file for reading to enable content processing.
operations.name: openUploadedFile

and: the file exists in temporary storage
scenarios.given: a valid FileHeader from a completed upload
scenarios.name: successfulFileOpen
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a readable File interface
scenarios.when: Open is called on the FileHeader

scenarios.given: a FileHeader referencing a deleted temporary file
scenarios.name: missingTempFile
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return an error indicating file not found
scenarios.when: Open is called on the FileHeader

subSpecs.purpose: Opens the uploaded backup file to provide a readable interface for subsequent processing.

subSpecs.name: errorStringExtraction

operations.description: The error string extraction SHALL convert an error value to its string representation for analysis and logging.
operations.name: extractErrorMessage

scenarios.given: a sqlite3.ErrBusy error
scenarios.name: databaseLockedError
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a string containing 'database is locked'
scenarios.when: Error is called on the error value

scenarios.given: a nil error value
scenarios.name: nilErrorPanic
scenarios.then: the function SHALL panic with nil pointer dereference

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts the human-readable message from an error value for inspection and classification.

subSpecs.name: sourceClosing

operations.description: The source closing operation SHALL release file descriptors and temporary resources associated with the uploaded file.
operations.name: closeUploadedFile

scenarios.given: an open multipart.File from a completed upload
scenarios.name: successfulClose
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return nil and release resources
scenarios.when: Close is called on the file

scenarios.given: a multipart.File that was previously closed
scenarios.name: doubleClose
scenarios.then: the function SHOULD return an error or be idempotent
