subSpecs.purpose: Releases system resources held by the uploaded backup file after processing is complete.

subSpecs.name: backupRestore

operations.description: The backup restore operation SHALL replace the current database with the contents of the uploaded backup file.
operations.name: executeDatabaseRestore

and: the backup file is a valid SQLite database
the password matches or is empty
scenarios.given: a valid .db backup file and correct database path
scenarios.name: successfulRestore
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return nil and the database SHALL contain backup data
scenarios.when: Restore is called with the file reader

scenarios.given: an encrypted backup file with wrong password
scenarios.name: wrongPasswordRestore
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return an error indicating authentication failure
scenarios.when: Restore is called with incorrect password

operations.description: The backup restore operation SHALL handle password-protected backups by accepting and validating the provided password.
operations.name: handleEncryptedBackup

and: the password parameter matches the backup encryption key
scenarios.given: a password-protected backup with known password 'secret123'
scenarios.name: correctPasswordDecryption
scenarios.then: the function SHALL decrypt and restore successfully

scenarios.given: a password-protected backup with no password provided
scenarios.name: missingPasswordDecryption
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return an error indicating password required
scenarios.when: Restore is called with empty password

subSpecs.purpose: Executes the database restoration process from a backup file, including password decryption if needed.

subSpecs.name: postFormExtraction

operations.description: The post form extraction SHALL retrieve non-file form values from the multipart form data.
operations.name: extractFormField

scenarios.given: a multipart form with field 'password=secret123'
scenarios.name: passwordFieldExtraction
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return 'secret123'

and: the field is not present in the form
scenarios.given: a multipart form without a 'restart' field
scenarios.name: missingFieldExtraction
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return an empty string

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves additional form fields such as passwords and flags from multipart upload requests.

subSpecs.name: restoreClientErrorClassification

operations.description: The client error classification SHALL determine if a restore error originated from client-provided data versus server/system failures.
operations.name: classifyRestoreError

scenarios.given: a restore error with message 'sqlcipher: bad password'
scenarios.name: badPasswordClientError
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return true

scenarios.given: a restore error with message 'disk I/O error'
scenarios.name: diskIOClientError
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return false

scenarios.given: a wrapped error with backup.ErrBadPassword in the chain
scenarios.name: wrappedBadPasswordError
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return true

scenarios.given: a restore error with message 'file is not a database'
scenarios.name: invalidDatabaseClientError
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return true

subSpecs.purpose: Classifies restore errors to determine appropriate HTTP status codes (4xx vs 5xx responses).

subSpecs.name: restartDelay

operations.description: The restart delay operation SHALL pause execution to allow in-flight operations to complete before triggering application restart.
operations.name: pauseBeforeRestart

scenarios.given: a successful restore completion
scenarios.name: standardRestartDelay
scenarios.then: the function SHALL block for 100ms before returning

scenarios.given: a restore completion in test mode
scenarios.name: zeroDelayRestart
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return immediately

subSpecs.purpose: Introduces a controlled delay before application restart to ensure clean shutdown of active connections.

subSpecs.name: restoreRestartCallback

operations.description: The restart callback operation SHALL invoke the configured restart handler to gracefully restart the application after successful restore.
operations.name: triggerApplicationRestart

and: the handler is configured with os.Exit(0)
scenarios.given: a successful database restore and valid restart handler
scenarios.name: successfulRestartTrigger
scenarios.then: the function SHALL call the restart handler and may not return

scenarios.given: a BackupHandler with no restart handler configured
scenarios.name: nilRestartHandler
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return an error or no-op safely

subSpecs.purpose: Triggers the application restart mechanism after a successful database restore operation.

subSpecs.name: multipartRestoreTest

operations.description: The multipart restore test SHALL verify end-to-end backup restoration using multipart form upload with current database as target.
operations.name: executeMultipartRestoreTest

and: the test database is seeded with 100 rows
scenarios.given: a test environment with initialized database and backup handler
scenarios.name: successfulMultipartRestore
scenarios.then: the database SHALL contain 50 rows after restore
scenarios.when: the HTTP restore endpoint is called with multipart upload

scenarios.given: a valid multipart request with .db file
scenarios.name: httpSuccessResponse
scenarios.then: the response status SHALL be 200 OK
scenarios.when: the request is executed via httptest

and: the write queue is properly cleaned up
scenarios.given: a test with appdb.NewWriteQueue
scenarios.name: resourceCleanup
scenarios.then: t.Cleanup SHALL ensure queue closure

subSpecs.purpose: Validates the complete HTTP handler flow for multipart backup restore including database state verification.

subSpecs.name: refreshErrorString

operations.description: The refresh error string operation SHALL format the refreshError into a human-readable message containing the underlying error and loaded state.
operations.name: formatRefreshError

scenarios.given: a refreshError with underlying connection error and 'active' load state
scenarios.name: formattedRefreshError
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a string containing 'connection refused' and 'active'

scenarios.given: a refreshError with empty load state
scenarios.name: emptyLoadStateError
scenarios.then: the function SHALL include empty string representation in output

subSpecs.purpose: Provides string representation of refresh errors for logging and debugging purposes.

subSpecs.name: storeClosing

operations.description: The store closing operation SHALL close the underlying database connection and release all associated resources.
operations.name: closeDatabaseConnection

scenarios.given: a Store with active database connection
scenarios.name: successfulStoreClose
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return nil and the connection SHALL be closed

and: the database connection is already closed
scenarios.given: a Store with previously closed connection
scenarios.name: idempotentStoreClose
scenarios.then: the function SHOULD return an error or be safely idempotent

subSpecs.purpose: Releases database connections and resources held by the Store instance.

data.name: LogHandler

operations.description: Returns paginated request logs with optional filters for provider, connection, model, combo, modality, status, date range, and search terms.
operations.name: List request logs

and: legacy 'provider_type_id' parameter is accepted as alias for 'provider_id'
scenarios.given: Multiple request logs exist in the database
scenarios.name: Successful paginated query with filters
scenarios.then: Returns paginated results matching all specified filter criteria
scenarios.when: List is called with valid page, per_page, and filter parameters

and: legacy 'since' parameter accepted as milliseconds timestamp
scenarios.given: Request logs span multiple days
scenarios.name: Date range filtering with ISO dates
scenarios.then: Returns only logs within the specified date range
scenarios.when: List is called with start_date and end_date parameters

and: legacy 'until' parameter accepted as milliseconds timestamp
scenarios.given: Request logs exist with various status codes
scenarios.name: Filter by status code
scenarios.then: Returns only logs matching the specified HTTP status code
scenarios.when: List is called with status_code parameter

operations.description: Deletes request logs older than a specified retention window of 7, 30, or 90 days.
inputs: older_than_days: int (JSON body, must be 7, 30, or 90)
operations.name: Clear old request logs

scenarios.given: Request logs older than 30 days exist in the database
scenarios.name: Successful deletion with valid retention period
scenarios.then: Deletes all request logs with timestamp older than 30 days and returns count of deleted records

scenarios.given: Any request body with invalid retention period
scenarios.name: Rejection of invalid retention period
scenarios.then: Returns 400 Bad Request with validation error

operations.description: Returns currently in-flight proxied requests for real-time monitoring on the Logs page dashboard.
operations.name: Get active in-flight requests

and: Data sourced from active request tracking module
scenarios.given: Multiple requests are currently being proxied through the system
scenarios.name: List active requests during traffic
scenarios.then: Returns all in-flight requests with their current state

and: Returns empty array when no active requests
scenarios.given: No requests are currently being proxied
scenarios.name: Empty active requests during idle
scenarios.then: Returns empty array

operations.description: Retrieves a single request log entry by its unique identifier with all detailed fields including token breakdown, latency, cost, and client information.
operations.name: Get single log entry
outputs: log record with fields: id, timestamp, connection_id, provider_type_id, model_id, combo_id, modality, input_tokens, output_tokens, reasoning_tokens, cached_tokens, cache_creation_tokens, tokens_estimated, latency_ms, status_code, error_message, cost_usd, client_ip, user_agent, created_at

and: Nullable fields use pointer types in response
scenarios.given: A request log with ID 'abc123' exists in the database
scenarios.name: Successful retrieval of existing log
scenarios.then: Returns complete log record with all fields populated

scenarios.given: No request log with ID 'nonexistent' exists
scenarios.name: Log not found
scenarios.then: Returns 404 Not Found with error message

operations.description: Returns aggregated usage statistics across providers, models, and daily breakdown for dashboard analytics.
operations.name: Get aggregated log statistics

and: Daily usage always covers 30 days regardless of hours parameter
scenarios.given: Request logs exist for multiple providers and models over past 24 hours
scenarios.name: Default statistics for last 24 hours
scenarios.then: Returns provider usage, model usage for 24 hours, and daily usage for 30 days
scenarios.when: Stats is called without hours parameter

and: Allows custom time window for provider and model aggregations
scenarios.given: Request logs exist for the past 72 hours
scenarios.name: Custom hours window for statistics
scenarios.then: Returns provider and model usage for 72 hours, daily usage for 30 days

data.purpose: Handles HTTP requests for request log querying, retrieval, cleanup, real-time active request monitoring, and aggregated usage statistics for administrative dashboard.
sideEffects: Reads from request_logs database table
Deletes from request_logs database table
Reads from active in-flight request tracking

data.name: ComboHandler

operations.description: The system SHALL return a list of all existing combos.
operations.name: List

scenarios.given: Multiple combos exist in the system
scenarios.name: List all combos
scenarios.then: All combos are returned in the response

operations.description: The system SHALL return a single combo including its associated steps.
operations.name: Get
outputs: HTTP response with combo details and steps

scenarios.given: A combo with ID exists and has steps
scenarios.name: Get existing combo with steps
scenarios.then: The combo and its steps are returned
scenarios.when: Get is invoked with valid combo ID

scenarios.given: A combo with ID does not exist
scenarios.name: Get non-existent combo
scenarios.then: Not found error is returned
scenarios.when: Get is invoked with invalid combo ID

operations.description: The system SHALL create a new combo with steps after validating the strategy and configuration.
operations.name: Create
outputs: HTTP response with created combo ID

scenarios.given: Valid combo payload with supported strategy and valid fusion config
scenarios.name: Create combo with valid data
scenarios.then: Combo is persisted with steps and combo ID returned
scenarios.when: Create is invoked with valid request body

scenarios.given: Invalid or unsupported strategy
scenarios.name: Reject invalid strategy
scenarios.then: Validation error is returned
scenarios.when: Create is invoked with unsupported strategy

operations.description: The system SHALL modify an existing combo's properties and steps.
operations.name: Update

scenarios.given: Combo exists and update payload is valid
scenarios.name: Update existing combo
scenarios.then: Combo is modified and success response returned
scenarios.when: Update is invoked with valid changes

scenarios.given: Invalid fusion configuration in payload
scenarios.name: Reject invalid fusion config
scenarios.then: Validation error is returned
scenarios.when: Update is invoked with invalid config

operations.description: The system SHALL remove a combo from the system.
operations.name: Delete

scenarios.given: Combo with ID exists
scenarios.name: Delete existing combo
scenarios.then: Combo is removed from system
scenarios.when: Delete is invoked with valid combo ID

operations.description: The system SHALL add a new step to an existing combo.
operations.name: AddStep
outputs: HTTP response with updated combo steps

scenarios.given: Combo exists and step data is valid
scenarios.name: Add step to existing combo
scenarios.then: Step is appended to combo's step list
scenarios.when: AddStep is invoked with valid step data

operations.description: The system SHALL remove a step from an existing combo.
operations.name: RemoveStep
outputs: HTTP response with updated combo steps

scenarios.given: Combo and step exist
scenarios.name: Remove step from combo
scenarios.then: Step is removed from combo's step list
scenarios.when: RemoveStep is invoked with valid step identifier

operations.description: The system SHALL return aggregated request metrics for combos within a configurable time window.
operations.name: Metrics

and: metrics contain latency and request counts
scenarios.given: Request logs exist within default 86400 second window
scenarios.name: Get default window metrics
scenarios.then: Aggregated combo metrics are returned
scenarios.when: Metrics is invoked without window parameter

scenarios.given: Custom window parameter provided (within max 2592000 seconds)
scenarios.name: Get custom window metrics
scenarios.then: Metrics aggregated for specified window are returned
scenarios.when: Metrics is invoked with valid window query parameter

operations.description: The system SHALL populate the database with predefined default combos.
operations.name: SeedDefaults

scenarios.given: Database is initialized and default combo definitions exist
scenarios.name: Seed default combos
scenarios.then: Default combos are inserted into database

data.purpose: HTTP handler for combo administration: manages combo lifecycle (CRUD), step management, metrics retrieval, and default seeding.
sideEffects: Reads from combos and combo_steps tables
Writes to combos and combo_steps tables
Reads from request logs for metrics aggregation

data.name: HealthHandler

operations.description: SHALL return a liveness probe response indicating whether the service is operational, degraded, or unavailable based on in-memory store and usage-tracker state without blocking on database access.
operations.name: Health
outputs: JSON response with status, db, version, latest_version, update_available, must_change_password

and: no usage events have been dropped
scenarios.given: Service is running normally
scenarios.name: Healthy service
scenarios.then: Returns HTTP 200 with status 'ok' and current version information

scenarios.given: Critical dependencies are uninitialized
scenarios.name: Unhealthy service
scenarios.then: Returns HTTP 503 with status 'error'

scenarios.given: Write pipeline is saturated
scenarios.name: Degraded service
scenarios.then: Returns HTTP 200 with status 'degraded' to avoid load balancer flapping

operations.description: SHALL determine whether the admin user must change their password by checking the admin_password_changed setting flag, with fallback to checking for existence of admin_password_hash for backward compatibility.
operations.name: mustChangePassword

scenarios.given: Admin password changed flag exists in settings
scenarios.name: Password already changed
scenarios.then: Returns false indicating no password change required

scenarios.given: Legacy database without password changed flag
scenarios.name: Legacy database with password hash
scenarios.then: Returns true indicating password change is required

scenarios.given: Fresh installation without password configured
scenarios.name: No password configured
scenarios.then: Returns false indicating no password change required

operations.description: SHALL force a fresh fetch of the latest version information from the version checker and return current version, latest version, and update availability status.
operations.name: CheckUpdate
outputs: JSON response with version, latest_version, update_available

scenarios.given: Version checker is available
scenarios.name: Successful version check
scenarios.then: Returns HTTP 200 with current version, latest version, and update availability

scenarios.given: Version checker is unavailable
scenarios.name: Version checker unavailable
scenarios.then: Returns HTTP 503 with error message

scenarios.given: Version checker cannot reach upstream
scenarios.name: Version refresh failure
scenarios.then: Returns HTTP 502 with error message

operations.description: SHALL return the project changelog markdown content via the version checker to avoid direct browser requests to external sources.
operations.name: Changelog

scenarios.given: Version checker is available
scenarios.name: Successful changelog retrieval
scenarios.then: Returns HTTP 200 with markdown content

scenarios.given: Version checker is unavailable
scenarios.name: Changelog unavailable
scenarios.then: Returns HTTP 503 with error message

scenarios.given: Upstream changelog unavailable
scenarios.name: Changelog fetch failure
scenarios.then: Returns HTTP 502 with error message

operations.description: SHALL return operational metrics including connection health, usage buffer state, rate-limited and quota-exhausted connection counts, and daily aggregated usage statistics.
operations.name: Metrics
outputs: JSON response with buffer_length, healthy_connections, dropped_usage_events, rate_limited, quota_exhausted, requests_today, tokens_today, cost_today

scenarios.given: Normal operational state
scenarios.name: Full metrics retrieval
scenarios.then: Returns HTTP 200 with complete operational metrics

and: database query for rate-limited connections fails
scenarios.given: Partial database degradation
scenarios.name: Metrics with database issues
scenarios.then: Returns available metrics with zero or error values for failed queries

data.purpose: Exposes health, readiness, version, and operational metrics endpoints for load balancer probes, dashboards, and observability systems.
sideEffects: Reads from settings table in database
Reads from connections table in database
Invokes usage.Aggregator for daily statistics
Invokes version.Checker for remote version information
Reads from connstate.Store for healthy connection count
Reads from usage.Tracker for buffer and dropped event counts

data.name: DevicesHandler

operations.description: Returns the key name and masked device list for the given API key.
operations.name: GetDevices
outputs: JSON response with keyId, name, count, and devices

scenarios.given: API key exists in database with ID '123' and has 2 tracked devices
scenarios.name: Valid API key with tracked devices
scenarios.then: Response contains key name, device count of 2, and full device details including fingerprint, IP, userAgent, and lastSeen timestamp

scenarios.given: No API key exists in database with ID '999'
scenarios.name: Non-existent API key
scenarios.then: Response contains error message 'key not found'

and: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error response returned
scenarios.given: Database query fails due to connection error
scenarios.name: Database error during key lookup
scenarios.then: Response contains error message with database error details
scenarios.when: GetDevices is called with any id

scenarios.given: API key exists in database with ID '456' but has no tracked devices
scenarios.name: Valid API key with no tracked devices
scenarios.then: Response contains key name, device count of 0, and empty devices array

data.purpose: Exposes administrative endpoint to retrieve distinct devices tracked for each API key, combining key metadata from database with device tracking data.
sideEffects: Reads from api_keys database table
Reads from usage.DeviceTracker in-memory state

data.name: DashboardHandler

operations.description: The DashboardHandler SHALL be initialized with a database connection, connection state store, and usage tracker.
operations.name: Initialize DashboardHandler

scenarios.given: Valid dependencies are provided
scenarios.name: Successful initialization
scenarios.then: A new DashboardHandler is returned with all dependencies wired

operations.description: The Stats operation SHALL return aggregated dashboard statistics combining usage data and system metrics.
operations.name: Get Aggregated Dashboard Statistics

scenarios.given: Dashboard handler is initialized with valid dependencies
scenarios.name: Successful stats retrieval with all data sources
scenarios.then: Response includes today's usage summary and current system metrics

and: Usage tracker returns error or no data
scenarios.given: Dashboard handler is initialized but dependencies are degraded
scenarios.name: Stats retrieval with partial failures
scenarios.then: Response includes available data with appropriate defaults for missing metrics

operations.description: The cpuUsage operation SHALL measure current CPU utilization with a 5-second sampling window to optimize for frequent dashboard polls.
operations.name: Measure CPU Utilization

scenarios.given: System CPU metrics are available
scenarios.name: Successful CPU measurement
scenarios.then: Returns current CPU utilization percentage as float64

and: System may be containerized without /proc access
scenarios.given: System CPU metrics are unavailable
scenarios.name: CPU measurement failure
scenarios.then: Returns 0 or default value indicating unavailability

operations.description: The collectSystemMetrics operation SHALL gather comprehensive system metrics including CPU count, memory usage, disk usage, and working directory information.
operations.name: Collect System Metrics

scenarios.given: System is operational with standard OS interfaces
scenarios.name: Successful system metrics collection
scenarios.then: Returns populated systemMetrics struct with all fields

scenarios.given: System metrics are partially unavailable
scenarios.name: Partial system metrics collection
scenarios.then: Returns systemMetrics with available fields populated and defaults for unavailable fields

operations.description: The ProviderSummary operation SHALL return a summary of providers including disabled reasons so the dashboard can display account status information.
operations.name: Get Provider Summary

scenarios.given: Provider accounts exist with various states
scenarios.name: Successful provider summary retrieval
scenarios.then: Returns summary including provider statuses and disabled_reasons

scenarios.given: No provider accounts exist
scenarios.name: Empty provider summary
scenarios.then: Returns empty or minimal summary response

operations.description: The RecentLogs operation SHALL return the last N log entries for display on the dashboard.
operations.name: Get Recent Log Entries

scenarios.given: Log entries exist in the system
scenarios.name: Successful recent logs retrieval
scenarios.then: Returns last N log entries ordered by recency

and: Database is empty or newly initialized
scenarios.given: No log entries exist
scenarios.name: Empty recent logs
scenarios.then: Returns empty array or appropriate no-data response

data.purpose: Provides administrative dashboard capabilities including aggregated statistics, system metrics, provider summaries, and recent log access for operational monitoring.
sideEffects: Reads from database (usage data, provider data, logs)
Reads system metrics (CPU, memory, disk)
Writes HTTP JSON responses

subSpecs.name: rowsClose

operations.description: The rows.Close operation SHALL release all resources associated with the active database result set.
operations.name: closeResultSet

and: no subsequent operations on the closed rows SHALL succeed
scenarios.given: a database query has returned an active rows cursor with pending results
scenarios.name: closeActiveResultSet
scenarios.then: all resources associated with the rows cursor are released and the cursor becomes invalid
scenarios.when: rows.Close is invoked on the active cursor

subSpecs.purpose: Releases database resources held by an active query result set to prevent connection leaks.

subSpecs.name: rowsNext

operations.description: The rows.Next operation SHALL advance the cursor to the next row in the result set and report whether a row is available.
operations.name: advanceCursor

and: subsequent rows.Scan SHALL populate values from the current row
scenarios.given: a database query has returned a rows cursor with multiple result rows
scenarios.name: advanceToNextRow
scenarios.then: the cursor advances to the next row and returns true
scenarios.when: rows.Next is called and additional rows remain in the result set

and: rows.Close SHOULD be called after iteration completes
scenarios.given: a database query has returned a rows cursor and all rows have been iterated
scenarios.name: noMoreRows
scenarios.then: rows.Next returns false indicating no further rows are available
scenarios.when: rows.Next is called after the final row has been consumed

subSpecs.purpose: Advances the database cursor through the result set row by row for sequential processing.

subSpecs.name: rowsScan

operations.description: The rows.Scan operation SHALL copy column values from the current row into the provided destination variables.
inputs: active database rows cursor positioned on a row
pointer variables for each column destination
operations.name: scanRowValues
outputs: error if type mismatch or scan failure

and: the number of destination arguments MUST match the number of columns in the query
scenarios.given: rows.Next has returned true and destination pointers are provided for all columns
scenarios.name: scanValidRow
scenarios.then: column values are copied into the destination variables and nil error is returned
scenarios.when: rows.Scan is called with properly typed destination pointers

and: the current row position MUST NOT advance automatically
scenarios.given: rows.Next has returned true but destination pointer type does not match column type
scenarios.name: scanTypeMismatch
scenarios.then: an error is returned and destination variables remain unmodified
scenarios.when: rows.Scan is called with a destination pointer of incompatible type

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts column values from the current database row into Go variables for application use.

subSpecs.name: usageNewAggregator

operations.description: The usage.NewAggregator operation SHALL create a new Aggregator instance configured to compute usage statistics over a specified time range.
inputs: usage data source or configuration parameters
operations.name: createAggregator

and: the returned Aggregator MUST be ready to accept aggregation queries
scenarios.given: valid configuration parameters for usage data aggregation are provided
scenarios.name: createValidAggregator
scenarios.then: a functional Aggregator instance is returned with no error
scenarios.when: usage.NewAggregator is invoked with valid parameters

and: error details SHOULD indicate which configuration parameter was invalid
scenarios.given: configuration parameters contain invalid or missing required values
scenarios.name: aggregatorCreationFailure
scenarios.then: nil Aggregator and a descriptive error are returned
scenarios.when: usage.NewAggregator is invoked with invalid parameters

subSpecs.purpose: Instantiates a usage statistics aggregator for computing aggregated metrics over time windows.

subSpecs.name: aggGetTodaySummary

operations.description: The agg.GetTodaySummary operation SHALL retrieve aggregated statistics for the current calendar day including error counts and latency metrics.
inputs: Aggregator instance with access to current day's usage data
operations.name: retrieveTodayStats
outputs: TodaySummary containing error counts and latency statistics

and: latency statistics SHALL include minimum, maximum, and average response times
scenarios.given: the Aggregator has collected usage data throughout the current day
scenarios.name: todayWithData
scenarios.then: a TodaySummary is returned with accurate aggregated error counts and latency metrics
scenarios.when: agg.GetTodaySummary is called during operational hours with active data collection

and: error counts SHALL be zero and latency metrics SHALL reflect empty state
scenarios.given: the current day has no recorded usage events
scenarios.name: todayNoData
scenarios.then: a TodaySummary is returned with zero values for all statistics
scenarios.when: agg.GetTodaySummary is called at day start before any events are recorded

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves pre-computed daily aggregate statistics including error rates and latency distributions for dashboard display.

subSpecs.name: errError

operations.description: The err.Error operation SHALL return a human-readable string describing the error condition.
operations.name: getErrorMessage

and: the returned string MUST NOT be empty for non-nil errors
scenarios.given: an error value has been produced by a failed operation
scenarios.name: retrieveErrorMessage
scenarios.then: a non-empty string describing the failure is returned
scenarios.when: err.Error is called on the error value

and: nil error invocation behavior is undefined per Go specification
scenarios.given: a nil error value
scenarios.name: nilErrorBehavior
scenarios.then: the method call panics or returns empty string per Go runtime behavior

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts the descriptive text from an error value for logging, reporting, or user communication.

subSpecs.name: collectSystemMetrics

operations.description: The collectSystemMetrics operation SHALL gather current system resource utilization including CPU, memory, disk, and working directory information.
inputs: DashboardHandler instance with access to system APIs
operations.name: gatherSystemMetrics

and: CPU metrics SHALL include both per-core and total utilization percentages
scenarios.given: the system is operational with multiple CPU cores under variable load
scenarios.name: collectUnderNormalLoad
scenarios.then: a systemMetrics struct is returned with accurate CPU, memory, disk, and working directory data
scenarios.when: collectSystemMetrics is invoked on the DashboardHandler

and: metrics collection failures for individual components MUST NOT prevent return of partial data
scenarios.given: one or more system metric sources are temporarily unavailable
scenarios.name: partialCollectionFailure
scenarios.then: a systemMetrics struct is returned with available data and zero values for failed components
scenarios.when: collectSystemMetrics encounters errors from cpuUsage, mem.VirtualMemory, or disk.Usage

subSpecs.purpose: Collects real-time system resource metrics for display on the operational monitoring dashboard.

subSpecs.name: intConversion

operations.description: The int conversion operation SHALL convert a numeric value to the platform-dependent integer type.
operations.name: convertToInt

and: overflow behavior is implementation-defined for values exceeding int range
scenarios.given: a floating-point or larger integer value within the valid int range
scenarios.name: validConversion
scenarios.then: the value is truncated or converted to integer representation
scenarios.when: int() is applied to the numeric value

and: negative values MUST be preserved in the conversion
scenarios.given: a negative floating-point value
scenarios.name: negativeConversion
scenarios.then: the negative integer value is returned with fractional part truncated toward zero
scenarios.when: int() is applied to a negative float

subSpecs.purpose: Converts numeric values to the native integer type for indexing, counting, or API compatibility.

subSpecs.name: timeSince

operations.description: The time.Since operation SHALL calculate the elapsed duration between a given past time and the current time.
operations.name: calculateElapsed

and: the result MUST be non-negative when the input is in the past
scenarios.given: a timestamp recorded at the start of an operation
scenarios.name: measureOperationDuration
scenarios.then: a positive duration equal to the elapsed time is returned
scenarios.when: time.Since is called with the start timestamp after operation completion

and: the result MUST be negative or zero when the input is in the future
scenarios.given: a timestamp representing a future scheduled event
scenarios.name: futureTimestamp
scenarios.then: a negative duration or zero is returned indicating time until the event
scenarios.when: time.Since is called with a future timestamp

subSpecs.purpose: Measures elapsed time from a recorded timestamp for performance metrics and timeout calculations.

subSpecs.name: testDashboardStats

operations.description: The TestDashboardStats_IncludesTodayAndSystemMetrics operation SHALL verify that the dashboard statistics endpoint returns both today's usage summary and current system metrics.
operations.name: verifyDashboardStats

and: the response MUST contain valid JSON with both todaySummary and systemMetrics fields
systemMetrics MUST include non-zero CPU count
todaySummary MUST reflect seeded tracker data
scenarios.given: a test environment with initialized database, usage tracker with today's data, and mock HTTP context
scenarios.name: successfulStatsRetrieval
scenarios.then: the HTTP response has status 200 with JSON body containing today's aggregated usage and current system metrics
scenarios.when: the Stats endpoint is invoked via HTTP GET request

and: database setup failures MUST fail the test immediately
handler initialization errors MUST fail the test immediately
scenarios.given: database connection or handler initialization fails during test setup
scenarios.name: setupFailure
scenarios.then: the test fails with fatal error describing the setup failure
scenarios.when: newDashboardTestDB or NewDashboardHandler returns an error

subSpecs.purpose: End-to-end test validating that the dashboard statistics API returns complete and accurate operational data.

subSpecs.name: storeClose

operations.description: The Close operation SHALL terminate the underlying database connection and release associated resources.
inputs: Store instance with active database connection
operations.name: closeStoreConnection

and: subsequent Store operations MUST return connection errors
scenarios.given: a Store instance with an active healthy database connection
scenarios.name: closeHealthyConnection
scenarios.then: the database connection is closed and nil error is returned
scenarios.when: Close is called on the Store

and: multiple Close calls SHOULD be safe or return appropriate errors
scenarios.given: a Store instance whose connection has already been closed
scenarios.name: doubleClose
scenarios.then: an error indicating connection already closed is returned, or nil if idempotent
scenarios.when: Close is called on the already-closed Store

subSpecs.purpose: Cleanly terminates database connectivity for a Store instance to prevent resource leaks.

subSpecs.name: aggregatorGetTodaySummary

operations.description: The GetTodaySummary operation SHALL compute and return today's aggregate statistics including total errors and latency percentiles.
inputs: Aggregator instance with access to time-bucketed usage data
operations.name: computeTodaySummary
outputs: TodaySummary struct with error count and latency statistics

and: latency statistics SHALL include p50, p95, and p99 percentiles
error count SHALL include both total and rate per request
scenarios.given: the Aggregator has processed request logs for the current calendar day
scenarios.name: todayWithTraffic
scenarios.then: a TodaySummary with accurate error counts and latency percentiles is returned
scenarios.when: GetTodaySummary is called with active aggregation window

and: all numeric fields SHALL be zero
timestamp SHALL indicate start of current day
scenarios.given: no requests have been recorded since midnight in the aggregator's timezone
scenarios.name: todayEmptyTraffic
scenarios.then: a TodaySummary with zero values and today's start timestamp is returned
scenarios.when: GetTodaySummary is called before any daily traffic is recorded

subSpecs.purpose: Computes daily operational KPIs from aggregated request data for dashboard presentation.

subSpecs.name: refreshErrorError

operations.description: The Error operation SHALL return the string representation of a refresh error for logging and debugging purposes.
operations.name: getRefreshErrorMessage

and: the returned string MUST include the underlying error cause if available
scenarios.given: a refreshError created during a failed metrics refresh operation
scenarios.name: refreshFailureDescription
scenarios.then: a descriptive string explaining why the refresh failed is returned
scenarios.when: Error is called on the refreshError

and: the string MUST be non-empty for any initialized refreshError
scenarios.given: a refreshError initialized with context about the failure
scenarios.name: nonEmptyError
scenarios.then: a non-empty string is returned suitable for logging or user display
scenarios.when: Error is called on any non-nil refreshError

subSpecs.purpose: Provides human-readable error descriptions for dashboard data refresh failures.

data.name: UpgradeHandler

operations.description: The system SHALL download the latest release asset, verify its SHA256 checksum against the published checksums file, replace the current binary with the verified binary, and return restart instructions.
operations.name: Upgrade
outputs: HTTP response with restart command and hint

and: Binary is written to a staging path
scenarios.given: A newer release is available and the checksums file contains a matching entry
scenarios.name: Successful upgrade with valid checksum
scenarios.then: The binary is verified and replaced

and: HTTP 500 or error response is returned
scenarios.given: The downloaded binary checksum does not match the published checksum
scenarios.name: Checksum verification failure
scenarios.then: The binary is NOT replaced and an error is returned

and: HTTP 500 or error response is returned
scenarios.given: The release asset or checksums file cannot be downloaded
scenarios.name: Download failure
scenarios.then: No local binary changes are made

operations.description: The system SHALL perform an HTTP GET request to the specified URL, read the response body with a size limit, and return the raw bytes.
operations.name: download

scenarios.given: A valid HTTP URL returning 200 OK with a body
scenarios.name: Successful download
scenarios.then: The response body bytes are returned with nil error

scenarios.given: The URL returns a non-2xx status or the request fails
scenarios.name: Failed download
scenarios.then: An error is returned

and: Response is truncated or error is returned
scenarios.given: The response body exceeds the size limit
scenarios.name: Oversized response
scenarios.then: Reading is limited and an error may be returned

operations.description: The system SHALL determine the filesystem path for the upgraded binary by resolving the current executable path, evaluating symlinks, and joining with a versioned filename.
operations.name: upgradeBinaryPath

and: Path includes the versioned asset name
scenarios.given: The current executable can be located and symlinks resolved
scenarios.name: Successful path resolution
scenarios.then: An absolute path string is returned

scenarios.given: The current executable cannot be determined
scenarios.name: Path resolution failure
scenarios.then: An error is returned

operations.description: The system SHALL atomically write the new binary to a staging path, preserve the original binary as a backup, and swap the staged binary into place.
operations.name: writeBinary

and: Staging directory is created if needed
scenarios.given: Valid binary bytes and a writable filesystem
scenarios.name: Successful binary replacement
scenarios.then: The new binary is written and swapped into place

scenarios.given: The filesystem is read-only or lacks space
scenarios.name: Write failure
scenarios.then: No changes are made to the running binary

and: Cleanup of temporary files is attempted
scenarios.given: The swap operation fails after staging
scenarios.name: Swap failure with rollback
scenarios.then: Temporary files are removed and an error is returned

operations.description: The system SHALL compute the platform-specific release asset name based on the current operating system and architecture.
operations.name: assetName

scenarios.given: The program is running on a supported platform
scenarios.name: Asset name generation
scenarios.then: A platform-specific asset filename is returned

operations.description: The system SHALL parse the checksums file content, locate the line matching the specified asset name, and return the hex-encoded SHA256 digest.
operations.name: findChecksum

and: Checksum string is trimmed of whitespace
scenarios.given: A valid checksums file containing the asset entry
scenarios.name: Checksum found
scenarios.then: The SHA256 hex string is returned

scenarios.given: The asset name does not appear in the checksums file
scenarios.name: Checksum not found
scenarios.then: An error is returned

scenarios.given: The checksums file is malformed or empty
scenarios.name: Malformed checksums file
scenarios.then: An error is returned

data.purpose: Manages in-place binary upgrades by downloading verified release artifacts, validating cryptographic checksums, and performing atomic filesystem replacement of the running executable.
sideEffects: HTTP GET requests to release URL and checksums URL
Reads from os.Executable path and symlinks
Writes to filesystem: creates staging directory, writes new binary, copies permissions, renames files
Removes temporary and backup files on success or failure

data.name: RestartHandler

operations.description: The system SHALL trigger a restart of the systemd service unit and return an appropriate HTTP response indicating the restart was initiated.
operations.name: Restart

scenarios.given: The process is running under systemd with a valid unit name
scenarios.name: Successful restart trigger
scenarios.then: The systemd unit restart is requested

scenarios.given: The process is not managed by systemd or the unit is unknown
scenarios.name: Restart unavailable
scenarios.then: An error is returned without triggering restart

data.purpose: Provides an administrative endpoint to trigger restart of the service via systemd, enabling zero-downtime or post-upgrade restart workflows.
sideEffects: Systemd D-Bus or socket communication to request unit restart
May terminate the current process

subSpecs.name: assetName

operations.description: The assetName function SHALL construct a platform-specific binary asset name using the current operating system and architecture.
operations.name: constructAssetName

scenarios.given: the function is called on a linux/amd64 system
scenarios.name: linuxAmd64Asset
scenarios.then: the returned string SHALL be 'linux_amd64' formatted according to the expected pattern

scenarios.given: the function is called on a darwin/arm64 system
scenarios.name: darwinArm64Asset
scenarios.then: the returned string SHALL be 'darwin_arm64' formatted according to the expected pattern

subSpecs.purpose: Generates the platform-specific asset name for the binary download based on current OS and architecture.

subSpecs.name: fmtSprintf

operations.description: fmt.Sprintf SHALL format strings according to a format specifier and return the resulting string.
operations.name: formatString

scenarios.given: a format string with two string placeholders
scenarios.name: formatTwoStrings
scenarios.then: the returned string SHALL be 'linux_amd64'
scenarios.when: Sprintf is called with the format and arguments

subSpecs.purpose: Provides string formatting capabilities used throughout the upgrade and restart workflow.

subSpecs.name: download

operations.description: The download method SHALL perform an HTTP GET request to the specified URL and return the response body as bytes.
operations.name: downloadFromURL

and: response body contains valid binary data
scenarios.given: a valid URL pointing to a downloadable binary
scenarios.name: successfulDownload
scenarios.then: the returned byte slice SHALL contain the complete response body and error SHALL be nil
scenarios.when: download is called with the URL

scenarios.given: a URL that returns a 404 status
scenarios.name: downloadNotFound
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a non-nil error indicating the download failure
scenarios.when: download is called with the URL

operations.description: The download method SHALL limit the maximum readable response size to prevent unbounded memory consumption.
operations.name: limitDownloadSize

and: response body exceeds the configured limit
scenarios.given: a URL returning a response larger than the maximum allowed size
scenarios.name: downloadSizeLimitExceeded
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return an error indicating the size limit was exceeded
scenarios.when: download is called with the URL

subSpecs.purpose: Downloads a binary from a remote URL with size limits and proper error handling.

subSpecs.name: findChecksum

operations.description: The findChecksum function SHALL parse checksum data to locate and extract the SHA-256 checksum for a specified asset file.
operations.name: extractAssetChecksum

scenarios.given: a valid checksum file with matching asset name
scenarios.name: findMatchingChecksum
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return 'abc123' as the checksum and nil error
scenarios.when: findChecksum is called with the data and asset

and: checksum file does not contain the asset name
scenarios.given: a checksum file without the requested asset
scenarios.name: checksumNotFound
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return a non-nil error indicating checksum not found
scenarios.when: findChecksum is called with the data and asset

operations.description: The findChecksum function SHALL handle various checksum file formats including standard GNU coreutils format and prefixed formats.
operations.name: parseChecksumFormats

scenarios.given: a checksum file with BSD-style prefixed format
scenarios.name: parsePrefixedChecksum
scenarios.then: the function SHALL correctly extract 'abc123' as the checksum
scenarios.when: findChecksum is called with the data and asset

subSpecs.purpose: Parses checksum files to find and extract the SHA-256 hash for a specific asset.

subSpecs.name: sha256Sum256

operations.description: sha256.Sum256 SHALL compute the SHA-256 hash of the provided byte slice and return a fixed-size array.
operations.name: computeSHA256

scenarios.given: a known input string
scenarios.name: verifyKnownHash
scenarios.then: the returned array SHALL match the expected SHA-256 hash value
scenarios.when: Sum256 is called with the byte slice

subSpecs.purpose: Computes SHA-256 cryptographic hashes for binary verification.

subSpecs.name: stringsEqualFold

operations.description: strings.EqualFold SHALL perform case-insensitive string comparison and return true if the strings are equal.
operations.name: caseInsensitiveCompare

scenarios.given: two strings differing only in case
scenarios.name: caseInsensitiveMatch
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return true

scenarios.given: two different strings
scenarios.name: noMatch
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return false

subSpecs.purpose: Performs case-insensitive string comparison for platform detection.

subSpecs.name: writeBinary

operations.description: The writeBinary method SHALL write the provided binary data to a temporary location and atomically move it to the final destination.
operations.name: atomicBinaryWrite

scenarios.given: a new binary to install
scenarios.name: freshInstall
scenarios.then: the function SHALL create necessary directories, write the binary, and return the installation path

scenarios.given: an existing binary that needs updating
scenarios.name: upgradeExisting
scenarios.then: the function SHALL preserve the existing binary as backup before writing the new one

operations.description: The writeBinary method SHALL ensure the written binary has appropriate permissions and is ready for execution.
operations.name: setBinaryPermissions

scenarios.given: a valid binary payload
scenarios.name: executablePermissions
scenarios.then: the installed binary SHALL have executable permissions set

subSpecs.purpose: Atomically writes a new binary to disk with proper permissions and backup handling.

subSpecs.name: restartInstructions

operations.description: The restartInstructions function SHALL return the systemctl command and user-facing hint for restarting the service.
operations.name: provideRestartCommand

and: service name is determined from executable
scenarios.given: the function is called to get restart information
scenarios.name: systemdRestart
scenarios.then: the returned command SHALL be a valid systemctl restart command and hint SHALL explain the restart process

subSpecs.purpose: Generates the systemd restart command and user instructions for service restart.

subSpecs.name: testUpgradeDownloadsAndVerifiesBinary

operations.description: The TestUpgrade_DownloadsAndVerifiesBinary test SHALL verify the complete upgrade flow including download, checksum verification, and binary installation.
operations.name: endToEndUpgradeTest

and: mock server serves binary and checksums
scenarios.given: a test environment with mock HTTP server
scenarios.name: successfulUpgradeFlow
scenarios.then: the test SHALL verify binary download, SHA-256 checksum validation, and successful installation

scenarios.given: a mock server with invalid checksum
scenarios.name: checksumVerificationFailure
scenarios.then: the test SHALL detect and report the checksum mismatch
scenarios.when: the upgrade handler processes the download

subSpecs.purpose: Integration test that validates the complete binary download, verification, and installation workflow.

subSpecs.name: upgradeBinaryPath

operations.description: The upgradeBinaryPath method SHALL determine the filesystem path where the upgraded binary should be installed.
operations.name: determineInstallPath

scenarios.given: a running service instance
scenarios.name: standardInstallPath
scenarios.then: the returned path SHALL be the directory containing the current executable joined with the upgrade binary name

scenarios.given: a symlinked executable path
scenarios.name: resolveSymlinks
scenarios.then: the function SHALL resolve all symlinks before determining the final path

subSpecs.purpose: Resolves the installation path for the upgraded binary, handling symlinks and executable location.

subSpecs.name: osMkdirAll

operations.description: os.MkdirAll SHALL create a directory and all necessary parent directories with the specified permissions.
operations.name: createDirectoryTree

scenarios.given: a nested directory path that does not exist
scenarios.name: createNestedDirectories
scenarios.then: all directories in the path SHALL be created with the specified permissions

subSpecs.purpose: Creates directory hierarchies needed for binary installation.

subSpecs.name: filepathDir

operations.description: filepath.Dir SHALL return the directory portion of a file path, excluding the final path element.
operations.name: extractDirectory

scenarios.given: a file path
scenarios.name: getParentDirectory
scenarios.then: the returned string SHALL be /usr/local/bin

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts the directory component from a file path for directory creation.

subSpecs.name: osStat

operations.description: os.Stat SHALL return file information for the named file, or an error if the file does not exist.
operations.name: checkFileExists

and: file exists at the specified path
scenarios.given: an existing file
scenarios.name: fileExists
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return non-nil FileInfo and nil error

scenarios.given: a non-existent file path
scenarios.name: fileNotFound
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return an error matching os.IsNotExist

subSpecs.purpose: Checks for file existence and retrieves file metadata.

subSpecs.name: copyFile

operations.description: The copyFile function SHALL copy both the contents and permissions from the source file to the destination file.
operations.name: copyWithPermissions

scenarios.given: a source file to backup
scenarios.name: copyAndPreservePermissions
scenarios.then: the destination file SHALL have identical contents and permissions as the source

scenarios.given: a valid source file
scenarios.name: successfulCopy
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return nil error after complete copy

subSpecs.purpose: Copies file contents and preserves original permissions for backup operations.

subSpecs.name: fmtErrorf

operations.description: fmt.Errorf SHALL format an error message and return it as an error value.
operations.name: createFormattedError

scenarios.given: an error condition to report
scenarios.name: formatErrorMessage
scenarios.then: the returned error SHALL have message 'download failed: connection refused'

subSpecs.purpose: Creates formatted error values with context for error propagation.

subSpecs.name: osWriteFile

operations.description: os.WriteFile SHALL write data to the named file with the specified permissions, creating or truncating as needed.
operations.name: writeFileData

scenarios.given: binary data to persist
scenarios.name: writeBinaryFile
scenarios.then: the file SHALL contain the exact data and have the specified permissions

subSpecs.purpose: Writes binary data to temporary files during atomic installation.

subSpecs.name: osRename

operations.description: os.Rename SHALL atomically move a file from old path to new path.
operations.name: atomicFileMove

scenarios.given: a temporary file to promote
scenarios.name: atomicPromotion
scenarios.then: the file SHALL be moved atomically and oldpath SHALL no longer exist

subSpecs.purpose: Performs atomic file moves for safe binary replacement.

subSpecs.name: osRemove

operations.description: os.Remove SHALL delete the named file or empty directory.
operations.name: deleteFile

scenarios.given: a file to clean up
scenarios.name: removeExistingFile
scenarios.then: the file SHALL no longer exist after successful call

subSpecs.purpose: Removes temporary files during cleanup or rollback operations.

subSpecs.name: tokenStoreRemove

operations.description: The Remove method SHALL delete a token from the token store for the specified connection ID.
operations.name: removeToken

scenarios.given: a valid connection ID with stored token
scenarios.name: removeExistingToken
scenarios.then: the token SHALL be removed and nil error returned
scenarios.when: Remove is called with the connID

and: token does not exist for connID
scenarios.given: a connection ID without stored token
scenarios.name: removeMissingToken
scenarios.then: the function SHALL return nil error without panic
scenarios.when: Remove is called with the connID

subSpecs.purpose: Removes authentication tokens from the token store.

data.name: FileUtility

operations.description: The system SHALL copy the contents of the source file to the destination path and preserve the original file permissions.
operations.name: copyFile

and: Destination file has identical content and permissions to source
scenarios.given: Source file exists and is readable, destination path is writable
scenarios.name: Successful file copy
scenarios.then: Destination file is created or overwritten with source content and permissions

and: Destination may be partially written or absent
scenarios.given: Source file does not exist or is not readable
scenarios.name: Source missing
scenarios.then: An error is returned

scenarios.given: Destination directory does not exist or is not writable
scenarios.name: Destination inaccessible
scenarios.then: An error is returned

data.purpose: Provides filesystem utility operations for atomic and permission-preserving file operations used during binary upgrade procedures.
sideEffects: Reads from source file path
Writes to destination file path
Queries file info with os.Stat

subSpecs.name: assetName

operations.description: The assetName function SHALL generate a platform-specific asset identifier string based on the current operating system and architecture.
operations.name: generateAssetIdentifier

and: the returned string SHALL follow the format 'chainlink-%s-%s'
scenarios.given: the function is called on a system with GOOS='linux' and GOARCH='amd64'
scenarios.name: linuxAMD64Asset
scenarios.then: the function returns 'chainlink-linux-amd64'

and: the returned string SHALL follow the format 'chainlink-%s-%s'
scenarios.given: the function is called on a system with GOOS='darwin' and GOARCH='arm64'
scenarios.name: darwinARM64Asset
scenarios.then: the function returns 'chainlink-darwin-arm64'

subSpecs.purpose: Generates a platform-specific asset name string for identifying the correct binary distribution to download during upgrades.

subSpecs.name: fmt.Sprintf

operations.description: The fmt.Sprintf function SHALL format a string according to the provided format specifier and arguments, returning the formatted string.
operations.name: formatString

and: the returned string SHALL contain all formatted values in their specified positions
scenarios.given: a format string '%s-%s' and arguments 'linux', 'amd64'
scenarios.name: simpleFormat
scenarios.then: the function returns 'linux-amd64'
scenarios.when: fmt.Sprintf is called with the format and arguments

subSpecs.purpose: Provides formatted string generation used throughout the upgrade process for constructing paths, error messages, and identifiers.

subSpecs.name: download

operations.description: The download function SHALL perform an HTTP GET request to the specified URL and return the response body as a byte slice, with a maximum size limit to prevent unbounded memory consumption.
operations.name: fetchRemoteResource

and: the response body SHALL be limited to a maximum configurable size
the function SHALL return an error if the response exceeds the size limit
scenarios.given: a valid URL returning a 10MB response with a 100MB size limit
scenarios.name: successfulDownload
scenarios.then: the function returns the full response body with no error

and: the function SHALL return an error indicating the size limit was exceeded
scenarios.given: a URL returning a response larger than the configured maximum size limit
scenarios.name: sizeLimitExceeded
scenarios.then: the function returns an error and no body data

and: the function SHALL include the version string in the User-Agent header
scenarios.given: a valid URL with the current version set to '1.0.0'
scenarios.name: versionedRequest
scenarios.then: the HTTP request includes User-Agent header containing '1.0.0'

subSpecs.purpose: Downloads a remote resource via HTTP with size limits and version-identified requests for safe binary retrieval during upgrades.

subSpecs.name: findChecksum

operations.description: The findChecksum function SHALL parse checksum data to locate and extract the SHA-256 hash associated with a specific asset file, matching by base filename.
operations.name: extractAssetChecksum

and: the function SHALL match against the base filename of the asset
the returned checksum SHALL be trimmed of whitespace
scenarios.given: checksum data containing 'abc123  chainlink-linux-amd64' and asset 'chainlink-linux-amd64'
scenarios.name: matchingChecksum
scenarios.then: the function returns 'abc123' with no error

and: the function SHALL support both space and tab separated checksum formats
scenarios.given: checksum data with tab-separated 'def456\tchainlink-darwin-arm64' and asset 'chainlink-darwin-arm64'
scenarios.name: tabSeparatedChecksum
scenarios.then: the function returns 'def456' with no error

and: the function SHALL return an error indicating no matching checksum was found
scenarios.given: checksum data not containing the requested asset name
scenarios.name: missingChecksum
scenarios.then: the function returns an empty string and a not-found error

and: the function SHALL handle checksums with optional prefix markers like 'SHA256'
scenarios.given: checksum data containing 'SHA256(abc)= def789  chainlink-linux-amd64'
scenarios.name: prefixedChecksum
scenarios.then: the function returns 'def789' with no error

subSpecs.purpose: Parses checksum manifest data to extract the SHA-256 hash for a specific asset by matching its base filename.

subSpecs.name: sha256Sum256

operations.description: The sha256.Sum256 function SHALL compute the SHA-256 cryptographic hash of the provided data and return a fixed-size 32-byte array.
operations.name: computeSHA256

and: the output SHALL be deterministic for identical inputs
the output SHALL be a fixed 32-byte array
scenarios.given: input data 'hello world'
scenarios.name: standardHash
scenarios.then: the function returns the SHA-256 hash e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 for empty input, or equivalent for 'hello world'

and: the function SHALL handle empty input
scenarios.given: empty byte slice
scenarios.name: emptyInputHash
scenarios.then: the function returns the SHA-256 hash of empty data

subSpecs.purpose: Computes a cryptographically secure SHA-256 hash of binary data for integrity verification during upgrade procedures.

subSpecs.name: stringsEqualFold

operations.description: The strings.EqualFold function SHALL perform a case-insensitive comparison of two UTF-8 strings and return true if they are equal, regardless of case.
operations.name: caseInsensitiveCompare

and: the function SHALL return true for strings differing only in case
scenarios.given: strings 'Chainlink' and 'chainlink'
scenarios.name: differingCaseMatch
scenarios.then: the function returns true

and: the function SHALL return false for different content
scenarios.given: strings 'chainlink' and 'ethereum'
scenarios.name: differentContent
scenarios.then: the function returns false

subSpecs.purpose: Provides case-insensitive string comparison for matching identifiers, headers, or status values during upgrade operations.

subSpecs.name: writeBinary

operations.description: The writeBinary function SHALL atomically write a binary file to a temporary location and then move it to the final destination, preserving original file permissions when replacing an existing binary.
operations.name: atomicBinaryWrite

and: the function SHALL create parent directories if they do not exist
the function SHALL use a temporary file for atomic replacement
the function SHALL preserve existing file permissions when replacing
scenarios.given: a valid asset name 'chainlink-linux-amd64' and binary data, with no existing binary at the destination
scenarios.name: freshInstall
scenarios.then: the function writes the binary to the upgrade path and returns the path with no error

and: the function SHALL copy permissions from the existing file to the new file
the function SHALL perform atomic replacement using rename
scenarios.given: an existing binary at the destination with mode 0755 and new binary data
scenarios.name: permissionPreservingUpdate
scenarios.then: the new binary has mode 0755 and the old binary is replaced atomically

and: the function SHALL clean up temporary files on failure
scenarios.given: a destination directory that cannot be written to due to permissions
scenarios.name: failedWriteCleanup
scenarios.then: the function returns an error and leaves no temporary files

subSpecs.purpose: Atomically writes a binary to the upgrade path with permission preservation, using temporary files and rename for safe replacement.

subSpecs.name: restartInstructions

operations.description: The restartInstructions function SHALL return platform-appropriate command and hint strings for restarting the Chainlink node after a binary upgrade.
operations.name: generateRestartGuidance

and: the command SHALL be executable by the user
the hint SHALL provide context for when to run the command
scenarios.given: the function is called on a Linux system with systemd
scenarios.name: systemdRestart
scenarios.then: the function returns 'systemctl restart chainlink' and a hint about service restart

and: the outputs SHALL be non-empty strings
scenarios.given: any supported platform
scenarios.name: nonEmptyOutput
scenarios.then: both command and hint are non-empty strings

subSpecs.purpose: Provides platform-specific restart instructions for applying a binary upgrade.

subSpecs.name: testUpgradeDownloadsAndVerifiesBinary

operations.description: The TestUpgrade_DownloadsAndVerifiesBinary test SHALL verify the complete upgrade flow including download, checksum verification, binary writing, and restart instruction generation.
operations.name: verifyUpgradeIntegration

and: the test SHALL set up a mock HTTP server serving a binary and checksums
the test SHALL verify the downloaded binary matches the expected checksum
the test SHALL verify the binary is written to the correct location
the test SHALL verify restart instructions are generated
scenarios.given: a mock server with valid binary and SHA-256 checksums, and a temporary directory for installation
scenarios.name: successfulUpgradeFlow
scenarios.then: the test passes with all assertions successful

and: the test SHALL clean up test versions after completion
scenarios.given: a test version set to '1.0.0-test'
scenarios.name: versionCleanup
scenarios.then: the test version is cleared after test completion

subSpecs.purpose: Integration test that validates the complete binary upgrade flow from download through verification to installation.

subSpecs.name: upgradeBinaryPath

operations.description: The upgradeBinaryPath function SHALL determine the filesystem path for storing the upgraded binary, resolving symlinks to find the true executable location and constructing a sibling path.
operations.name: resolveUpgradePath

and: the function SHALL resolve any symlinks in the current executable path
the function SHALL return a path in the same directory as the resolved executable
the returned path SHALL have a '.upgrade' suffix
scenarios.given: the current executable is at /usr/local/bin/chainlink with no symlinks
scenarios.name: directExecutable
scenarios.then: the function returns '/usr/local/bin/chainlink.upgrade' with no error

and: the function SHALL follow symlinks to their target
scenarios.given: the current executable path /usr/bin/chainlink is a symlink to /opt/chainlink/bin/chainlink
scenarios.name: symlinkResolution
scenarios.then: the function returns '/opt/chainlink/bin/chainlink.upgrade' with no error

and: the function SHALL return an error if the executable path cannot be determined
scenarios.given: a system where os.Executable returns an error
scenarios.name: unresolvablePath
scenarios.then: the function returns an empty string and an error

subSpecs.purpose: Resolves the filesystem path for the upgrade binary by finding the current executable location and constructing a sibling path with upgrade suffix.

subSpecs.name: osMkdirAll

operations.description: The os.MkdirAll function SHALL create a directory and all necessary parent directories with the specified permissions, succeeding silently if the directory already exists.
operations.name: createDirectoryHierarchy

and: the function SHALL create parent directories recursively
the function SHALL apply the specified permissions to new directories
scenarios.given: a path '/tmp/chainlink/upgrade/bin' where only '/tmp' exists, with permissions 0755
scenarios.name: deepPathCreation
scenarios.then: all directories are created with permissions 0755 and no error is returned

and: the function SHALL return nil if the directory already exists
scenarios.given: an existing directory '/tmp/chainlink'
scenarios.name: existingDirectory
scenarios.then: the function returns nil with no changes to existing permissions

subSpecs.purpose: Creates directory hierarchies needed for staging upgrade binaries, ensuring parent directories exist.

subSpecs.name: filepathDir

operations.description: The filepath.Dir function SHALL return the directory portion of a file path, handling both absolute and relative paths according to OS conventions.
operations.name: extractDirectory

and: the function SHALL handle trailing path separators
the function SHALL return '.' for single-element relative paths
scenarios.given: a path '/usr/local/bin/chainlink'
scenarios.name: absolutePath
scenarios.then: the function returns '/usr/local/bin'

and: the function SHALL use OS-specific path separators
scenarios.given: a Windows path 'C:\\Program Files\\Chainlink\\chainlink.exe'
scenarios.name: windowsPath
scenarios.then: the function returns 'C:\\Program Files\\Chainlink'

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts the directory component from a file path for constructing parent directory paths during upgrade staging.

subSpecs.name: osStat

operations.description: The os.Stat function SHALL return file information for the named file, including size, permissions, modification time, and mode, or an error if the file does not exist.
operations.name: getFileInfo

and: the returned FileInfo SHALL include the file mode for permission copying
scenarios.given: an existing file at '/usr/local/bin/chainlink' with mode 0755
scenarios.name: existingFile
scenarios.then: the function returns FileInfo with Mode() == 0755 and no error

and: the function SHALL return an error that satisfies os.IsNotExist
scenarios.given: a non-existent path '/tmp/does-not-exist'
scenarios.name: missingFile
scenarios.then: the function returns an error where os.IsNotExist(err) is true

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves file metadata, particularly permissions, for preserving existing binary attributes during upgrade.

subSpecs.name: copyFile

operations.description: The copyFile function SHALL copy both the contents and permissions of a source file to a destination file, creating or truncating the destination as needed.
operations.name: copyWithPermissions

and: the function SHALL read the entire source file contents
the function SHALL preserve the source file's permission mode on the destination
the function SHALL create the destination if it does not exist
scenarios.given: a source file '/tmp/original' with content 'binary data' and mode 0755
scenarios.name: successfulCopy
scenarios.then: the destination '/tmp/duplicate' contains 'binary data' with mode 0755 and no error

subSpecs.purpose: Copies file contents and permissions for staging upgrade binaries with preserved attributes.

data.name: RestartInstructions

operations.description: The system SHALL return the platform-appropriate shell command and a human-readable hint for restarting the service after upgrade.
operations.name: restartInstructions

and: Command is suitable for the detected platform
scenarios.given: The service is running on a supported platform
scenarios.name: Instructions generated
scenarios.then: A restart command string and hint string are returned

data.purpose: Generates user-facing restart guidance tailored to the execution platform, enabling operators to complete the upgrade workflow.

data.name: ConsoleLogsHandler

operations.description: Reads the console log file from the end and returns up to maxConsoleLines lines as a JSON response.
operations.name: GetConsoleLogs

scenarios.given: Console log file exists with content
scenarios.name: Successful log retrieval
scenarios.then: Returns up to 500 lines from end of file in reverse chronological order
scenarios.when: Get is invoked via HTTP request

scenarios.given: Console log file does not exist
scenarios.name: Missing log file
scenarios.then: Returns empty lines array without error
scenarios.when: Get is invoked via HTTP request

and: Error message is returned in JSON
scenarios.given: Console log file exists but is unreadable
scenarios.name: Unreadable log file
scenarios.then: Returns HTTP 500 with error message
scenarios.when: Get is invoked via HTTP request

data.purpose: Provides administrative HTTP endpoint to retrieve recent console log entries for monitoring and debugging.
sideEffects: Reads file from /tmp/axonrouter.log

subSpecs.name: openLogFile

operations.description: SHALL open the console log file at the configured path for read access.
operations.name: openLogFile

and: log file exists at configured path
scenarios.given: a valid log file path is configured
scenarios.name: successfulOpen
scenarios.then: SHALL return a non-nil file handle with no error

scenarios.given: the configured log file path does not exist
scenarios.name: missingFile
scenarios.then: SHALL return an error

subSpecs.purpose: Opens the console log file for subsequent read operations.

subSpecs.name: checkFileNotExist

operations.description: SHALL determine whether an error indicates that a file does not exist.
operations.name: checkNotExist

scenarios.given: an error from a file operation
scenarios.name: notExistError
scenarios.then: SHALL return true
scenarios.when: checking if error indicates missing file

scenarios.given: an error from a file operation
scenarios.name: otherError
scenarios.then: SHALL return false
scenarios.when: checking if error indicates missing file

subSpecs.purpose: Determines if a file operation failed because the target file does not exist.

subSpecs.name: closeFileHandle

operations.description: SHALL release all resources associated with the open file handle.
operations.name: closeFile

scenarios.given: a valid open file handle
scenarios.name: successfulClose
scenarios.then: SHALL return nil error and release resources

scenarios.given: a file handle that has been closed
scenarios.name: doubleClose
scenarios.then: SHALL return an error

subSpecs.purpose: Releases system resources held by an open file handle.

subSpecs.name: getFileStats

operations.description: SHALL retrieve metadata about the open file including size and modification time.
operations.name: retrieveFileInfo

scenarios.given: a valid open file handle
scenarios.name: successfulStat
scenarios.then: SHALL return non-nil FileInfo with accurate size

scenarios.given: a closed or invalid file handle
scenarios.name: invalidHandle
scenarios.then: SHALL return an error

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves metadata about the open log file to determine its size.

subSpecs.name: getFileSize

operations.description: SHALL return the size in bytes of the file from its metadata.
operations.name: retrieveSize

and: file contains 1024 bytes of log data
scenarios.given: a FileInfo object from a valid file
scenarios.name: nonEmptyFile
scenarios.then: SHALL return 1024

scenarios.given: a FileInfo object from an empty file
scenarios.name: emptyFile
scenarios.then: SHALL return 0

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts the byte size of the log file to calculate read offset.

subSpecs.name: queueLength

operations.description: SHALL return the number of elements currently stored in the byte queue.
operations.name: getQueueLength

scenarios.given: a byte queue with elements enqueued
scenarios.name: nonEmptyQueue
scenarios.then: SHALL return 5

scenarios.given: a newly initialized byte queue
scenarios.name: emptyQueue
scenarios.then: SHALL return 0

subSpecs.purpose: Returns the current element count in the byte queue for capacity planning.

subSpecs.name: convertToInt64

operations.description: SHALL convert a numeric value to a signed 64-bit integer.
operations.name: convertInt64

scenarios.given: a non-negative integer value
scenarios.name: positiveConversion
scenarios.then: SHALL return int64(1024)

scenarios.given: a negative integer value
scenarios.name: negativeConversion
scenarios.then: SHALL return int64(-1)

subSpecs.purpose: Converts numeric values to int64 for offset calculations in file operations.

subSpecs.name: readAtOffset

operations.description: SHALL read bytes from the file starting at a specified offset into the provided buffer.
operations.name: readFileAt

scenarios.given: a valid open file and read buffer
scenarios.name: successfulRead
scenarios.then: SHALL return 5 bytes read with 'hello' in buffer and nil error

scenarios.given: a valid open file
scenarios.name: offsetBeyondEOF
scenarios.then: SHALL return io.EOF error

subSpecs.purpose: Reads log data from a specific byte offset in the file for tail operations.

subSpecs.name: bytesToString

operations.description: SHALL convert a byte slice to its string representation.
operations.name: convertBytes

scenarios.given: a byte slice containing UTF-8 data
scenarios.name: validUTF8
scenarios.then: SHALL return equivalent string

scenarios.given: a byte slice with ASCII values
scenarios.name: asciiBytes
scenarios.then: SHALL return 'ABC'

subSpecs.purpose: Converts raw byte data read from the log file into a human-readable string.

subSpecs.name: closeStore

operations.description: SHALL close the underlying database connection and release associated resources.
operations.name: closeDatabase

scenarios.given: a Store with an open database connection
scenarios.name: successfulClose
scenarios.then: SHALL return nil and close the connection

scenarios.given: a Store whose connection was previously closed
scenarios.name: idempotentClose
scenarios.then: SHALL return an error or nil based on implementation

subSpecs.purpose: Releases database resources held by the Store when shutting down.

data.name: HealthService

operations.description: SHALL initiate periodic health checks against the configured API endpoint to monitor connectivity, latency, and system status.
operations.name: startHealthChecks

and: healthLatencyMs is updated with measured round-trip time
scenarios.given: Application is initialized and API endpoint is configured
scenarios.name: Health check cycle begins successfully
scenarios.then: Periodic health check interval is established

and: healthLatencyMs is set to maximum value
scenarios.given: Previous health check failed or network is unavailable
scenarios.name: Health check recovers from offline state
scenarios.then: Health status transitions to online with updated metrics
scenarios.when: startHealthChecks is called and fetch succeeds

scenarios.given: Health endpoint returns version information
