scenarios.name: Version information applied from health response
scenarios.then: Version stores reflect server state and update availability
scenarios.when: health check response contains version data

and: password warning dismissal may be cleared
scenarios.given: Health endpoint returns authentication policy data
scenarios.name: Password policy state synchronized from health check
scenarios.then: Local password policy state matches server requirements

operations.description: SHALL expose reactive store indicating whether the API is currently reachable and responding to health checks.
operations.name: getHealthOnline

scenarios.given: Health check has been performed
scenarios.name: Read current online status
scenarios.then: Returns true if last check succeeded, false otherwise

operations.description: SHALL expose reactive store containing the most recent health check latency measurement in milliseconds.
operations.name: getHealthLatencyMs

scenarios.given: At least one health check has completed
scenarios.name: Read current latency measurement
scenarios.then: Returns measured round-trip time or maximum value if offline

operations.description: SHALL expose reactive store containing the version identifier of the currently running server instance.
operations.name: getHealthCurrentVersion

scenarios.given: Health check has returned version data
scenarios.name: Read current server version
scenarios.then: Returns version string from most recent health response

operations.description: SHALL expose reactive store containing the latest available version identifier from the server.
operations.name: getHealthLatestVersion

scenarios.given: Health check has returned version data
scenarios.name: Read latest available version
scenarios.then: Returns latest version string from most recent health response

operations.description: SHALL expose reactive store indicating whether a newer version is available than the currently running version.
operations.name: getHealthUpdateAvailable

scenarios.given: Health check has returned version data
scenarios.name: Read update availability status
scenarios.then: Returns true when healthLatestVersion differs from healthCurrentVersion

data.purpose: Monitors API connectivity and system health through periodic health checks, exposing reactive stores for online status, latency metrics, and version information to drive UI state and operational decisions.
sideEffects: Periodic fetch requests to health endpoint
Updates to in-memory Svelte writable stores
Writes to preferred storage for password policy state
Removes items from storage when clearing password warnings

subSpecs.name: timestampCapture

operations.description: SHALL capture a high-resolution timestamp immediately before initiating a network request to measure latency.
operations.name: captureStartTimestamp

and: the returned value SHALL be a non-negative number
scenarios.given: the health check is about to begin
scenarios.name: timestampRecordedBeforeFetch
scenarios.then: a start timestamp is captured

subSpecs.purpose: Captures high-resolution timestamps to enable precise latency measurement for health checks.

subSpecs.name: healthCheckRequest

operations.description: SHALL initiate an HTTP GET request to the configured health endpoint and return the response for processing.
operations.name: executeHealthFetch

and: the request timeout SHALL be enforced if configured
scenarios.given: a valid health endpoint URL is configured
scenarios.name: successfulHealthEndpointFetch
scenarios.then: a Response object is returned
scenarios.when: fetch is called with the health endpoint

and: the error SHALL be propagated to the caller for handling
scenarios.given: the network is unreachable or the endpoint is down
scenarios.name: failedHealthEndpointFetch
scenarios.then: a network error is thrown
scenarios.when: fetch is called with the health endpoint

subSpecs.purpose: Executes the HTTP health check request to the configured API endpoint.

subSpecs.name: latencyFloorEnforcement

operations.description: SHALL ensure calculated latency is never reported as negative by enforcing a minimum value of zero.
operations.name: enforceNonNegativeLatency

scenarios.given: the end timestamp is less than the start timestamp due to clock adjustment
scenarios.name: negativeLatencyClampedToZero
scenarios.then: zero is returned
scenarios.when: Math.max is called with the calculated latency and 0

scenarios.given: the end timestamp is greater than the start timestamp
scenarios.name: positiveLatencyPreserved
scenarios.then: the calculated positive latency is returned
scenarios.when: Math.max is called with the calculated latency and 0

subSpecs.purpose: Guarantees latency metrics are never reported as negative values due to clock anomalies.

subSpecs.name: latencyPrecisionRounding

operations.description: SHALL round the computed latency to the nearest whole millisecond for consistent metric display and storage.
operations.name: roundToWholeMilliseconds

and: the result SHALL be an integer
scenarios.given: a latency value of 123.7 milliseconds
scenarios.name: fractionalLatencyRoundedUp
scenarios.then: 124 is returned

and: the result SHALL be an integer
scenarios.given: a latency value of 123.2 milliseconds
scenarios.name: fractionalLatencyRoundedDown
scenarios.then: 123 is returned

subSpecs.purpose: Rounds floating-point latency calculations to whole milliseconds for uniform metric representation.

subSpecs.name: latencyStoreUpdate

operations.description: SHALL update the reactive latency store with the rounded millisecond value and notify all subscribers of the change.
operations.name: publishLatencyMetric

and: all reactive subscribers SHALL receive the updated value
scenarios.given: a new rounded latency value of 150
scenarios.name: latencyStoreUpdatedAndPropagated
scenarios.then: the store contains 150 and subscribers are notified

and: the store SHALL accept zero as a valid latency value
scenarios.given: a latency value of 0 from a cached or instant response
scenarios.name: zeroLatencyAccepted
scenarios.then: the store contains 0 and subscribers are notified

subSpecs.purpose: Publishes measured latency metrics to the reactive store for UI and operational consumption.

subSpecs.name: responseBodyParsing

operations.description: SHALL asynchronously parse the response body as JSON and return the parsed object for health data extraction.
operations.name: parseHealthResponseJson

and: the promise SHALL resolve with the parsed object
scenarios.given: a response with valid JSON body containing health data
scenarios.name: validJsonParsedSuccessfully
scenarios.then: the parsed health data object is returned
scenarios.when: res.json is called on the response

and: the error SHALL be catchable by the caller
scenarios.given: a response with malformed JSON body
scenarios.name: invalidJsonRejection
scenarios.then: a SyntaxError is thrown
scenarios.when: res.json is called on the response

subSpecs.purpose: Extracts structured health data from the HTTP response body by parsing JSON content.

subSpecs.name: connectivityStatusUpdate

operations.description: SHALL update the reactive online status store with a boolean indicating API reachability and notify all subscribers.
operations.name: publishConnectivityStatus

and: all reactive subscribers SHALL receive the updated value
scenarios.given: the health check completed successfully
scenarios.name: onlineStatusSetToTrue
scenarios.then: the store contains true and subscribers are notified

and: all reactive subscribers SHALL receive the updated value
scenarios.given: the health check failed or timed out
scenarios.name: onlineStatusSetToFalse
scenarios.then: the store contains false and subscribers are notified

subSpecs.purpose: Publishes API connectivity status to the reactive store to drive UI online/offline indicators.

subSpecs.name: healthDataApplication

operations.description: SHALL update the current version store with the running application version from health data.
operations.name: setCurrentVersion

scenarios.given: health data contains currentVersion '1.2.3'
scenarios.name: currentVersionStored
scenarios.then: healthCurrentVersion store contains '1.2.3'

operations.description: SHALL update the latest version store with the newest available version from health data.
operations.name: setLatestVersion

scenarios.given: health data contains latestVersion '1.3.0'
scenarios.name: latestVersionStored
scenarios.then: healthLatestVersion store contains '1.3.0'

operations.description: SHALL compute and set whether an update is available by comparing current and latest versions.
operations.name: determineUpdateAvailability

scenarios.given: currentVersion is '1.2.3' and latestVersion is '1.3.0'
scenarios.name: updateAvailableWhenVersionsDiffer
scenarios.then: healthUpdateAvailable store contains true

scenarios.given: currentVersion is '1.3.0' and latestVersion is '1.3.0'
scenarios.name: noUpdateWhenVersionsMatch
scenarios.then: healthUpdateAvailable store contains false

operations.description: SHALL set the must-change-password flag based on health data security requirements.
operations.name: enforcePasswordChange

scenarios.given: health data indicates password change is required
scenarios.name: passwordChangeRequiredSet
scenarios.then: the must-change-password state is true

operations.description: SHALL clear any previously dismissed password warning when a mandatory change is enforced.
operations.name: resetPasswordWarningDismissal

and: the UI SHALL re-display the password warning if applicable
scenarios.given: a password warning was previously dismissed by the user
scenarios.name: dismissalClearedOnMandatoryChange
scenarios.then: the dismissal state is reset
scenarios.when: applyHealthData enforces a mandatory password change

operations.description: SHALL invoke the SafeLogger set method to configure structured logging for health operations.
operations.name: configureHealthLogger

scenarios.given: a valid slog.Logger instance is available
scenarios.name: loggerConfiguredForHealth
scenarios.then: the SafeLogger is configured with the provided logger
scenarios.when: applyHealthData calls set with the logger

subSpecs.purpose: Applies parsed health data to multiple reactive stores and triggers related security and logging side effects.

subSpecs.name: safeLoggerConfiguration

operations.description: SHALL configure the SafeLogger instance with the provided structured logger for subsequent health-related log output.
operations.name: assignStructuredLogger

and: subsequent log calls SHALL use the configured logger
nil logger SHALL be handled safely without panic
scenarios.given: a SafeLogger instance and a valid slog.Logger
scenarios.name: loggerAssignedToSafeLogger
scenarios.then: the SafeLogger stores the reference to the slog.Logger
scenarios.when: set is called with the logger

scenarios.given: a SafeLogger instance and a nil slog.Logger
scenarios.name: nilLoggerHandledSafely
scenarios.then: the SafeLogger handles nil gracefully without panic

subSpecs.purpose: Configures the SafeLogger with a structured logger instance for safe, contextual logging within health operations.

data.name: ClipboardService

operations.description: Copies the provided text to the system clipboard using the best available mechanism, and provides user feedback via toast notifications.
operations.name: copyToClipboard

and: A success toast is displayed with the label if provided, or a default message
scenarios.given: The application is running in a secure context and the navigator.clipboard API is available
scenarios.name: Modern clipboard API succeeds
scenarios.then: The text is written to the system clipboard and true is returned
scenarios.when: copyToClipboard is called with valid text

and: A fallback mechanism using a hidden textarea and document.execCommand is attempted
The original text selection is preserved and restored after the operation
scenarios.given: The navigator.clipboard API is unavailable or throws an error
scenarios.name: Fallback execCommand succeeds
scenarios.then: The text is written to the system clipboard via the copy event interceptor and true is returned
scenarios.when: copyToClipboard is called with valid text

and: The target element's text is selected using selectElementText
An info toast prompts the user to manually copy with keyboard shortcut
scenarios.given: All automatic copy mechanisms fail and a selectTarget element is provided
scenarios.name: Fallback with manual selection
scenarios.then: The target element's text is selected, false is returned, and the user is instructed to copy manually
scenarios.when: copyToClipboard is called with valid text and selectTarget

and: An error toast is displayed with a manual copy instruction
scenarios.given: All automatic copy mechanisms fail and no selectTarget is provided
scenarios.name: Complete copy failure
scenarios.then: False is returned and the user is informed that copy failed
scenarios.when: copyToClipboard is called with valid text and no selectTarget

scenarios.given: The text parameter is empty or falsy
scenarios.name: Empty text rejection
scenarios.then: False is returned immediately without attempting any copy mechanism
scenarios.when: copyToClipboard is called with empty text

operations.description: Programmatically selects all text content within a given DOM element by creating a Range and updating the window selection.
operations.name: selectElementText

scenarios.given: A valid HTMLElement exists in the DOM
scenarios.name: Select element contents
scenarios.then: All text content within the element is selected in the document's current selection
scenarios.when: selectElementText is called with the element

scenarios.given: The window selection contains ranges from other elements
scenarios.name: Replace existing selection
scenarios.then: All previous selection ranges are removed and only the target element's contents are selected

data.purpose: Provides cross-browser clipboard text copying with graceful degradation, user feedback, and programmatic text selection capabilities.
sideEffects: Writes to system clipboard via navigator.clipboard
Writes to system clipboard via document.execCommand and ClipboardEvent
Modifies DOM by creating and removing temporary textarea element
Modifies window selection and document ranges
Displays toast notifications via svelte-sonner

subSpecs.name: copySuccessFeedback

operations.description: The system SHALL display a success toast notification to the user when text has been successfully copied to the clipboard.
operations.name: displaySuccessToast

and: The toast notification SHALL be visible to the user
The toast notification SHALL automatically dismiss after the configured duration
scenarios.given: Text has been successfully copied to the clipboard via the modern or legacy API
scenarios.name: successfulCopyDisplaysToast
scenarios.then: A success toast notification is displayed with the message 'Copied to clipboard'

subSpecs.purpose: Provides visual confirmation to the user that clipboard copy operation completed successfully.

subSpecs.name: getCurrentSelection

operations.description: The system SHALL obtain the current Selection object representing the user's text selection state in the document.
operations.name: retrieveSelectionObject

and: The returned Selection object SHALL reflect the current document selection state
The returned value SHALL be null if the browser does not support selections
scenarios.given: The document may have an active text selection or no selection
scenarios.name: getDocumentSelection
scenarios.then: A Selection object representing the current selection state is returned
scenarios.when: The system needs to preserve or manipulate the current text selection

subSpecs.purpose: Retrieves the current text selection state from the browser to enable selection preservation and manipulation.

subSpecs.name: getSelectionRange

operations.description: The system SHALL retrieve the Range object at the specified index from the current selection to preserve user selection state before programmatic manipulation.
operations.name: retrieveRangeAtIndex

and: The Range object SHALL represent the boundaries of the selected text
An IndexSizeError SHALL be thrown if the specified index exceeds the number of ranges in the selection
scenarios.given: The user has selected text in the document and the selection contains at least one range
scenarios.name: preserveUserSelectionRange
scenarios.then: The Range object at index 0 is returned representing the user's original selection
scenarios.when: The system needs to save the current selection before creating a temporary selection for copying

subSpecs.purpose: Captures the user's current text selection range to enable restoration after programmatic clipboard operations.

subSpecs.name: addDocumentListener

operations.description: The system SHALL register an event listener on the document for the 'copy' event to intercept and customize clipboard behavior during programmatic copy operations.
operations.name: registerCopyEventListener

and: The listener SHALL be invoked during the capture phase if useCapture is true
The listener SHALL only be invoked for 'copy' events dispatched on or bubbling to the document
scenarios.given: The system is preparing to execute a programmatic copy operation
scenarios.name: interceptCopyEvent
scenarios.then: The copy event listener is registered and active on the document
scenarios.when: A copy event listener needs to be attached to control clipboard data

subSpecs.purpose: Registers event listeners on the document to intercept and control clipboard events during copy operations.

subSpecs.name: createTextAreaElement

operations.description: The system SHALL create a new HTMLTextAreaElement in the document to serve as a temporary container for text that will be copied to the clipboard.
operations.name: createTemporaryTextArea

and: The created element SHALL NOT be visible to the user
The created element SHALL be removed from the document after the copy operation completes
scenarios.given: The modern Clipboard API is unavailable and fallback copy mechanism is required
scenarios.name: createHiddenTextAreaForCopy
scenarios.then: A new HTMLTextAreaElement is created and available for text insertion
scenarios.when: The system needs a temporary DOM element to hold text for legacy clipboard copying

subSpecs.purpose: Creates temporary DOM elements needed for legacy clipboard operations when modern APIs are unavailable.

subSpecs.name: configureTextAreaAttributes

operations.description: The system SHALL set attributes on the temporary textarea element to ensure it is not visually displayed and does not interfere with the user interface layout.
operations.name: setTextAreaDisplayAttributes

and: The 'readonly' attribute SHALL be set to prevent on-screen keyboard activation on mobile devices
The 'style' attribute SHALL position the element off-screen with absolute positioning
scenarios.given: A temporary textarea element has been created for legacy clipboard copying
scenarios.name: hideTemporaryTextArea
scenarios.then: The textarea has attributes set that render it invisible and non-interactive to users
scenarios.when: The system configures the temporary element before inserting it into the document

subSpecs.purpose: Configures the temporary textarea element to be invisible and non-interactive while remaining functional for clipboard operations.

subSpecs.name: focusTextArea

operations.description: The system SHALL focus the temporary textarea element to ensure it is the active element for subsequent selection and copy operations.
operations.name: focusTemporaryElement

and: The document.activeElement SHALL be the temporary textarea after focus
Focus events SHALL be dispatched as per standard DOM behavior
scenarios.given: A temporary textarea element has been created, configured, and appended to the document
scenarios.name: focusForSelection
scenarios.then: The textarea receives focus and becomes the active element
scenarios.when: The system needs to select text within the element for clipboard copying

subSpecs.purpose: Activates the temporary textarea element to enable text selection for legacy clipboard operations.

subSpecs.name: selectTextRange

operations.description: The system SHALL set the selection range within the textarea to encompass all text content, preparing it for the copy command.
operations.name: selectAllTextInTextArea

and: The selection end SHALL equal the length of the textarea's value
scenarios.given: The temporary textarea contains the text to be copied
scenarios.name: selectAllTextForCopy
scenarios.then: All text in the textarea is selected (highlighted) and ready for the copy command
scenarios.when: The system prepares text for the document.execCommand('copy') operation

subSpecs.purpose: Selects all text within the temporary textarea to ensure the copy command captures the complete content.

subSpecs.name: executeCopyCommand

operations.description: The system SHALL execute the 'copy' command via the legacy document.execCommand API to copy the currently selected text to the system clipboard.
operations.name: legacyClipboardCopy

and: The command SHALL return true if the copy operation was successful
The command SHALL return false if the copy operation failed or is unsupported
The currently selected text in the focused element SHALL be copied to the clipboard
scenarios.given: Text is selected in the focused temporary textarea element
scenarios.name: executeLegacyCopy
scenarios.then: The selected text is copied to the system clipboard and the command returns true
scenarios.when: The modern Clipboard API is unavailable and the legacy fallback is invoked

subSpecs.purpose: Executes the legacy clipboard copy command as a fallback mechanism when modern APIs are unavailable.

subSpecs.name: removeDocumentListener

operations.description: The system SHALL remove a previously registered event listener from the document to clean up after programmatic copy operations complete.
operations.name: unregisterCopyEventListener

and: The listener SHALL no longer be invoked for subsequent 'copy' events
The removal SHALL only affect the specific listener instance provided
scenarios.given: A copy event listener was previously registered on the document for a programmatic copy operation
scenarios.name: cleanupCopyListener
scenarios.then: The copy event listener is removed from the document
scenarios.when: The programmatic copy operation has completed, whether successful or not

subSpecs.purpose: Cleans up event listeners after clipboard operations to prevent memory leaks and unintended side effects.

subSpecs.name: clearSelectionRanges

operations.description: The system SHALL remove all ranges from the current selection to clear any programmatically created selections before restoring the original user selection.
operations.name: clearCurrentSelection

and: The document selection SHALL be empty after this operation
Any visually highlighted text from programmatic selection SHALL be deselected
scenarios.given: A programmatic selection was created in a temporary element for clipboard copying
scenarios.name: clearProgrammaticSelection
scenarios.then: All ranges are removed from the selection and no text is selected
scenarios.when: The system prepares to restore the user's original selection state

subSpecs.purpose: Clears the current selection to prepare for restoring the user's original selection state after clipboard operations.

subSpecs.name: restoreSelectionRange

operations.description: The system SHALL add a previously saved Range object to the current selection to restore the user's original text selection after clipboard operations complete.
operations.name: restoreOriginalSelection

and: The restored selection SHALL match the user's original selection boundaries
The visually highlighted text SHALL correspond to the original user selection
scenarios.given: The user's original selection range was preserved before programmatic manipulation
scenarios.name: restoreUserSelection
scenarios.then: The original selection range is added to the current selection and the user's text is re-selected
scenarios.when: Clipboard operations have completed and cleanup is in progress

subSpecs.purpose: Restores the user's original text selection state after temporary programmatic selections are used for clipboard operations.

subSpecs.name: selectElementContents

operations.description: The system SHALL select all text content within a specified HTML element by creating a range that encompasses the element's contents and adding it to the current selection.
operations.name: selectAllElementText

and: The element's text content SHALL be visually highlighted
The document selection SHALL contain a single range spanning the element's contents
scenarios.given: A valid HTMLElement exists in the document with text content
scenarios.name: selectElementTextContent
scenarios.then: All text within the specified element is selected in the document
scenarios.when: The selectElementText function is called with the target element

subSpecs.purpose: Programmatically selects all text content within a given DOM element for copying or visual emphasis.

subSpecs.name: copyInfoFeedback

operations.description: The system SHALL display an informational toast notification to the user when the clipboard copy operation requires manual intervention or uses a fallback mechanism.
operations.name: displayInfoToast

and: The toast notification SHALL be distinguishable from success/error toasts by visual styling
The toast notification SHALL provide actionable guidance to the user
scenarios.given: The clipboard copy operation used a fallback mechanism or requires user awareness
scenarios.name: informFallbackCopy
scenarios.then: An informational toast is displayed with guidance such as 'Press Ctrl+C to copy'
scenarios.when: The system detects that automatic copying may not have succeeded and manual copy is needed

subSpecs.purpose: Provides informational feedback to users when clipboard operations require manual intervention or use fallback mechanisms.
